target: Recorrimos el barrio gótico a pie.
english: We explored the Gothic Quarter on foot.

target: Nos alojamos en una casa rural.
english: We stayed in a rural house.

target: Es una lástima que el museo esté cerrado hoy.
english: It's a shame that the museum is closed today.

target: A juzgar por las reseñas, ese restaurante es excelente.
english: Judging by the reviews, that restaurant is excellent.

target: El despacho del director está al fondo.
english: The director's office is at the end.

target: Soy ingeniero de software.
english: I'm a software engineer.
grammar: In Spanish, no article before professions: Soy ingeniero (not Soy un ingeniero).

target: Imprimí veinte copias del informe.
english: I printed twenty copies of the report.

target: Avísame cuando llegue el cliente.
english: Let me know when the client arrives.

target: Los domingos comemos en familia.
english: On Sundays we eat as a family.
grammar: Los domingos = on Sundays (definite article + day = habitual)

target: El perro quiere salir al jardín.
english: The dog wants to go out to the garden.

target: Mi madre es profesora.
english: My mother is a teacher.
grammar: Ser for professions: soy/es + profession (no article needed)

target: Mi suegra cocina arroz con pollo.
english: My mother-in-law cooks rice with chicken.

target: Mi hijo dibuja muy bien.
english: My son draws very well.

target: El niño no quiere comer verdura.
english: The boy doesn't want to eat vegetables.

target: Mi mujer prepara el almuerzo.
english: My wife prepares lunch.

target: Mi padre sabe arreglar todo.
english: My father knows how to fix everything.

target: Mi hijo pequeño ya camina.
english: My little son already walks.

target: Pasamos la Navidad en casa de los abuelos.
english: We spent Christmas at the grandparents' house.
grammar: En casa de = at someone's house (no article needed)

target: Te pareces mucho a tu madre.
english: You look a lot like your mother.

target: Llámame cuando llegues al hotel.
english: Call me when you arrive at the hotel.

target: Ese chiquillo es un terremoto; no para ni un segundo.
english: That little kid is a whirlwind — he doesn't stop for a second.

target: Ella camina al parque cada mañana.
english: She walks to the park every morning.

target: El tren sale a las ocho.
english: The train leaves at eight.

target: Los pájaros cantan al amanecer.
english: The birds sing at dawn.

target: Nosotros descubrimos un camino nuevo.
english: We discovered a new path.

target: Yo perdí el autobús esta mañana.
english: I missed the bus this morning.

target: Ellos construyeron una casa de madera.
english: They built a wooden house.

target: Nosotros reservamos una mesa para seis.
english: We reserved a table for six.

target: Si tuviéramos coche, iríamos al campo.
english: If we had a car, we'd go to the countryside.

target: El bebé está a punto de dar sus primeros pasos.
english: The baby is about to take his first steps.

target: Este sitio está en el quinto pino; tardamos una hora en llegar.
english: This place is in the middle of nowhere (lit. in the fifth pine) — it took us an hour to get there.
grammar: Estar en el quinto pino = to be very far away / in the middle of nowhere.

target: Faltan nueve días para las vacaciones.
english: There are nine days until the vacation.

target: En diciembre celebramos la Navidad.
english: In December we celebrate Christmas.

target: Me gusta mucho esta ciudad.
english: I really like this city.
grammar: "Gustar" works backwards from English: the thing you like is the subject. "Me gusta la ciudad" = the city pleases me. "Me gustan los gatos" = cats please me (plural).

target: Los lunes siempre me cuesta levantarme.
english: On Mondays it's always hard for me to get up.
grammar: "Me cuesta" works like "gustar" — the difficult thing is the subject: "me cuesta levantarme" = getting up costs/is hard for me. Literally: "lifting myself costs me."

target: Me gusta ducharme con agua caliente después de hacer ejercicio.
english: I like showering with hot water after exercising.
grammar: "Me gusta ducharme" — when gustar is followed by an infinitive, use singular "gusta" (not gustan). The infinitive acts as the subject.

target: Me lleva unos veinte minutos llegar al trabajo en carro.
english: It takes me about twenty minutes to get to work by car.
grammar: "llevar" + time = to take (time). Like gustar, the time is the subject: "twenty minutes takes me." Also: "me llevó" (past).

target: Ella tiene los ojos verdes y pecas en la nariz.
english: She has green eyes and freckles on her nose.
grammar: Physical descriptions with 'tener': 'tiene los ojos verdes' = she has green eyes. Spanish uses the article 'los' with body parts.

target: Mi hija se graduó de la universidad con honores.
english: My daughter graduated from university with honors.
grammar: 'Graduarse de' = to graduate from. Reflexive verb in preterite: 'se graduó.' 'Con honores' = with honors.

target: Soy alérgico a los frutos secos, ¿este plato lleva alguno?
english: I'm allergic to nuts, does this dish contain any?
grammar: 'Llevar' can mean 'to contain' for food: '¿lleva alguno?' = does it contain any? 'Ser alérgico a' = to be allergic to.

target: Hay una cascada impresionante al final del sendero de montaña.
english: There's an impressive waterfall at the end of the mountain trail.
grammar: 'Hay' for existence: 'Hay una cascada' = There is a waterfall. 'Al final de' = at the end of. 'Del' = de + el.

target: Me duele la cabeza.
english: My head hurts.
grammar: 'Doler' works like 'gustar': the body part is the subject. 'Me duele la cabeza' = My head hurts (lit. the head hurts to me). Plural: 'me duelen los pies.'

target: La música regional refleja la diversidad cultural de Latinoamérica.
english: Regional music reflects the cultural diversity of Latin America.
grammar: 'Reflejar' = to reflect. Academic/formal vocabulary: 'diversidad cultural' = cultural diversity. 'De Latinoamérica' = of Latin America.

target: El tenista ganó el partido en tres sets muy reñidos.
english: The tennis player won the match in three very close sets.
grammar: 'Ganar' in preterite: ganó = he/she won. 'Reñido' = close/tight (for competitions). Sports vocabulary: 'partido' = match, 'set' = set.

target: Me gusta mucho la música.
english: I like music a lot.
grammar: "Gustar" literally = to please. The thing liked is the subject: "Me gusta el café" = Coffee pleases me. Not "I like coffee" structure.

target: ¿Te apetece ir a tomar algo?
english: Do you feel like going for a drink?

target: Me cae muy bien tu amigo.
english: I really like your friend (as a person).
grammar: to like someone (personality), different from "gustar."

target: A mi hermana le fascina la cocina.
english: My sister is fascinated by cooking.
grammar: "Fascinar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me fascina..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le interesan mucho los deportes.
english: He's very interested in sports.
grammar: "Interesar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me interesa..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Nos falta tiempo para todo.
english: We lack time for everything.
grammar: "Faltar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me falta..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me sorprende que todavía no haya llegado.
english: It surprises me that he still hasn't arrived.
grammar: "Sorprender" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me sorprende..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me duele la espalda.
english: My back hurts.
grammar: "Doler" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me doler..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le sobra dinero a fin de mes.
english: He has money left over at the end of the month.
grammar: "Sobrar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me sobrar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Nos queda una semana de vacaciones.
english: We have one week of vacation left.
grammar: "Quedar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me quedar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le preocupa el examen de mañana.
english: She's worried about tomorrow's exam.
grammar: "Preocupar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me preocupar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me aburren las películas largas.
english: Long movies bore me.
grammar: "Aburrir" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me aburrir..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: No me conviene ese horario.
english: That schedule doesn't work for me.
grammar: "Convenir" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me convenir..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Les hace falta más práctica.
english: They need more practice.
grammar: "Hacer falta" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me hacer..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me parece bien tu idea.
english: Your idea sounds good to me.
grammar: "Parecer" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me parecer..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me da miedo manejar de noche.
english: I'm scared of driving at night.
grammar: "Dar miedo" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me dar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le llama la atención la arquitectura.
english: Architecture catches his attention.
grammar: "Llamar la atención" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me llamar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le da vergüenza hablar en público.
english: He's embarrassed to speak in public.
grammar: "Dar vergüenza" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me dar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me extraña que no haya llamado.
english: It's strange to me that he hasn't called.
grammar: "Extrañar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me extrañar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me sale más barato comprar online.
english: It comes out cheaper for me to buy online.
grammar: "Salir + adjective" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me salir..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le sienta bien ese color.
english: That color looks good on her.
grammar: "Sentar bien/mal" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me sentar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Nos toca pagar a nosotros.
english: It's our turn to pay.
grammar: "Tocar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me tocar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Me resulta difícil concentrarme aquí.
english: I find it hard to concentrate here.
grammar: "Resultar" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me resultar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le da pereza ir al gimnasio.
english: He's too lazy to go to the gym.
grammar: "Dar pereza" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me dar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Nos hace ilusión el viaje.
english: We're excited about the trip.
grammar: "Hacer ilusión" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me hacer..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Le queda poco para jubilarse.
english: He's close to retiring.
grammar: "Quedar" for remaining: "Quedan tres" = Three are left. Like gustar pattern — the remaining thing is the subject.

target: Me hacen gracia sus chistes.
english: His jokes make me laugh.
grammar: "Hacer gracia" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me hacer..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Se parecen mucho el padre y el hijo.
english: The father and son look very similar.
grammar: "Parecerse" = to resemble each other. Reciprocal: "Nos parecemos" = We look alike.

target: No me apetece salir; estoy hecho polvo.
english: I don't feel like going out — I'm exhausted (lit. I'm made into dust).

target: Me importa un bledo lo que piensen los demás.
english: I couldn't care less what others think.

target: Me gusta mucho mi trabajo.
english: I really like my job.
grammar: Gustar: me gusta + singular noun. Subject is 'mi trabajo', not 'yo'

target: Nos falta un documento para cerrar el contrato.
english: We're missing one document to close the contract.
grammar: Faltar: nos falta — we're missing/lacking. Works like gustar

target: Me interesan las ofertas de empleo.
english: I'm interested in the job offers.
grammar: Interesar: me interesan (plural) — agrees with 'las ofertas'

target: Nos quedan dos días para entregar el proyecto.
english: We have two days left to hand in the project.
grammar: Quedar: nos quedan — we have... left. Plural agrees with 'dos días'

target: Me sobra comida, ¿quieres un poco?
english: I have leftover food, do you want some?
grammar: Sobrar: me sobra — I have extra/leftover. Opposite of faltar

target: Me conviene más el turno de mañana.
english: The morning shift suits me better.
grammar: Convenir: me conviene — it suits me, it's better for me

target: Le cuesta mucho madrugar.
english: Getting up early is hard for him.
grammar: Costar: le cuesta — it's hard for him/her. Works like gustar

target: Todavía nos quedan cosas por hacer.
english: We still have things left to do.
grammar: "Por" + infinitive = still to be done: "Queda mucho por hacer" = There's a lot left to do.

target: Me encantó la comida local.
english: I loved the local food.
grammar: "Encantar" works like "gustar": the thing loved is the subject. "Me encantó" = it enchanted me.

target: ¿Hay un restaurante cerca?
english: Is there a restaurant nearby?

target: ¿Sabes dónde hay una farmacia?
english: Do you know where there's a pharmacy?

target: Hay veinte estudiantes en la clase.
english: There are twenty students in the class.

target: Hay cien personas en la sala.
english: There are a hundred people in the room.
grammar: time uses "ser": es la una (1:00), son las dos/tres/etc. "Y media" = half past, "y cuarto" = quarter past.

target: Cada cuatro años hay un año bisiesto.
english: Every four years there's a leap year.

target: Mi suegro es una persona muy generosa.
english: My father-in-law is a very generous person.

target: Mi padre es calvo y bastante alto.
english: My father is bald and quite tall.

target: Mi jefa es muy trabajadora y organizada.
english: My boss is very hardworking and organized.

target: Hay un espejo grande en la entrada.
english: There's a big mirror in the entryway.

target: ¿Tienes sed? Hay jugo de naranja en la nevera.
english: Are you thirsty? There's orange juice in the fridge.

target: ¿Hay algún restaurante por aquí que sirva comida vegana?
english: Is there any restaurant around here that serves vegan food?

target: La playa está muy cerca.
english: The beach is very close.

target: ¿Hay wifi en el hotel?
english: Is there wifi at the hotel?

target: La maleta es muy pesada.
english: The suitcase is very heavy.

target: ¿Hay descuento para estudiantes?
english: Is there a student discount?

target: Mi jefe es muy estricto.
english: My boss is very strict.

target: La cena en familia es importante.
english: Family dinner is important.

target: Mi familia es pequeña pero unida.
english: My family is small but close-knit.

target: No hay nadie que sepa la respuesta.
english: There's nobody who knows the answer.

target: Donde hay voluntad, hay un camino.
english: Where there's a will, there's a way.

target: Hay algo que quiero contarte.
english: There's something I want to tell you.

target: Este barrio es muy tranquilo.
english: This neighborhood is very quiet.

target: Mi hermano es el más joven.
english: My brother is the youngest.
grammar: Ser for identity: "Soy Ana." Use ser to say WHO or WHAT something is.

target: La reunión es importante.
english: The meeting is important.
grammar: Ser for characteristics: traits that define what something IS use ser, not estar.

target: Mi casa es pequeña pero cómoda.
english: My house is small but cozy.
grammar: Ser for defining features: "La casa es grande." Ser for traits seen as defining, not temporary.

target: Este restaurante es nuevo.
english: This restaurant is new.
grammar: Ser for classification: "Es un mamífero." Use ser to say what category something belongs to.

target: Mi carro es azul.
english: My car is blue.
grammar: Ser for inherent color: "El cielo es azul." Use ser for colors seen as defining traits.

target: Este hotel es bastante caro.
english: This hotel is quite expensive.
grammar: Ser for price: "Es barato." Use ser to classify something as cheap/expensive in general.

target: El sofá es nuevo.
english: The sofa is new.

target: Mi padre es muy alto.
english: My father is very tall.

target: Este restaurante es muy bueno.
english: This restaurant is very good.

target: Mi jefe es muy exigente.
english: My boss is very demanding.

target: La casa de mis padres es grande.
english: My parents' house is big.

target: Mi hija es muy inteligente.
english: My daughter is very intelligent.

target: Es importante llegar a tiempo.
english: It's important to arrive on time.

target: El carro de mi padre es blanco.
english: My father's car is white.

target: Las ventanas están sucias.
english: The windows are dirty.

target: Mi vecino es el más amable del barrio.
english: My neighbor is the friendliest in the neighborhood.

target: Mi padre es más paciente que mi madre.
english: My father is more patient than my mother.

target: La sala donde hacemos las reuniones es pequeña.
english: The room where we hold meetings is small.

target: La catedral es el monumento más famoso.
english: The cathedral is the most famous monument.

target: La playa es muy bonita.
english: The beach is very beautiful.

target: El restaurante es bastante caro.
english: The restaurant is quite expensive.

target: La ciudad es muy antigua.
english: The city is very old.

target: La habitación del hotel es pequeña.
english: The hotel room is small.

target: Mi oficina es grande y luminosa.
english: My office is big and bright.

target: El trabajo es interesante pero difícil.
english: The work is interesting but difficult.

target: El cliente es muy exigente.
english: The client is very demanding.

target: Mi madre es muy cariñosa.
english: My mother is very caring.

target: Mi abuela es la mejor cocinera.
english: My grandmother is the best cook.

target: La familia es lo más importante.
english: Family is the most important thing.

target: Mi vecino es muy amable.
english: My neighbor is very kind.

target: Mi primo es muy divertido.
english: My cousin is very funny.

target: Mi sobrina es muy simpática.
english: My niece is very nice.

target: Las habitaciones del hotel son cómodas.
english: The hotel rooms are comfortable.

target: La cocina es la habitación más grande.
english: The kitchen is the biggest room.

target: La maleta es grande.
english: The suitcase is big.

target: El aeropuerto es muy grande.
english: The airport is very big.

target: La habitación es cómoda.
english: The room is comfortable.

target: La cama es muy blanda.
english: The bed is very soft.

target: La catedral es muy antigua.
english: The cathedral is very old.

target: La plaza está llena de gente.
english: The square is full of people.
grammar: "Estar" for temporary states. The square is full now, but it won't always be.

target: El parque es bonito.
english: The park is pretty.

target: La playa es bonita.
english: The beach is pretty.

target: La cerveza es muy barata aquí.
english: Beer is very cheap here.

target: No hay ningún taxi que pueda llevarme ahora.
english: There's no taxi that can take me now.

target: Mi jefe es muy amable.
english: My boss is very kind.
grammar: Ser + personality adjective: "Es amable" = kind by nature. Ser describes character — who someone is, not how they currently feel.

target: El correo electrónico es importante.
english: Email is important.

target: La empresa es grande.
english: The company is big.

target: El proyecto es interesante.
english: The project is interesting.

target: Mi oficina es pequeña pero cómoda.
english: My office is small but comfortable.

target: Hoy hay mucho tráfico.
english: There's a lot of traffic today.

target: Mi jefa es muy exigente.
english: My boss is very demanding.

target: Hay mucho ruido en la oficina.
english: There is a lot of noise in the office.

target: El departamento de marketing es grande.
english: The marketing department is big.

target: Hay una nueva política de teletrabajo.
english: There is a new remote work policy.

target: Los lunes son los días más difíciles.
english: Mondays are the hardest days.

target: Mi hermano es más alto que yo.
english: My brother is taller than me.

target: Hay juguetes en el suelo.
english: There are toys on the floor.
grammar: Hay = there is/there are (invariable, same for singular and plural)

target: Mi familia es muy unida.
english: My family is very close.

target: El cuarto de los niños es azul.
english: The kids' room is blue.

target: Mi primo es muy gracioso.
english: My cousin is very funny.

target: Hay ropa en la lavadora.
english: There are clothes in the washing machine.

target: Mi madre es la mejor cocinera.
english: My mother is the best cook.

target: Mi padre es muy cariñoso.
english: My father is very affectionate.

target: Hay un parque cerca de casa.
english: There is a park near home.

target: Los vecinos son muy simpáticos.
english: The neighbors are very friendly.

target: Hay fotos de la familia en la pared.
english: There are family photos on the wall.

target: Mi suegro es muy generoso.
english: My father-in-law is very generous.

target: Mi padre es ingeniero muy dedicado.
english: My father is a very dedicated engineer.

target: Ella es alta pero está cansada.
english: She is tall but she's tired.

target: La ciudad donde nací es pequeña.
english: The city where I was born is small.

target: El gato es negro.
english: The cat is black.

target: Mi color favorito es el amarillo.
english: My favorite color is yellow.

target: Las naranjas son de color naranja.
english: Oranges are orange in color.

target: Las rosas son de color rosa.
english: Roses are pink.

target: El sofá es marrón.
english: The sofa is brown.

target: Esta calle es muy larga.
english: This street is very long.

target: Esta flor es muy bonita.
english: This flower is very pretty.

target: Ese edificio es muy feo.
english: That building is very ugly.

target: Hola.
english: Hello.

target: Adiós.
english: Goodbye.

target: Gracias.
english: Thank you.

target: Sí.
english: Yes.

target: No.
english: No.

target: Perdón.
english: Sorry.

target: Buenas tardes.
english: Good afternoon.

target: Buenas noches.
english: Good evening.

target: De nada.
english: You're welcome.

target: Lo siento.
english: I'm sorry.
grammar: "Lo" is a neuter pronoun meaning "it." "Siento" comes from sentir (to feel). Literally: "I feel it."

target: Mucho gusto.
english: Nice to meet you.

target: ¿Cómo estás?
english: How are you?
grammar: "Estar" is used for temporary states (how you feel right now). Compare with "ser" for permanent traits. ¿Cómo estás? = How are you feeling? vs. ¿Cómo eres? = What are you like?

target: Estoy bien.
english: I'm fine.
grammar: "Estoy" is the yo (I) form of estar. Used here because feeling fine is a temporary state.

target: Hasta luego.
english: See you later.

target: Hasta mañana.
english: See you tomorrow.

target: No entiendo.
english: I don't understand.
grammar: Negation in Spanish: just put "no" before the verb. No entiendo, no hablo, no sé.

target: Soy de México.
english: I'm from Mexico.
grammar: "Ser" is used for origin, identity, and permanent traits. Soy = I am (permanent). "Soy de" = I am from.

target: Hablo un poco.
english: I speak a little.

target: Necesito ayuda.
english: I need help.

target: Ellos son amigos.
english: They are friends.

target: No hablo español muy bien.
english: I don't speak Spanish very well.

target: Tengo una pregunta.
english: I have a question.
grammar: "Tener" (to have) is irregular: tengo (I have), tienes (you have), tiene (he/she has). It's also used for age, hunger, thirst, and more.

target: Es muy importante.
english: It's very important.

target: No hay problema.
english: No problem.
grammar: "Hay" means "there is" or "there are" — it's the same for singular and plural. Hay un gato. Hay tres gatos.

target: Tengo hambre.
english: I'm hungry.
grammar: Spanish uses "tener" (to have) not "estar" (to be) for hunger, thirst, cold, heat, fear, and age. Literally: "I have hunger."

target: Tengo sed.
english: I'm thirsty.

target: ¿Estás bien?
english: Are you okay?

target: Estoy aprendiendo español.
english: I'm learning Spanish.
grammar: Present continuous: estar + gerund (-ando/-iendo). Estoy aprendiendo = I am learning. Estoy comiendo = I am eating.

target: No sé dónde está.
english: I don't know where it is.
grammar: "Saber" (to know facts) vs "conocer" (to know/be familiar with). Sé la respuesta (I know the answer). Conozco Madrid (I know Madrid).

target: Está muy cerca.
english: It's very close.

target: Tengo veinticinco años.
english: I'm twenty-five years old.

target: Encantado de conocerte.
english: Pleased to meet you.

target: No recuerdo bien.
english: I don't remember well.

target: Disculpa, ¿puedo pasar?
english: Excuse me, may I pass?

target: Salgo de casa a las ocho.
english: I leave home at eight.
grammar: "Salir" has an irregular yo form: salgo. Other common -go verbs: hago (hacer), tengo (tener), vengo (venir), pongo (poner), digo (decir).

target: Llego al trabajo a las nueve.
english: I arrive at work at nine.

target: Trabajo hasta las dos de la tarde.
english: I work until two in the afternoon.

target: Tengo treinta y dos años.
english: I'm thirty-two years old.

target: Son las cinco menos cuarto.
english: It's a quarter to five.

target: No como carne, soy vegetariano.
english: I don't eat meat, I'm vegetarian.

target: Hace frío, ponte un abrigo.
english: It's cold, put on a coat.

target: Está lloviendo mucho, lleva paraguas.
english: It's raining a lot, take an umbrella.

target: No puedo respirar bien por la nariz.
english: I can't breathe well through my nose.

target: Quien busca, encuentra.
english: He who seeks, finds.

target: Perdón, ¿por dónde se va al centro?
english: Excuse me, how do you get to the center?

target: Tengo una reunión a las diez.
english: I have a meeting at ten.

target: La empresa tiene cien empleados.
english: The company has a hundred employees.

target: Mi sobrino tiene tres años.
english: My nephew is three years old.

target: Buenos días, ¿cómo estás?
english: Good morning, how are you?

target: Soy de España.
english: I'm from Spain.

target: Son las dos y media.
english: It's two thirty.

target: Hace buen tiempo hoy.
english: The weather is nice today.
grammar: "Hacer" is used for weather: "hace calor" (it's hot), "hace frío" (it's cold).

target: Está lloviendo mucho.
english: It's raining a lot.

target: Es demasiado caro.
english: It's too expensive.

target: Lo siento mucho.
english: I'm very sorry.

target: Quien calla, otorga.
english: Silence gives consent.

target: Están aquí por tres días.
english: They're here for three days.
grammar: "Por" for duration: "Estudié por dos horas." Por marks how long something lasted.

target: Quiero comprártelo.
english: I want to buy it for you.

target: Te quiero mucho.
english: I love you very much.

target: Nos vemos mañana.
english: See you tomorrow.

target: Siéntate aquí.
english: Sit down here.

target: Escúchame bien.
english: Listen to me carefully.

target: No llegues tarde mañana.
english: Don't be late tomorrow.

target: Llámame cuando llegues.
english: Call me when you arrive.

target: Mi vecino tiene tres perros.
english: My neighbor has three dogs.

target: Ella habla cuatro idiomas.
english: She speaks four languages.

target: Yo duermo solo seis horas.
english: I only sleep six hours.

target: Ella sale del trabajo a las cinco.
english: She leaves work at five.

target: La reunión empieza a las tres.
english: The meeting starts at three.
grammar: Present tense for scheduled events: "El tren sale a las 8." Spanish uses present tense for fixed schedules.

target: Ella sonríe mucho.
english: She smiles a lot.

target: Ella lleva gafas.
english: She wears glasses.

target: Mi sobrino tiene cinco años.
english: My nephew is five years old.

target: Mi empresa tiene cien empleados.
english: My company has a hundred employees.

target: Yo odio madrugar.
english: I hate getting up early.

target: Somos cinco en mi familia.
english: There are five of us in my family.
grammar: Ser for inherent facts: use ser for truths that define what something IS.

target: El concierto es a las nueve.
english: The concert is at nine.
grammar: Ser for scheduled events: "La reunión es a las 3." Use ser for when/where events happen.

target: Estamos perdidos.
english: We're lost.
grammar: Estar for states/conditions: "Está cansado." Estar describes how someone feels or a resulting condition — not who they are.

target: Son las tres y media.
english: It's three thirty.
grammar: Ser for time: "Son las tres." Time always uses ser.

target: Ella está embarazada.
english: She's pregnant.
grammar: Estar for conditions: "Está roto." Use estar for the current state/condition of something.

target: Estamos a veinte de marzo.
english: It's March twentieth.
grammar: Estar for dates: "Estamos a 15 de marzo." The current date uses estar.

target: No estoy seguro de eso.
english: I'm not sure about that.
grammar: Estar + adjective for current state: "Estoy seguro." How you feel right now → estar.

target: Ella es soltera.
english: She's single.
grammar: Ser for civil status: "Es casada." Marital status uses ser — it defines identity, not a passing state.

target: Necesito un paraguas.
english: I need an umbrella.

target: Necesito unas tijeras.
english: I need some scissors.

target: Me va bien quedar a las seis.
english: Meeting at six works for me.

target: Salí del trabajo a las siete.
english: I left work at seven.

target: Gracias por todo.
english: Thanks for everything.

target: Paso por tu casa a las ocho.
english: I'll swing by your house at eight.
grammar: "Por" = through/along: "Caminamos por el parque." Por for movement through or past a place.

target: Hablo por experiencia.
english: I speak from experience.

target: Voy por agua.
english: I'm going for water.
grammar: "Por" = to get/fetch: "Fui por pan." Going somewhere to pick something up.

target: Multiplicamos cinco por tres.
english: We multiply five by three.
grammar: "Por" in math: "Dos por tres son seis." Por for multiplication.

target: Avísame cuando termines.
english: Let me know when you finish.

target: Pregúntale al recepcionista.
english: Ask the receptionist.

target: Ellos hablan tres idiomas.
english: They speak three languages.

target: Somos cinco en el equipo.
english: There are five of us on the team.

target: Salgo para Madrid a las siete.
english: I leave for Madrid at seven.
grammar: Para + destination = heading toward

target: No es justo que trabajes tantas horas.
english: It's not fair that you work so many hours.

target: No le digas nada, es una sorpresa.
english: Don't tell him anything, it's a surprise.

target: No cambies de tema.
english: Don't change the subject.

target: No es seguro que abran la frontera mañana.
english: It's not certain they'll open the border tomorrow.

target: No es seguro que haya puestos vacantes.
english: It's not certain there are open positions.

target: Llego al aeropuerto a las ocho.
english: I arrive at the airport at eight.

target: Paso tres días en Barcelona.
english: I spend three days in Barcelona.

target: Llevo mucho equipaje.
english: I carry a lot of luggage.

target: Llego a la oficina a las nueve.
english: I arrive at the office at nine.

target: Termino el trabajo a las seis.
english: I finish work at six.

target: La reunión es a las tres.
english: The meeting is at three.

target: La entrevista es mañana a las diez.
english: The interview is tomorrow at ten.

target: El avión sale a las ocho.
english: The plane leaves at eight.
grammar: "Sale" is the present tense of "salir" (to leave). Irregular: salgo, sales, sale, salimos, salís, salen.

target: Necesito mi pasaporte.
english: I need my passport.

target: Tengo una reserva.
english: I have a reservation.

target: Necesito una toalla.
english: I need a towel.

target: Quiero un recuerdo.
english: I want a souvenir.

target: No hablo mucho español.
english: I don't speak much Spanish.

target: Quiero tres noches.
english: I want three nights.

target: El tren llega a las tres.
english: The train arrives at three.

target: Necesito cambiar dinero.
english: I need to exchange money.

target: Necesito un bolígrafo.
english: I need a pen.

target: Trabajo en equipo.
english: I work in a team.

target: Tengo una reunión a las tres.
english: I have a meeting at three.

target: Trabajo ocho horas al día.
english: I work eight hours a day.

target: Necesito unas grapas.
english: I need some staples.

target: El edificio tiene cinco pisos.
english: The building has five floors.

target: Hay una videoconferencia a las once.
english: There's a video conference at eleven.

target: Soy contable.
english: I'm an accountant.

target: Soy abogada.
english: I'm a lawyer.

target: Soy diseñador gráfico.
english: I'm a graphic designer.

target: Mi hijo tiene tres años.
english: My son is three years old.
grammar: Tener + años = to be X years old (Spanish uses 'have' not 'be')

target: La casa tiene tres habitaciones.
english: The house has three bedrooms.

target: Tenemos comida para tres días.
english: We have food for three days.

target: Mi vecino tiene tres gatos.
english: My neighbor has three cats.

target: Mi jefe habla tres idiomas.
english: My boss speaks three languages.

target: Eso es malo.
english: That is bad.

target: No es bueno ni malo.
english: It is neither good nor bad.

target: No sé.
english: I do not know.

target: Yo también.
english: Me too.

target: Feliz cumpleaños.
english: Happy birthday.

target: Bienvenido.
english: Welcome.

target: Salud.
english: Bless you. / Cheers.

target: Tranquilo.
english: Calm down. / Take it easy.

target: Exacto.
english: Exactly.

target: Cuidado.
english: Careful. / Watch out.

target: Necesito seis huevos.
english: I need six eggs.

target: Tengo ocho primos.
english: I have eight cousins.

target: Hace mucho viento.
english: It's very windy.

target: Tiene buen corazón.
english: He/she has a good heart.

target: No como carne.
english: I don't eat meat.

target: Quiero una cerveza.
english: I want a beer.

target: Comemos muchas verduras.
english: We eat a lot of vegetables.

target: Tengo miedo.
english: I am scared.

target: Cenamos juntos en la cocina.
english: We have dinner together in the kitchen.
grammar: "-amos" ending on "-ar" verbs marks nosotros (we): cenamos = we have dinner. Subject pronouns are usually dropped because the ending tells you who.

target: Hoy desayuné solo un yogur con fruta.
english: Today I only had a yogurt with fruit for breakfast.

target: Cuando llegué a casa empecé a cocinar.
english: When I got home I started cooking.
grammar: "Empecé" — verbs ending in -zar change z→c before -é in preterite: empezar → empecé. Similar: almorzar → almorcé, comenzar → comencé.

target: Los viernes salimos a cenar fuera.
english: On Fridays we go out for dinner.

target: Tardé media hora en llegar al centro.
english: It took me half an hour to get downtown.

target: La reunión duró exactamente una hora.
english: The meeting lasted exactly one hour.

target: Hace dos meses que no la veo.
english: I haven't seen her for two months.

target: Pagamos setecientos euros al mes de alquiler.
english: We pay seven hundred euros a month in rent.

target: El semestre pasado aprobé todas las asignaturas.
english: Last semester I passed all my subjects.

target: Ella es viuda desde hace dos años.
english: She's been a widow for two years.

target: La profesora explicó la lección muy bien.
english: The teacher explained the lesson very well.
grammar: "dedicarse a" = to work in / devote oneself to. "Desde hace" + time = for (duration up to now).

target: Me lastimé la pierna jugando al fútbol.
english: I hurt my leg playing soccer.

target: Tienes que descansar si estás enfermo.
english: You have to rest if you are sick.
grammar: "alojarse" (reflexive) = to stay/lodge. "Cerca de" = near. Preterite or present depending on context.

target: Le dejé un recado en el contestador automático.
english: I left him a message on the answering machine.

target: La prensa publicó un reportaje sobre la contaminación.
english: The press published a report on pollution.

target: Necesito mejorar mi expresión oral para las presentaciones.
english: I need to improve my oral expression for presentations.
grammar: "lo que" = what (that which). "Dijiste" = preterite of "decir" (irregular stem: dij-). Preterite for the specific moment.

target: Me pidió disculpas por el malentendido de ayer.
english: He apologized to me for yesterday's misunderstanding.

target: Se nos acabó la leche.
english: We ran out of milk.
grammar: "Se nos acabó" — "se" construction for unplanned events.

target: Me quedé boquiabierto.
english: I was speechless / jaw-dropped.
grammar: "Boquiabierto" — literally "open-mouthed"; stunned.

target: Hace tres meses que empecé este curso.
english: I started this course three months ago.

target: Ayer hablé con mis amigos.
english: Yesterday I talked with my friends.

target: Ayer jugué con mis amigos.
english: Yesterday I played with my friends.

target: Ayer corrí con mis amigos.
english: Yesterday I ran with my friends.

target: Pasamos por Madrid.
english: We passed through Madrid.
grammar: "Por" = through/along: "Caminamos por el parque." Por for movement through or past a place.

target: Salimos para Madrid.
english: We left for Madrid.
grammar: "Para" = heading to: "Salgo para Madrid." Para for where you're going.

target: Los vi en el parque.
english: I saw them in the park.

target: Nos contó una historia increíble.
english: He told us an incredible story.

target: Ella llegó tarde a la reunión.
english: She arrived late to the meeting.

target: Comimos en un restaurante italiano.
english: We ate at an Italian restaurant.

target: Nos mudamos el mes pasado.
english: We moved last month.

target: Encontré un billete de cincuenta en la calle.
english: I found a fifty-euro bill on the street.

target: Ayer llovió todo el día.
english: Yesterday it rained all day.

target: Él empezó un curso nuevo.
english: He started a new course.

target: Ella se cortó el pelo.
english: She cut her hair.

target: Nació mi sobrina el viernes.
english: My niece was born on Friday.

target: Mi abuelo fumaba mucho pero lo dejó.
english: My grandfather used to smoke a lot but he quit.

target: Nos llamamos por teléfono cada semana.
english: We call each other every week.
grammar: "Llamarse" can be reciprocal: "Nos llamamos" = We call each other. Also reflexive: "Me llamo Ana" = I'm called Ana.

target: Ella se acostumbró al frío.
english: She got used to the cold.

target: Ella se pintó las uñas de rojo.
english: She painted her nails red.

target: Se me rompieron las gafas.
english: My glasses broke.
grammar: "Se me" = accidental/unintentional. "Se me cayó" = It fell on me / I dropped it (accidentally). Removes blame — it just happened.

target: Ella se inscribió en un curso de cocina.
english: She signed up for a cooking class.

target: Me acordé de ti ayer.
english: I thought of you yesterday.
grammar: "Acordarse de" = to remember (always reflexive + de). "Me acuerdo de ti" = I remember you. Compare: "acordar" (no se) = to agree.

target: Te llamé por teléfono tres veces.
english: I called you by phone three times.
grammar: "Por" = via / by means of: "Lo dije por teléfono." Por for the method of communication.

target: Viajamos por carretera.
english: We traveled by road.
grammar: "Por" = via / by way of: "Fuimos por la autopista." Por for the route or means of travel.

target: Los vi en el supermercado.
english: I saw them at the supermarket.

target: Me quedé en blanco durante la presentación.
english: My mind went blank during the presentation.

target: Le dieron gato por liebre en esa tienda.
english: They ripped him off at that store.

target: Mis padres viajan una vez al año.
english: My parents travel once a year.

target: Nosotros vivimos cerca del centro.
english: We live near downtown.

target: Ayer terminé el informe a las seis.
english: Yesterday I finished the report at six.
grammar: Terminar (regular -ar preterite): terminé, terminaste, terminó, terminamos

target: Ella consiguió el puesto que quería.
english: She got the position she wanted.

target: Él pagó la cena para todos.
english: He paid for dinner for everyone.

target: El avión llegó con una hora de retraso.
english: The plane arrived an hour late.

target: Ella abrió su propia tienda el año pasado.
english: She opened her own shop last year.

target: Mi madre me regaló este reloj.
english: My mother gave me this watch as a gift.

target: Ellos decidieron cancelar la reunión.
english: They decided to cancel the meeting.

target: Ella me mandó un correo con los detalles.
english: She sent me an email with the details.

target: Ella cocinó una cena riquísima.
english: She cooked a delicious dinner.

target: Mis abuelos celebraron cincuenta años de casados.
english: My grandparents celebrated fifty years of marriage.

target: Ella corrió cinco kilómetros esta mañana.
english: She ran five kilometers this morning.

target: Olvidé traer los documentos a la reunión.
english: I forgot to bring the documents to the meeting.

target: Mis padres se conocieron en una fiesta.
english: My parents met each other at a party.
grammar: Conocerse (reciprocal preterite): se conocieron — they met each other

target: Se cayó la taza y se rompió.
english: The cup fell and broke.
grammar: Caerse/romperse — unplanned events: se cayó, se rompió

target: Nos mudamos a otra ciudad el año pasado.
english: We moved to another city last year.

target: Me equivoqué de dirección y me perdí.
english: I got the address wrong and got lost.

target: Pasamos por el centro de camino a casa.
english: We passed through downtown on the way home.
grammar: Por = through/along a place

target: Viajamos por toda España durante el verano.
english: We traveled throughout Spain during the summer.

target: Me multaron por estacionar en doble fila.
english: I got fined for double parking.

target: Compré un juguete para mi sobrino.
english: I bought a toy for my nephew.

target: Caminamos por el parque después de comer.
english: We walked through the park after eating.

target: Por suerte, encontramos un hotel barato.
english: Luckily, we found a cheap hotel.
grammar: "Por suerte" = luckily. Introduces a fortunate outcome: "Por suerte, llegamos a tiempo."

target: La vi ayer en el supermercado.
english: I saw her yesterday at the supermarket.

target: Las terminé ayer por la noche.
english: I finished them last night.

target: Lo dejé en casa sin querer.
english: I left it at home by accident.

target: Los vi salir de la oficina juntos.
english: I saw them leave the office together.

target: Aunque no tenía experiencia, le dieron el puesto.
english: Even though he had no experience, they gave him the position.
grammar: "Aunque" + indicative = although (known fact): "Aunque llueve, salgo." Compare: aunque + subjunctive = even if (hypothetical).

target: La casa donde crecí ya no existe.
english: The house where I grew up no longer exists.

target: El barrio donde vivimos es muy tranquilo.
english: The neighborhood where we live is very quiet.

target: Aunque el vuelo se retrasó, llegamos a tiempo.
english: Although the flight was delayed, we arrived on time.

target: Jugamos con los niños en el parque.
english: We play with the kids in the park.

target: El avión despegó con media hora de retraso.
english: The plane took off half an hour late.

target: Visitamos tres museos en un solo día.
english: We visited three museums in a single day.

target: Compré un souvenir para mi madre.
english: I bought a souvenir for my mother.

target: El autobús nos dejó en el centro.
english: The bus dropped us off downtown.

target: El ferry tardó tres horas en llegar.
english: The ferry took three hours to arrive.

target: Probé la comida local y me encantó.
english: I tried the local food and loved it.

target: Terminé el informe antes de las cinco.
english: I finished the report before five.

target: Mi colega renunció la semana pasada.
english: My colleague resigned last week.

target: Recibí un ascenso el mes pasado.
english: I got a promotion last month.

target: El supervisor revisó mi trabajo.
english: The supervisor reviewed my work.

target: Trabajé hasta las nueve de la noche.
english: I worked until nine at night.

target: Mi hijo sacó buenas notas.
english: My son got good grades.

target: Mi abuelo contó una historia muy divertida.
english: My grandfather told a very funny story.

target: Mi primo se mudó a otra ciudad.
english: My cousin moved to another city.

target: Mi hija pintó un cuadro precioso.
english: My daughter painted a beautiful picture.

target: Compramos un sofá nuevo para el salón.
english: We bought a new couch for the living room.

target: Mi tía nos visitó el fin de semana.
english: My aunt visited us over the weekend.

target: Mi hermano y yo jugamos al fútbol.
english: My brother and I played soccer.

target: Mi esposo cocinó por primera vez.
english: My husband cooked for the first time.

target: Nos encontramos en la estación a las ocho.
english: We meet at the station at eight.

target: Pagamos veinte euros por la excursión.
english: We paid twenty euros for the excursion.

target: Salimos para la montaña a las ocho.
english: We set off for the mountains at eight.

target: Compré un souvenir por cinco euros.
english: I bought a souvenir for five euros.

target: Firmé el contrato por tres años.
english: I signed the contract for three years.

target: Llamé a mi madre por teléfono.
english: I called my mother by phone.

target: Compré flores por el aniversario.
english: I bought flowers for the anniversary.

target: Pasamos por el parque de camino a casa.
english: We went through the park on the way home.

target: El guía nos explicó la historia del monumento.
english: The guide explained the monument's history to us.

target: Lo encontré en el autobús.
english: I found it on the bus.

target: La visitamos el último día del viaje.
english: We visited it on the last day of the trip.

target: Lo firmó el director esta mañana.
english: The director signed it this morning.

target: Me dieron el turno de noche.
english: They gave me the night shift.

target: Lo arregló mi padre en cinco minutos.
english: My father fixed it in five minutes.

target: Mi hermana me prestó su carro.
english: My sister lent me her car.

target: Compré los billetes por internet.
english: I bought the tickets online.
grammar: "Por internet" — "por" indicates the means/medium through which something is done.

target: Me senté en el asiento equivocado.
english: I sat in the wrong seat.
grammar: "Sentarse" is reflexive: me senté (I sat down). The pretérito of sentarse: me senté, te sentaste, se sentó.

target: Me desperté tarde y perdí el desayuno.
english: I woke up late and missed breakfast.
grammar: "Despertarse" is reflexive. In pretérito: me desperté, te despertaste, se despertó.

target: La cena costó cuarenta euros.
english: Dinner cost forty euros.

target: Comimos tapas en el mercado.
english: We ate tapas at the market.

target: Dejé propina en la mesa.
english: I left a tip on the table.

target: El taxi tardó veinte minutos en llegar.
english: The taxi took twenty minutes to arrive.

target: El autobús nos dejó en la plaza.
english: The bus dropped us off at the square.

target: Caminamos por el casco antiguo toda la tarde.
english: We walked through the old town all afternoon.
grammar: "Por" for movement through a place. "Caminamos por" = we walked through/around.

target: Me quemé con el sol en la playa.
english: I got sunburned on the beach.

target: Salimos para el aeropuerto a las cinco.
english: We left for the airport at five.
grammar: "Para" indicates destination: we left heading toward the airport.

target: Pasamos por el centro de la ciudad.
english: We passed through the city center.

target: Busqué mi equipaje por toda la cinta.
english: I looked for my luggage all along the belt.

target: Me acosté tarde porque salimos de fiesta.
english: I went to bed late because we went out partying.

target: El taxista nos cobró por persona.
english: The taxi driver charged us per person.

target: Llamamos al hotel para confirmar la hora.
english: We called the hotel to confirm the time.

target: Los horarios españoles me resultaron desconcertantes al principio.
english: Spanish schedules were confusing to me at first.

target: Terminé el informe ayer.
english: I finished the report yesterday.
grammar: Pretérito: terminé (I finished). Regular -ar past tense.

target: Me acostumbré al horario nuevo.
english: I got used to the new schedule.

target: Firmé el contrato ayer por la tarde.
english: I signed the contract yesterday afternoon.

target: Hablé con el departamento de ventas.
english: I spoke with the sales department.

target: Le pasé tu número al cliente.
english: I gave your number to the client.

target: Empecé a trabajar aquí en enero.
english: I started working here in January.

target: El cliente no contestó mi llamada.
english: The client didn't answer my call.

target: El contrato se venció el mes pasado.
english: The contract expired last month.

target: Me estresé mucho con ese proyecto.
english: I got very stressed with that project.

target: El jefe nos invitó a comer.
english: The boss invited us to lunch.

target: Se rompió mi computadora en la peor semana.
english: My computer broke during the worst week.

target: La empresa me pagó el curso.
english: The company paid for my course.

target: Llegué tarde por culpa del tráfico.
english: I arrived late because of traffic.
grammar: Por culpa de = because of (blaming). Llegué = I arrived.

target: El fin de semana trabajé desde casa.
english: I worked from home on the weekend.

target: Me tocó organizar el evento de empresa.
english: I had to organize the company event.

target: El candidato que entrevistamos ayer era muy bueno.
english: The candidate we interviewed yesterday was very good.

target: Vivimos en un departamento pequeño.
english: We live in a small apartment.
grammar: Piso = apartment (Spain). In Latin America: departamento or apartamento

target: Mi abuela nos visitó el domingo.
english: My grandmother visited us on Sunday.

target: Mi madre me llamó por teléfono ayer.
english: My mother called me on the phone yesterday.

target: Ayer lavé toda la ropa.
english: Yesterday I washed all the clothes.

target: Los niños se despertaron a las seis.
english: The kids woke up at six.
grammar: Despertarse = reflexive (to wake up). Preterite: se despertaron

target: Mi hermana se mudó a Barcelona.
english: My sister moved to Barcelona.

target: Mi hermano me ayudó con la mudanza.
english: My brother helped me with the move.

target: Mi hijo perdió su mochila en el colegio.
english: My son lost his backpack at school.

target: Los niños se divirtieron mucho en la fiesta.
english: The kids had a lot of fun at the party.
grammar: Divertirse: stem-changing in preterite (e→i): se divirtieron

target: Mi padre se jubiló el año pasado.
english: My father retired last year.
grammar: Jubilarse = to retire (reflexive): se jubiló

target: Le peiné el pelo a mi hija.
english: I combed my daughter's hair.

target: Mi padre me enseñó a montar en bici.
english: My father taught me to ride a bike.

target: Los niños se aburrieron en la cena.
english: The kids got bored at dinner.

target: Mi madre me abrazó al verme.
english: My mother hugged me when she saw me.

target: Nos reunimos toda la familia por Año Nuevo.
english: The whole family got together for New Year's.
grammar: Reunirse = to get together (reflexive). Preterite: nos reunimos

target: Mi tío nos contó una historia muy divertida.
english: My uncle told us a very funny story.

target: Mi abuela se alegró mucho de vernos.
english: My grandmother was very happy to see us.

target: Nos divertimos mucho en la fiesta de anoche.
english: We had a lot of fun at last night's party.

target: Se quejaron del ruido durante toda la noche.
english: They complained about the noise all night.

target: Se despidió de todos antes de irse.
english: He said goodbye to everyone before leaving.

target: Nos mudamos a esta ciudad hace cinco años.
english: We moved to this city five years ago.

target: Se enamoraron durante un viaje a París.
english: They fell in love during a trip to Paris.

target: Me olvidé de comprar leche en el supermercado.
english: I forgot to buy milk at the supermarket.

target: Se acostumbró rápidamente al nuevo horario.
english: He quickly got used to the new schedule.

target: Lo cambiaron por otro modelo más nuevo.
english: They exchanged it for another newer model.

target: Por suerte, encontramos el hotel fácilmente.
english: Luckily, we found the hotel easily.

target: No existe nadie que pueda resolver esto solo.
english: There's nobody who can solve this alone.

target: Aunque parezca mentira, lo logré sin ayuda.
english: Hard as it may be to believe, I did it without help.

target: Preguntó si podíamos enviarle los documentos por correo.
english: He asked if we could send him the documents by email.

target: Habrán sido las tres cuando llegamos al pueblo.
english: It must have been about three when we arrived at the town.

target: Le echó una mano con las maletas.
english: He gave him a hand with the suitcases.

target: Nosotros vivimos cerca de la playa.
english: We live near the beach.

target: Nosotros visitamos el museo ayer.
english: We visited the museum yesterday.

target: Él perdió las llaves del coche.
english: He lost the car keys.

target: Yo encontré un billete en el suelo.
english: I found a ticket on the ground.

target: Ellos comieron paella en la playa.
english: They ate paella at the beach.

target: El vuelo salió con retraso.
english: The flight departed late.

target: Él pagó la cuenta del restaurante.
english: He paid the restaurant bill.

target: Ellos se mudaron a otra ciudad.
english: They moved to another city.

target: Mi padre reparó la bicicleta.
english: My father fixed the bicycle.

target: Yo hablé con el director ayer.
english: I spoke with the director yesterday.

target: Ella conoció a su esposo en la universidad.
english: She met her husband at university.

target: Él vendió su coche viejo.
english: He sold his old car.

target: Ella terminó el proyecto a tiempo.
english: She finished the project on time.

target: Tú olvidaste tu pasaporte en casa.
english: You forgot your passport at home.

target: Él rompió el jarrón por accidente.
english: He broke the vase by accident.

target: Caminamos por el centro de la ciudad.
english: We walked through the city center.

target: Pagué mucho por este billete.
english: I paid a lot for this ticket.

target: Ella nos invitó a su fiesta.
english: She invited us to her party.

target: La tienda donde compro el pan cerró.
english: The shop where I buy bread closed.

target: El regalo que me dieron es perfecto.
english: The gift they gave me is perfect.

target: A pesar del ruido, logré dormir bien.
english: Despite the noise, I managed to sleep well.

target: Se encontraron restos arqueológicos en la zona.
english: Archaeological remains were found in the area.

target: Después del trabajo fui al gimnasio una hora.
english: After work I went to the gym for an hour.

target: El sábado pasado fui al cine.
english: Last Saturday I went to the movies.
grammar: present progressive = estar + gerund (-ando/-iendo). Describes what's happening RIGHT NOW. "Llueve" (simple present) = it rains / it's raining in general.

target: El año pasado fue un año muy difícil.
english: Last year was a very difficult year.
grammar: days of the week are masculine: el lunes, el martes. They don't capitalize in Spanish. "Próximo" = next.

target: Estuvimos esperando durante cuarenta minutos bajo la lluvia.
english: We were waiting for forty minutes in the rain.
grammar: "medio" (half) agrees with the noun: medio kilo (masc), media hora (fem). "Un litro y medio" — "medio" after.

target: Anteayer fue el treinta de noviembre.
english: The day before yesterday was the thirtieth of November.

target: Esta catedral fue construida a mediados del siglo dieciséis.
english: This cathedral was built in the mid-sixteenth century.

target: Hicimos un álbum de fotos familiar para el aniversario de bodas de mis padres.
english: We made a family photo album for my parents' wedding anniversary.
grammar: "su" can mean his/her/their/your. "De bodas" = of wedding. "Bodas" is usually plural in Spanish.

target: El camarero nos trajo el pan y las bebidas enseguida.
english: The waiter brought us the bread and drinks right away.

target: Me pusieron una multa por estacionar en doble fila.
english: I got a fine for double parking.

target: El paquete llegó dañado y tuve que pedir una devolución.
english: The package arrived damaged and I had to request a return.

target: La entrevista de trabajo fue bastante bien.
english: The job interview went quite well.
grammar: "tener que" + infinitive = to have to (personal obligation). "Antes de" + noun = before. "Del" = de + el.

target: La enfermera me puso una inyección en el brazo.
english: The nurse gave me a shot in the arm.

target: Me tropecé y me hice daño en la rodilla.
english: I tripped and hurt my knee.

target: Le pusieron una escayola en la pierna rota.
english: They put a cast on his broken leg.

target: Tuvo que quedarse ingresado en el hospital durante tres días.
english: He had to stay admitted in the hospital for three days.

target: Le hicieron una resonancia magnética para examinar la columna vertebral.
english: They did an MRI to examine his spine.

target: Tuvimos una discusión muy acalorada sobre política.
english: We had a very heated discussion about politics.

target: No quise decir eso; me expresé mal.
english: I didn't mean that — I expressed myself poorly.

target: Desde mi punto de vista, la negociación fue un éxito.
english: From my point of view, the negotiation was a success.

target: Hicimos senderismo por una ruta preciosa con vistas al valle.
english: We went hiking along a beautiful trail with views of the valley.

target: La negociación fue muy difícil.
english: The negotiation was very difficult.

target: Ayer fui al cine con mis amigos.
english: Yesterday I went to the cinema with my friends.
grammar: "Fui" — irregular preterite of "ir" (to go).

target: La película fue muy divertida.
english: The movie was very funny.

target: El día en que nos conocimos fue especial.
english: The day we met was special.

target: Puse la maleta en el armario.
english: I put the suitcase in the closet.

target: Él tuvo un accidente de carro.
english: He had a car accident.

target: Él trajo flores para su madre.
english: He brought flowers for his mother.

target: Estuve enfermo toda la semana.
english: I was sick all week.

target: Ella se fue sin decir adiós.
english: She left without saying goodbye.

target: Él se puso contento al oír la noticia.
english: He became happy when he heard the news.

target: Lo que me dijiste ayer me sorprendió.
english: What you told me yesterday surprised me.
grammar: "Lo que" = what / that which: "Lo que dices es verdad." Used for abstract ideas or things not yet identified.

target: La playa donde fuimos el verano pasado estaba limpia.
english: The beach where we went last summer was clean.

target: Mi padre siempre quiso que fuéramos a la universidad.
english: My father always wanted us to go to university.

target: La carta fue enviada ayer por la tarde.
english: The letter was sent yesterday afternoon.

target: Los resultados fueron publicados la semana pasada.
english: The results were published last week.

target: La propuesta fue rechazada por la mayoría.
english: The proposal was rejected by the majority.

target: La ley fue aprobada por unanimidad.
english: The law was approved unanimously.

target: La empresa fue fundada en mil novecientos noventa.
english: The company was founded in 1990.

target: Lo hizo a propósito para molestarme.
english: He did it on purpose to annoy me.

target: No pude pegar ojo en toda la noche.
english: I couldn't sleep a wink all night.

target: Se me fue el santo al cielo y olvidé la cita.
english: It completely slipped my mind and I forgot the appointment.
grammar: Irse el santo al cielo — to completely forget, mind goes blank

target: Le puso verde delante de todos en la oficina.
english: She badmouthed him in front of everyone at the office.

target: El día fue bastante largo.
english: The day was quite long.

target: Fuimos al supermercado después del trabajo.
english: We went to the supermarket after work.

target: Recibí tu mensaje pero no pude contestar.
english: I got your message but I couldn't answer.
grammar: "Poder" has an irregular preterite stem (pude...). These irregular preterites share the same endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. No accents needed.

target: Ayer hice tres entrevistas.
english: Yesterday I did three interviews.
grammar: "Hacer" has an irregular preterite stem (hice...). These irregular preterites share the same endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. No accents needed.

target: Mi hermana tuvo un bebé en enero.
english: My sister had a baby in January.
grammar: "Tener" has an irregular preterite stem (tuve...). These irregular preterites share the same endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. No accents needed.

target: Le dije la verdad desde el principio.
english: I told him the truth from the beginning.
grammar: "Decir" has an irregular preterite stem (dije...). These irregular preterites share the same endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. No accents needed.

target: Ella se fue de la empresa en marzo.
english: She left the company in March.

target: Fui al médico la semana pasada.
english: I went to the doctor last week.

target: Ella puso las flores en la mesa.
english: She put the flowers on the table.
grammar: "Poner" has an irregular preterite stem (puse...). These irregular preterites share the same endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. No accents needed.

target: Por eso no pude ir a la cena.
english: That's why I couldn't go to the dinner.
grammar: "Por eso" = that's why / because of that. Connects a cause to its consequence: "Llovía, por eso me quedé."

target: La manera en que resolvió el problema fue brillante.
english: The way he solved the problem was brilliant.

target: El momento en que supe la verdad cambió todo.
english: The moment I found out the truth, everything changed.

target: La razón por la cual cerró la tienda fue económica.
english: The reason the store closed was financial.
grammar: "Por la cual" = for which (formal): "La razón por la cual..." More literary than "por la que" but same meaning.

target: La propuesta fue aprobada por unanimidad.
english: The proposal was approved unanimously.

target: La carta fue enviada ayer por correo certificado.
english: The letter was sent yesterday by registered mail.

target: Los documentos fueron entregados a tiempo.
english: The documents were delivered on time.

target: Las obras fueron terminadas el mes pasado.
english: The construction was finished last month.

target: La solicitud fue rechazada sin explicación.
english: The application was rejected without explanation.

target: Las quejas fueron escuchadas por la dirección.
english: The complaints were heard by management.

target: A pesar del cansancio, fuimos a ver el atardecer.
english: Despite the tiredness, we went to see the sunset.

target: No pude dormir en toda la noche.
english: I couldn't sleep all night.

target: Tu presentación fue mejor que la mía.
english: Your presentation was better than mine.

target: El cumpleaños de este año fue mejor que el anterior.
english: This year's birthday was better than last year's.

target: La ciudad que visitamos fue preciosa.
english: The city we visited was beautiful.

target: La reunión que tuvimos fue muy útil.
english: The meeting we had was very useful.

target: La formación que recibimos fue muy completa.
english: The training we received was very thorough.

target: El regalo que le hicimos a mamá le encantó.
english: Mom loved the gift we gave her.

target: Mi abuelo, que fue pescador, nos contaba historias del mar.
english: My grandfather, who was a fisherman, used to tell us sea stories.

target: La catedral fue construida en el siglo quince.
english: The cathedral was built in the fifteenth century.

target: La reserva fue confirmada por correo electrónico.
english: The reservation was confirmed by email.

target: La ruta fue diseñada para turistas.
english: The route was designed for tourists.

target: La cena fue preparada por mi padre.
english: Dinner was prepared by my father.

target: La tarta fue hecha en casa.
english: The cake was homemade.

target: La boda fue celebrada en el pueblo.
english: The wedding was held in the village.

target: La habitación fue decorada para el cumpleaños.
english: The room was decorated for the birthday.

target: La foto fue tomada en la boda de mis padres.
english: The photo was taken at my parents' wedding.

target: La mascota fue encontrada por los vecinos.
english: The pet was found by the neighbors.

target: Fui despedido por llegar tarde.
english: I was fired for arriving late.

target: Mi hermana vino por la tarde.
english: My sister came in the afternoon.

target: Fui al supermercado por la leche.
english: I went to the supermarket for the milk.

target: Mi tía hizo un pastel por nosotros.
english: My aunt made a cake for us.

target: Pedimos una botella de vino tinto.
english: We ordered a bottle of red wine.

target: Hicimos las maletas la noche anterior.
english: We packed our bags the night before.

target: Fuimos al mercado y compramos fruta.
english: We went to the market and bought fruit.

target: Nos hicimos una foto con la torre de fondo.
english: We took a photo with the tower in the background.

target: La aduana nos hizo abrir todas las maletas.
english: Customs made us open all the suitcases.

target: La playa que más me gustó fue la del norte.
english: The beach I liked the most was the one in the north.

target: La excursión que hicimos al volcán fue agotadora.
english: The excursion we did to the volcano was exhausting.

target: La catedral fue construida en el siglo trece.
english: The cathedral was built in the thirteenth century.

target: La excursión fue organizada por una agencia local.
english: The excursion was organized by a local agency.

target: Fue tal la impresión que nos causó que decidimos quedarnos una semana más.
english: The impression it made on us was such that we decided to stay one more week.
grammar: "Tal... que" (such... that) — consecutive construction expressing result/consequence.

target: A falta de transporte público, tuvimos que ir andando.
english: For lack of public transport, we had to go on foot.

target: Ayer tuvimos una reunión muy larga.
english: Yesterday we had a very long meeting.

target: No pude asistir a la reunión.
english: I couldn't attend the meeting.

target: La entrevista fue muy bien.
english: The interview went very well.

target: Estuve en una conferencia toda la semana.
english: I was at a conference all week.
grammar: Estuve = pretérito of estar. For temporary past location.

target: No me gustó cómo fue la reunión.
english: I didn't like how the meeting went.

target: El proyecto anterior fue un éxito.
english: The previous project was a success.

target: Ayer hubo un problema con el sistema.
english: Yesterday there was a problem with the system.
grammar: Hubo = pretérito of haber (there was/were). Singular form even for plurals.

target: Fui a una feria de empleo la semana pasada.
english: I went to a job fair last week.

target: La propuesta fue rechazada por la junta directiva.
english: The proposal was rejected by the board of directors.

target: Los resultados fueron presentados ante el consejo de administración.
english: The results were presented to the board of directors.

target: La factura fue emitida con un error de cálculo.
english: The invoice was issued with a calculation error.

target: Los datos fueron analizados por el departamento de finanzas.
english: The data was analyzed by the finance department.

target: La empresa fue adquirida por un grupo multinacional.
english: The company was acquired by a multinational group.

target: Las metas fueron establecidas al comienzo del trimestre.
english: The goals were set at the beginning of the quarter.

target: La sede fue trasladada a las afueras de la ciudad.
english: The headquarters was relocated to the outskirts of the city.

target: Los gastos fueron reducidos en un veinte por ciento.
english: Expenses were reduced by twenty percent.

target: La política de teletrabajo fue aprobada por unanimidad.
english: The remote work policy was approved unanimously.

target: La auditoría fue realizada por una firma externa.
english: The audit was carried out by an external firm.

target: Los beneficios fueron repartidos entre los accionistas.
english: The profits were distributed among the shareholders.

target: Las entrevistas fueron realizadas durante la primera semana de marzo.
english: The interviews were conducted during the first week of March.

target: Los cambios fueron bien recibidos por la mayoría del personal.
english: The changes were well received by most of the staff.

target: La marca fue registrada a nombre de la empresa matriz.
english: The brand was registered under the parent company's name.

target: Mi tío nos trajo fruta del pueblo.
english: My uncle brought us fruit from the village.

target: Hicimos una fiesta para el cumpleaños de papá.
english: We threw a party for dad's birthday.

target: Mi hermano se fue de casa a los veinte años.
english: My brother left home at twenty.

target: Los niños hicieron un dibujo para la abuela.
english: The kids made a drawing for grandma.

target: Mi hermana se fue a vivir al extranjero.
english: My sister went to live abroad.

target: Le hicimos una tarta sorpresa a mamá.
english: We made mom a surprise cake.

target: Fui al mercado por mi madre.
english: I went to the market for my mother.
grammar: Por = on behalf of: fui por mi madre (I went in her place)

target: El perro se puso muy contento al vernos.
english: The dog got very happy when he saw us.

target: La habitación del niño fue decorada con motivos marinos.
english: The boy's room was decorated with a nautical theme.

target: Las normas de la casa fueron establecidas desde el principio.
english: The house rules were established from the beginning.

target: La carta fue enviada ayer por la mañana.
english: The letter was sent yesterday morning.

target: Los resultados fueron publicados esta mañana.
english: The results were published this morning.

target: La reunión fue cancelada sin previo aviso.
english: The meeting was cancelled without prior notice.

target: La decisión fue tomada de forma unánime.
english: The decision was made unanimously.

target: La ciudad fue fundada hace quinientos años.
english: The city was founded five hundred years ago.

target: La nueva ley fue aprobada el mes pasado.
english: The new law was approved last month.

target: Los paquetes fueron entregados esta mañana.
english: The packages were delivered this morning.

target: La carretera fue reparada después del terremoto.
english: The road was repaired after the earthquake.

target: La exposición fue inaugurada por la alcaldesa.
english: The exhibition was inaugurated by the mayor.

target: En lugar de tomar un taxi, fuimos caminando.
english: Instead of taking a taxi, we walked.

target: Me puse nerviosa antes de la presentación.
english: I got nervous before the presentation.

target: Estuvimos caminando por la playa durante horas.
english: We were walking along the beach for hours.

target: Fueron multados por exceso de velocidad.
english: They were fined for speeding.

target: La idea que propusiste ayer fue la mejor.
english: The idea you proposed yesterday was the best.

target: El motivo por el cual no vine fue una emergencia familiar.
english: The reason I didn't come was a family emergency.
grammar: Por el cual = for which, more formal than por el que.

target: Me dejó plantado; esperé una hora y no vino.
english: He stood me up (lit. he left me planted) — I waited an hour and he didn't come.

target: Le puso los puntos sobre las íes al nuevo empleado.
english: She set things straight with the new employee.

target: La playa donde fuimos era preciosa.
english: The beach where we went was beautiful.

target: El tren que tomamos fue puntual.
english: The train we took was on time.

target: El médico que me atendió fue amable.
english: The doctor who treated me was kind.

target: Así como lo dijo, así se hizo.
english: As he said it, so it was done.

target: Se me fue el santo al cielo y olvide la cita.
english: I completely forgot and missed the appointment.
grammar: Irse el santo al cielo = to completely forget what you were doing.

target: Fue precisamente alli donde descubri mi pasion por la pintura.
english: It was precisely there that I discovered my passion for painting.

target: Una copa de vino tinto.
english: A glass of red wine.

target: Me di una ducha rápida porque no tenía mucho tiempo.
english: I took a quick shower because I didn't have much time.

target: Hoy me tomé el día libre porque necesitaba descansar.
english: Today I took the day off because I needed to rest.

target: Normalmente me echo una siesta corta después del almuerzo.
english: I normally take a short nap after lunch.

target: Intento no usar el celular durante la comida, pero no siempre lo consigo.
english: I try not to use my phone during meals, but I don't always manage to.
grammar: "Lo consigo" — "lo" replaces a whole clause ("not using my phone"). "Conseguir" (e→i) = to manage/succeed: consigo, consigues, consigue.

target: A medianoche las calles están vacías.
english: At midnight the streets are empty.

target: Mi abuelo nos cuenta historias de cuando era joven.
english: My grandfather tells us stories from when he was young.

target: Mi abuelo paterno era carpintero y hacía muebles a mano.
english: My paternal grandfather was a carpenter and made furniture by hand.

target: La mujer del kiosco siempre me pregunta por mis hijos.
english: The woman at the newsstand always asks me about my children.

target: Discutimos a veces, pero siempre nos reconciliamos rápido.
english: We argue sometimes, but we always make up quickly.

target: Encendí la luz porque la habitación estaba oscura.
english: I turned on the light because the room was dark.
grammar: "estar a" + distance/time for location relative to here. "Andando" (walking) = gerund used as adverb of manner.

target: Tomo el autobús número doce todos los días.
english: I take bus number twelve every day.

target: Las olas eran enormes y los surfistas estaban encantados.
english: The waves were huge and the surfers were thrilled.

target: Llamamos a una ambulancia porque era una emergencia.
english: We called an ambulance because it was an emergency.

target: Me apena enormemente que no hayamos podido despedirnos como merecíamos.
english: It saddens me enormously that we weren't able to say goodbye as we deserved.

target: Su sueño es crear una empresa de tecnología.
english: His dream is to create a technology company.

target: Si hubiéramos llegado a tiempo, habríamos visto el espectáculo.
english: If we had arrived on time, we would have seen the show.

target: Los niños de hoy saben más de tecnología que nosotros.
english: Kids today know more about technology than we do.

target: La gastronomía española es famosa por su variedad y calidad.
english: Spanish gastronomy is famous for its variety and quality.

target: El paisaje era tan bonito que paramos para hacer fotos.
english: The landscape was so beautiful that we stopped to take photos.
grammar: "Tan...que" = so...that: "Es tan alto que no cabe." Intensifier + adjective/adverb + que + result clause.

target: Si hubiéramos planificado mejor, no tendríamos estos problemas.
english: If we had planned better, we wouldn't have these problems.

target: Es triste que no podamos vernos más a menudo.
english: It's sad that we can't see each other more often.

target: Si viviéramos más cerca, nos veríamos todos los días.
english: If we lived closer, we'd see each other every day.

target: Aunque estemos lejos, siempre nos mantenemos en contacto.
english: Even though we're far away, we always stay in touch.

target: No creía que fuera posible.
english: I didn't believe it was possible.

target: Si hubiéramos salido antes, no habríamos llegado tarde.
english: If we had left earlier, we wouldn't have arrived late.

target: Habríamos ganado si hubiéramos jugado mejor.
english: We would have won if we had played better.

target: Si me hubieras escuchado, no tendríamos este problema.
english: If you had listened to me, we wouldn't have this problem.

target: Si hubieran llegado a tiempo, habrían visto el espectáculo.
english: If they had arrived on time, they would have seen the show.

target: El liderazgo efectivo requiere empatía y visión.
english: Effective leadership requires empathy and vision.

target: Mi madre camina una hora cada día.
english: My mother walks one hour every day.

target: Yo siempre olvido las contraseñas.
english: I always forget passwords.

target: De pequeño quería ser astronauta.
english: As a kid, I wanted to be an astronaut.

target: Mi padre siempre nos contaba un cuento antes de dormir.
english: My father always used to tell us a story before bed.

target: Antes este barrio era más tranquilo.
english: This neighborhood used to be quieter.

target: El jefe siempre llegaba el primero.
english: The boss always arrived first.
