target: Ella llevaba un vestido rojo.
english: She was wearing a red dress.
grammar: Imperfect for past descriptions: "Era alto y tenía pelo negro." Use imperfect to set the scene — describing people, places, weather, time in the past.

target: Antes no existían los celulares.
english: Before, cellphones didn't exist.

target: Mi tía vivía sola desde hacía años.
english: My aunt had been living alone for years.

target: De pequeño odiaba las verduras.
english: As a kid, I hated vegetables.

target: Mi profesor favorito siempre usaba corbata.
english: My favorite teacher always wore a tie.

target: Antes había más árboles en esta calle.
english: There used to be more trees on this street.

target: De joven creía que todo era posible.
english: When I was young, I believed anything was possible.

target: Siempre cenábamos juntos en familia.
english: We always used to have dinner together as a family.

target: Trabajo para una empresa de tecnología.
english: I work for a tech company.
grammar: "Para" = for (employer): "Trabajo para Google." Para for who you work for.

target: Manejo por esta calle todos los días.
english: I drive down this street every day.

target: Lo veo todos los días en el trabajo.
english: I see him every day at work.

target: La vecina que tiene gatos siempre es muy simpática.
english: The neighbor who has cats is always very nice.

target: El día en que nos conocimos llovía mucho.
english: The day we met it was raining a lot.

target: La oficina donde trabajaba antes era más pequeña.
english: The office where I used to work was smaller.

target: Necesitábamos que alguien nos tradujera el documento.
english: We needed someone to translate the document for us.

target: No creía que fuera tan difícil encontrar estacionamiento.
english: I didn't think it would be so hard to find parking.

target: Si hubiéramos pedido ayuda, habríamos acabado antes.
english: If we had asked for help, we would have finished earlier.

target: Si hubiéramos hablado antes, habríamos evitado este problema.
english: If we had talked earlier, we would have avoided this problem.

target: Si hubieras revisado el correo, habrías visto mi mensaje.
english: If you had checked your email, you would have seen my message.

target: El plan parecía bueno; ahora bien, necesitamos más datos.
english: The plan seemed good — now then, we need more data.
grammar: "Ahora bien" = now then / however. Introduces a reservation or counterpoint to what was just said.

target: Siempre como en la oficina los martes.
english: I always eat at the office on Tuesdays.

target: De pequeño jugaba al fútbol todos los días.
english: As a kid I played soccer every day.
grammar: Imperfect for habitual past: jugaba — used to play/would play

target: De niño yo tenía miedo a la oscuridad.
english: As a child I was afraid of the dark.

target: Ella siempre llegaba la primera a la oficina.
english: She was always the first one at the office.

target: Antes salíamos todos los viernes por la noche.
english: We used to go out every Friday night.

target: Hacía mucho calor y las ventanas estaban abiertas.
english: It was very hot and the windows were open.
grammar: Imperfect for background descriptions: hacía calor, estaban abiertas

target: El jefe anterior era más flexible con los horarios.
english: The previous boss was more flexible with schedules.

target: Ella siempre traía fruta de su jardín a la oficina.
english: She always used to bring fruit from her garden to the office.

target: Nosotros compartíamos habitación de niños.
english: We used to share a room as kids.

target: El autobús pasaba cada media hora.
english: The bus used to come every half hour.

target: Antes teníamos reuniones todos los lunes.
english: We used to have meetings every Monday.

target: Los fines de semana yo dormía hasta tarde.
english: On weekends I used to sleep in.

target: Antes las cosas eran más baratas.
english: Things used to be cheaper before.

target: Nos sentamos siempre en la misma mesa.
english: We always sit at the same table.

target: El sitio donde quedamos estaba cerrado.
english: The place where we were meeting was closed.

target: El hotel donde nos alojamos era muy acogedor.
english: The hotel where we stayed was very cozy.

target: No esperaba que fuera tan difícil.
english: I didn't expect it to be so difficult.

target: Si hubieras reservado con antelación, habrías conseguido mejor precio.
english: If you had booked in advance, you would have gotten a better price.

target: No habríamos tenido este problema si hubieran revisado el contrato.
english: We wouldn't have had this problem if they had reviewed the contract.

target: Nos habríamos perdido si no hubieras traído el mapa.
english: We would have gotten lost if you hadn't brought the map.

target: Si hubiéramos sabido del atasco, habríamos tomado otra ruta.
english: If we had known about the traffic jam, we would have taken another route.

target: Si hubiéramos negociado mejor, habríamos cerrado el trato.
english: If we had negotiated better, we would have closed the deal.

target: A pesar de ser lunes, la oficina estaba vacía.
english: Despite it being Monday, the office was empty.
grammar: A pesar de + infinitive — despite + -ing

target: Se me ha ido el santo al cielo, ¿de qué estábamos hablando?
english: I totally lost my train of thought, what were we talking about?

target: Siempre desayuno algo ligero antes de salir.
english: I always have something light for breakfast before leaving.

target: Mi padre siempre dice que hay que madrugar.
english: My father always says you have to get up early.

target: El restaurante donde cenamos estaba muy lleno.
english: The restaurant where we had dinner was very full.

target: El guía que nos atendió hablaba tres idiomas.
english: The guide who helped us spoke three languages.

target: La comida que probamos era deliciosa.
english: The food we tried was delicious.

target: El mercado donde compramos recuerdos era enorme.
english: The market where we bought souvenirs was huge.

target: El barrio donde nos perdimos era muy pintoresco.
english: The neighborhood where we got lost was very picturesque.

target: El candidato que entrevistamos ayer tenía mucha experiencia.
english: The candidate we interviewed yesterday had a lot of experience.

target: La cena que preparé no salió como esperaba.
english: The dinner I prepared didn't turn out as I expected.

target: Habríamos adoptado un perro si el departamento fuera más grande.
english: We would have adopted a dog if the apartment were bigger.

target: Mientras tanto, podemos visitar el mercado.
english: Meanwhile, we can visit the market.

target: La playa estaba siempre llena en agosto.
english: The beach was always full in August.

target: El tren llegaba tarde todos los días.
english: The train arrived late every day.

target: Recorríamos los mercados buscando recuerdos.
english: We wandered through markets looking for souvenirs.

target: El presupuesto era más limitado antes.
english: The budget was more limited before.

target: Mi colega llegaba tarde todos los días.
english: My colleague arrived late every day.

target: El supervisor revisaba todo antes de enviar.
english: The supervisor reviewed everything before sending.

target: El gerente siempre pedía más datos.
english: The manager always asked for more data.

target: Enviaba correos a los clientes cada mañana.
english: I sent emails to clients every morning.

target: Mis compañeros de trabajo eran muy amables.
english: My coworkers were very kind.

target: Cada trimestre presentábamos los resultados.
english: Every quarter we presented the results.

target: Los niños jugaban en la calle hasta las nueve.
english: The kids played in the street until nine.

target: Mi hermana y yo compartíamos habitación.
english: My sister and I shared a room.

target: Limpiábamos la casa entre todos.
english: We all cleaned the house together.

target: Mi padre nos contaba chistes en la cena.
english: My father told us jokes at dinner.

target: Mi madre nos despertaba temprano para el colegio.
english: My mother woke us up early for school.

target: El vuelo iba lleno y no pudimos sentarnos juntos.
english: The flight was full and we couldn't sit together.

target: Cuantas más ciudades visitábamos, más difícil era elegir una favorita.
english: The more cities we visited, the harder it was to choose a favorite.

target: Suponiendo que el tiempo mejore, podríamos hacer la ruta costera.
english: Assuming the weather improves, we could do the coastal route.

target: Antes trabajaba en una fábrica.
english: I used to work in a factory.
grammar: Imperfecto: trabajaba = I used to work / I was working.

target: Mi antiguo jefe era muy estricto.
english: My former boss was very strict.

target: Ayer no había nadie en la oficina.
english: Yesterday there was nobody in the office.

target: El jefe que teníamos antes era más flexible.
english: The boss we had before was more flexible.

target: Habríamos evitado el problema si hubiéramos leído las condiciones.
english: We would have avoided the problem if we had read the conditions.

target: Me acosté tarde porque el niño tenía fiebre.
english: I went to bed late because the boy had a fever.

target: Me levanté a las tres porque el bebé lloraba.
english: I got up at three because the baby was crying.

target: El restaurante donde celebramos la boda era precioso.
english: The restaurant where we held the wedding was beautiful.

target: No pensaba que fuera a llover hoy.
english: I didn't think it was going to rain today.

target: Si hubiéramos salido antes, habríamos llegado a tiempo.
english: If we had left earlier, we would have arrived on time.

target: Si hubieras venido a la fiesta, te habrías divertido mucho.
english: If you had come to the party, you would have had a lot of fun.

target: Si hubiéramos comprado la casa, ahora tendríamos hipoteca.
english: If we had bought the house, we would now have a mortgage.

target: Si hubiéramos tomado otro camino, no habríamos visto el accidente.
english: If we had taken another route, we wouldn't have seen the accident.

target: Mientras tanto, puedes ir preparando la mesa.
english: In the meantime, you can set the table.

target: Por favor, envíeme el informe antes del mediodía.
english: Please send me the report before noon.

target: Le dije que no podía ir porque tenía trabajo.
english: I told him I couldn't go because I had work.

target: Me contó que de pequeño quería ser astronauta.
english: He told me that as a child he wanted to be an astronaut.

target: Me preguntaron si sabía dónde estaba la estación.
english: They asked me if I knew where the station was.

target: De haberse enterado antes, habríamos actuado de otra manera.
english: Had we found out earlier, we would have acted differently.

target: Yo jugaba al fútbol de niño.
english: I used to play soccer as a child.

target: Nosotros íbamos a la playa cada verano.
english: We went to the beach every summer.

target: Mi abuela cocinaba los mejores postres.
english: My grandmother cooked the best desserts.

target: De pequeño, yo leía mucho.
english: As a child, I read a lot.

target: Mi padre trabajaba hasta muy tarde.
english: My father worked very late.

target: Tú siempre sonreías en las fotos.
english: You always smiled in photos.

target: Nosotros caminábamos al colegio juntos.
english: We walked to school together.

target: Nosotros compartíamos todo entre hermanos.
english: We shared everything among siblings.

target: Mi abuelo pescaba en el río.
english: My grandfather fished in the river.

target: El restaurante donde cenamos estaba lleno.
english: The restaurant where we dined was full.

target: El país donde viví era muy cálido.
english: The country where I lived was very warm.

target: El perro que encontramos estaba perdido.
english: The dog we found was lost.

target: La calle donde jugábamos era tranquila.
english: The street where we played was quiet.

target: El hotel donde nos quedamos era barato.
english: The hotel where we stayed was cheap.

target: El coche que vendí era muy viejo.
english: The car I sold was very old.

target: El parque que visitamos tenía un lago.
english: The park we visited had a lake.

target: Me preguntó si tenía hambre.
english: She asked me if I was hungry.

target: Mi companero me comento que estaba buscando otro trabajo.
english: My colleague told me he was looking for another job.

target: Segun recursos humanos, las vacaciones empezaban el dia quince.
english: According to HR, the vacation started on the fifteenth.

target: Llueve sobre mojado; ya teniamos bastantes problemas.
english: It never rains but it pours (lit. it rains on the wet) — we already had enough problems.
grammar: Llover sobre mojado = when bad things pile up on existing problems.

target: Eramos una y carne; haciamos todo juntas.
english: We were inseparable — we did everything together.

target: Cuanta mas informacion teniamos, mas confusa se volvia la situacion.
english: The more information we had, the more confusing the situation became.
grammar: Comparative correlative: Cuanto más...más = the more...the more.

target: Camino al trabajo todos los días.
english: I walk to work every day.

target: Mientras esperaba el autobús leí las noticias en el celular.
english: While I was waiting for the bus I read the news on my phone.
grammar: "Mientras esperaba... leí" — imperfect (esperaba) for the background/ongoing action, preterite (leí) for the interrupting completed action. Classic combo.

target: Mientras cocino suelo escuchar un pódcast.
english: While I cook I usually listen to a podcast.

target: Solía acostarme muy tarde, pero ahora intento dormirme antes de las once.
english: I used to go to bed very late, but now I try to fall asleep before eleven.
grammar: "Solía" is the imperfect of "soler" — used for past habits that no longer continue. "I used to go to bed late (but not anymore)."

target: Mis padres se divorciaron cuando yo tenía doce años.
english: My parents got divorced when I was twelve.

target: Mi hermana y yo nos peleábamos mucho de pequeñas, pero ahora somos inseparables.
english: My sister and I used to fight a lot as kids, but now we're inseparable.

target: Mi padre siempre dice que me parezco a él cuando tenía mi edad.
english: My father always says I look like him when he was my age.

target: El abuelo tiene problemas de memoria, pero todavía reconoce a todos.
english: Grandpa has memory problems, but he still recognizes everyone.

target: Mi madre me enseñó a cocinar cuando yo tenía ocho años.
english: My mother taught me to cook when I was eight years old.

target: Mi padre me llevaba al parque en bicicleta cuando era pequeño.
english: My father used to take me to the park on his bicycle when I was little.
grammar: possessives agree with the thing owned: nuestro perro (masc), nuestra casa (fem), nuestros hijos (masc pl).

target: Mi abuelo era muy estricto pero también muy justo con todos.
english: My grandfather was very strict but also very fair with everyone.

target: Mi hermana mayor siempre me protegía de los niños del barrio cuando era pequeño.
english: My older sister always protected me from the neighborhood kids when I was little.

target: Estoy haciendo un curso en línea de gestión de proyectos mientras trabajo.
english: I'm taking an online project management course while working.

target: Me quemé la mano con la estufa mientras cocinaba.
english: I burned my hand on the stove while cooking.

target: No concibo que en pleno siglo veintiuno todavía haya quienes justifiquen la violencia.
english: I can't conceive that in the twenty-first century there are still those who justify violence.

target: Cuando era pequeño vivíamos en una casa junto al río.
english: When I was little we lived in a house by the river.

target: Mientras caminaba por el parque me encontré con un antiguo compañero de clase.
english: While I was walking through the park I ran into an old classmate.

target: Mientras yo estudiaba para el examen, mis compañeros se fueron de fiesta.
english: While I was studying for the exam, my classmates went out partying.

target: Aprendí a esquiar cuando tenía diez años en los Pirineos.
english: I learned to ski when I was ten years old in the Pyrenees.

target: Me probé unos pantalones grises pero me quedaban demasiado grandes.
english: I tried on some gray pants but they were too big for me.

target: El agua está fría hoy, pero ayer estaba caliente.
english: The water is cold today, but yesterday it was hot.

target: ¿Estás listo para salir o todavía no estás preparado?
english: Are you ready to leave or are you still not prepared?

target: Cuando era niño, mi padre me llevaba al parque todos los sábados.
english: When I was a child, my father would take me to the park every Saturday.

target: Cuando era joven, vivía en el campo.
english: When I was young, I lived in the countryside.
grammar: Imperfect tense — describing habitual past states.

target: Cuesta creer que todavía haya tanta desigualdad en el mundo.
english: It's hard to believe there's still so much inequality in the world.

target: Mi abuelo todavía trabaja.
english: My grandfather still works.

target: Cuando era niño, vivía en el campo.
english: When I was a kid, I lived in the countryside.
grammar: Imperfect for habitual past: "Jugaba todos los días" (I used to play every day). Use imperfect for repeated past actions, routines, and "used to."

target: Mientras ella cocinaba, yo ponía la mesa.
english: While she cooked, I set the table.
grammar: Imperfect for simultaneous past actions: "Mientras yo cocinaba, él leía." Two ongoing actions happening at the same time → both in imperfect.

target: Cuando vivía en Madrid, comía mucho fuera.
english: When I lived in Madrid, I ate out a lot.

target: Antes ganaba menos pero estaba más contento.
english: Before, I earned less but I was happier.

target: Cuando era estudiante, dormía poco.
english: When I was a student, I slept very little.

target: Mientras esperaba, leía un libro.
english: While I waited, I read a book.

target: Hacía mucho calor aquel día.
english: It was very hot that day.

target: Yo tenía unos diez años cuando nos mudamos.
english: I was about ten when we moved.

target: Ella trabajaba mientras estudiaba.
english: She worked while she studied.

target: Si hubiéramos empezado antes, ya habríamos terminado.
english: If we had started earlier, we would have already finished.

target: Mientras tanto, puedes ir preparando la presentación.
english: In the meantime, you can start preparing the presentation.

target: Cuando era joven, trabajaba en una fábrica.
english: When I was young, I used to work in a factory.
grammar: Ser (imperfect): era, eras, era, éramos — one of three irregular verbs

target: Mi abuelo contaba historias de cuando era marinero.
english: My grandfather used to tell stories from when he was a sailor.

target: Mis padres trabajaban mucho cuando éramos pequeños.
english: My parents worked a lot when we were little.

target: Ella estudiaba mientras yo veía la tele.
english: She was studying while I was watching TV.

target: Ella cantaba mientras limpiaba la casa.
english: She used to sing while she cleaned the house.

target: Ella tenía el pelo largo cuando era joven.
english: She had long hair when she was young.

target: Mi padre escuchaba la radio mientras desayunaba.
english: My father used to listen to the radio while he had breakfast.

target: Ella trabajaba como camarera mientras estudiaba.
english: She used to work as a waitress while she was studying.

target: Él se cortó el dedo mientras cocinaba.
english: He cut his finger while cooking.

target: Cuando era niño, viajábamos a la playa cada verano.
english: When I was a child, we would travel to the beach every summer.

target: Mientras esperaba el avión, leía un libro.
english: While I waited for the plane, I read a book.

target: Mientras cruzábamos la frontera, llovía mucho.
english: While we crossed the border, it rained a lot.

target: Cuando empecé, no sabía usar la impresora.
english: When I started, I didn't know how to use the printer.

target: Mientras redactaba el contrato, sonó el teléfono.
english: While I was drafting the contract, the phone rang.

target: Yo ganaba menos cuando empecé.
english: I earned less when I started.

target: La empresa pagaba bien pero exigía mucho.
english: The company paid well but demanded a lot.

target: Mientras preparaba la presentación, mi colega me ayudaba.
english: While I prepared the presentation, my colleague helped me.

target: Cuando era pequeño, mi madre me leía cuentos.
english: When I was little, my mother read me stories.

target: Mientras mi madre cocinaba, yo ponía la mesa.
english: While my mother cooked, I set the table.

target: Nos cuidaba la vecina cuando éramos niños.
english: The neighbor looked after us when we were kids.

target: La habitación no estaba lista cuando llegamos.
english: The room wasn't ready when we arrived.

target: Cuando era joven, quería ser médico.
english: When I was young, I wanted to be a doctor.

target: Mientras hablaba por teléfono, llegó el jefe.
english: While I was talking on the phone, the boss arrived.
grammar: Imperfecto (hablaba) for ongoing action + pretérito (llegó) for interruption.

target: Cuando empecé, no sabía usar el sistema.
english: When I started, I didn't know how to use the system.

target: No tenía experiencia cuando empecé.
english: I had no experience when I started.

target: Mientras los niños no molesten, pueden quedarse en el salón.
english: As long as the kids don't bother anyone, they can stay in the living room.

target: Mientras llovía, llegamos al hotel.
english: While it was raining, we arrived at the hotel.
grammar: Imperfect (llovía) for background weather + preterite (llegamos) for completed action.

target: Cuando era niño, vivía cerca del mar.
english: When I was a child, I lived near the sea.
grammar: Both imperfect: era + vivía. Habitual past — describes how things used to be.

target: Estudiaba en la biblioteca cuando sonó mi teléfono.
english: I was studying in the library when my phone rang.
grammar: Imperfect (estudiaba) for ongoing action + preterite (sonó) for sudden interruption.

target: De repente se apagaron las luces y todos gritaron.
english: Suddenly the lights went out and everyone screamed.
grammar: "De repente" triggers preterite: both actions (apagaron, gritaron) are sudden, completed events.

target: Mientras caminábamos por la playa, encontramos unas conchas.
english: While we were walking on the beach, we found some shells.
grammar: Imperfect (caminábamos) for background + preterite (encontramos) for the specific event.

target: Ya sabía la noticia cuando me llamaste.
english: I already knew the news when you called me.
grammar: "Ya" + imperfect (sabía) for prior state + preterite (llamaste) for the moment of calling.

target: Todavía dormía cuando empezó a llover.
english: I was still sleeping when it started to rain.
grammar: "Todavía" + imperfect (dormía) for ongoing state + preterite (empezó) for interruption.

target: Cuando vivíamos en el campo, teníamos un huerto grande.
english: When we lived in the countryside, we had a big garden.
grammar: Both imperfect: vivíamos + teníamos. Describes a habitual past situation, not a single event.

target: Mientras mi madre cocinaba, mi padre puso la mesa.
english: While my mother was cooking, my father set the table.
grammar: Imperfect (cocinaba) for ongoing background + preterite (puso) for completed action.

target: Hacía mucho sol cuando salimos de excursión.
english: It was very sunny when we went on the excursion.
grammar: Imperfect (hacía) for weather backdrop + preterite (salimos) for the specific departure.

target: Cuando tenía veinte años, viajé a México por primera vez.
english: When I was twenty, I traveled to Mexico for the first time.
grammar: Imperfect (tenía) for age/background + preterite (viajé) for the one-time event.

target: Mientras esperaba el autobús, vi a un viejo amigo.
english: While I was waiting for the bus, I saw an old friend.
grammar: Imperfect (esperaba) for ongoing wait + preterite (vi) for the sudden encounter.

target: De repente el perro salió corriendo y cruzó la calle.
english: Suddenly the dog ran out and crossed the street.
grammar: "De repente" + two preterites (salió, cruzó) — both sudden completed actions.

target: Mientras leía el periódico, alguien tocó la puerta.
english: While I was reading the newspaper, someone knocked on the door.
grammar: Imperfect (leía) for ongoing activity + preterite (tocó) for the interruption.

target: Ya estaba dormido cuando llegó mi esposa.
english: I was already asleep when my wife arrived.
grammar: "Ya" + imperfect (estaba) for existing state + preterite (llegó) for arrival.

target: Todos los veranos íbamos a la casa de mis abuelos.
english: Every summer we used to go to my grandparents' house.
grammar: Imperfect (íbamos) for repeated habitual action. "Todos los veranos" signals repetition.

target: Mientras hablaba por teléfono, el gato se subió a la mesa.
english: While I was talking on the phone, the cat jumped on the table.
grammar: Imperfect (hablaba) for background action + preterite (se subió) for the sudden event.

target: ¿Cómo te llamas?
english: What's your name?
grammar: Reflexive verb: llamarse (to call oneself). "Te llamas" = you call yourself. Literally: "How do you call yourself?"

target: Me llamo Ana.
english: My name is Ana.
grammar: "Me llamo" = I call myself. The reflexive pronoun "me" refers back to the subject (yo). This is how Spanish states names.

target: Me despierto temprano todos los días.
english: I wake up early every day.
grammar: "Despertarse" is reflexive — the action is done to oneself. Reflexive verbs use me/te/se/nos/se before the conjugated verb: me despierto, te despiertas, se despierta.

target: Me levanto a las siete de la mañana.
english: I get up at seven in the morning.
grammar: Time uses "a las" + number: "a las siete" = at seven. For 1:00 use "a la una" (singular). "De la mañana" specifies AM.

target: Me lavo la cara con agua fría.
english: I wash my face with cold water.
grammar: With reflexive verbs and body parts, Spanish uses the definite article ("la cara"), NOT a possessive ("mi cara"). The reflexive "me" already tells you whose face.

target: Me visto rápido para salir.
english: I get dressed quickly to go out.
grammar: "Vestirse" is an e→i stem-changing reflexive verb: me visto, te vistes, se viste. The stem only changes when stressed.

target: Por la mañana escucho música mientras me preparo.
english: In the morning I listen to music while I get ready.

target: Me pongo el abrigo porque hace frío fuera.
english: I put on my coat because it's cold outside.
grammar: "Ponerse" means "to put on" (clothing). It's reflexive + irregular yo: me pongo. "Porque hace frío" — weather uses "hacer": hace frío/calor/sol/viento.

target: A veces me quedo dormido en el sofá.
english: Sometimes I fall asleep on the sofa.
grammar: "Quedarse dormido" = to fall asleep (accidentally). "Quedarse" + adjective means "to end up/become": quedarse solo, quedarse callado.

target: Me afeito cada dos días porque mi piel es sensible.
english: I shave every other day because my skin is sensitive.

target: El sol se pone por el oeste.
english: The sun sets in the west.

target: Mi hermano mayor vive en otra ciudad.
english: My older brother lives in another city.
grammar: "llamarse" (reflexive) = to be called. Literally: "My mother calls herself Ana." All names use this construction.

target: Mi suegra nos invita a comer todos los domingos.
english: My mother-in-law invites us to eat every Sunday.
grammar: "mayor" (older/bigger) and "menor" (younger/smaller) are irregular comparatives. No "más": NOT *"más mayor."

target: Mi hijo adolescente pasa mucho tiempo con sus amigos.
english: My teenage son spends a lot of time with his friends.
grammar: "reunirse" is reflexive. "Toda la familia" — "todo" agrees: todo el día, toda la noche, todos los días.

target: Mi familia tiene la tradición de cenar junta todos los viernes por la noche.
english: My family has the tradition of having dinner together every Friday night.
grammar: "mudarse" (reflexive) = to move (house). "Más grande" = bigger — comparatives use "más" + adjective.

target: El vecino de abajo se queja del ruido que hacemos.
english: The downstairs neighbor complains about the noise we make.

target: Intento no gastar más del cuarenta por ciento de mi sueldo en el alquiler.
english: I try not to spend more than forty percent of my salary on rent.
grammar: "romperse" reflexive for body injuries. "Jugando" — gerund explains how/when. "Al fútbol" — sports use "a + el."

target: Hoy hace mucho sol.
english: It's very sunny today.
grammar: "sentirse" (e→ie, reflexive) for emotions/physical states. "Me siento" = I feel. Uses estar logic (temporary state).

target: Estoy nervioso por el examen de mañana.
english: I am nervous about tomorrow's exam.
grammar: "tener fiebre/tos/gripe" — illness uses "tener" (not estar). "Sentirse mal" = to feel bad (reflexive).

target: Me preocupa que llueva durante la fiesta.
english: I'm worried that it will rain during the party.

target: Me siento frustrado porque nada sale como quiero.
english: I feel frustrated because nothing goes the way I want.

target: Me pone nervioso hablar en público delante de mucha gente.
english: It makes me nervous to speak in public in front of a lot of people.

target: Ella se siente sola desde que sus hijos se mudaron.
english: She feels lonely since her children moved away.

target: Se me olvidó comprar pan.
english: I forgot to buy bread.
grammar: "Se me olvidó" — accidental/unintentional construction with "se".

target: Perderse en una ciudad desconocida puede ser la mejor aventura.
english: Getting lost in an unknown city can be the best adventure.

target: Te llamo por teléfono.
english: I'll call you by phone.
grammar: "Por" = via: "Lo dije por teléfono." Por for the method of communication.

target: Me preocupo por mi familia.
english: I worry about my family.
grammar: "Por" = about / concerning: "Preguntó por ti." Por when asking or worrying about someone.

target: Me cae bien tu amigo.
english: I like your friend.
grammar: Caer bien/mal for people

target: Me queda grande esta camiseta.
english: This t-shirt is too big for me.
grammar: "Quedar" for fit/appearance: "Te queda bien" = It looks good on you / It fits you well. Uses indirect object like gustar.

target: Me cae mal mi compañero de piso.
english: I don't like my roommate.
grammar: "Caer mal" = to not like someone (as a person). "Me cae mal" = I don't like him. Uses gustar pattern — the person is the subject.

target: Me despierto sin alarma.
english: I wake up without an alarm.
grammar: "Despertarse" = to wake (oneself) up. The reflexive shows you do it to yourself: "Me despierto a las 7."

target: Nos vamos a las ocho.
english: We're leaving at eight.
grammar: "Irse" = to leave/go away (different from "ir" = to go). The reflexive changes the meaning: "Me voy" = I'm leaving.

target: Nos reunimos cada lunes.
english: We meet every Monday.
grammar: "Reunirse" = to meet/gather. Reciprocal: "Nos reunimos los lunes" = We meet on Mondays.

target: Me quedo en casa esta noche.
english: I'm staying home tonight.
grammar: "Quedarse" = to stay/remain. Different from "quedar" (to arrange to meet). "Me quedé en casa" = I stayed home.

target: Él se concentra mejor con música.
english: He concentrates better with music.

target: Me caí en la calle y me hice daño.
english: I fell in the street and hurt myself.
grammar: "Caerse" = to fall down (accidentally). Reflexive adds accidental nuance: "Me caí" = I fell (it happened to me).

target: Él se equivocó de puerta.
english: He went to the wrong door.
grammar: "Equivocarse" = to make a mistake / be wrong. "Me equivoqué" = I was wrong. Always reflexive.

target: Ellos se ayudan mutuamente.
english: They help each other.
grammar: Reciprocal "se": plural reflexive pronouns (nos, os, se) can mean "each other." "Se ayudan" = They help each other.

target: Él se dedica a la fotografía.
english: He works in photography.
grammar: "Dedicarse a" = to work as / dedicate oneself to. "¿A qué te dedicas?" = What do you do (for work)?

target: Nos echamos de menos.
english: We miss each other.
grammar: "Echarse de menos" = to miss each other. "Nos echamos de menos" = We miss each other.

target: El profesor que nos enseña historia es muy divertido.
english: The teacher who teaches us history is very fun.

target: Nos llevamos de maravilla desde el primer día.
english: We've gotten along wonderfully since day one.

target: No me queda más remedio que aceptar la oferta.
english: I have no choice but to accept the offer.

target: Si por mí fuera, nos iríamos de viaje mañana.
english: If it were up to me, we'd go on a trip tomorrow.

target: Eso me suena a chino, explícamelo de otra forma.
english: That sounds like Greek to me, explain it another way.
grammar: Sonar a chino — to be incomprehensible (like Greek in English)

target: No me cae bien el nuevo director.
english: I don't like the new director.
grammar: Caer bien/mal: me cae bien/mal — I like/dislike someone (personality)

target: Me despierto a las seis y media todos los días.
english: I wake up at six thirty every day.
grammar: Despertarse (reflexive, e→ie): me despierto, te despiertas, se despierta

target: Ella se maquilla en el carro camino al trabajo.
english: She puts on makeup in the car on the way to work.

target: Ella se quita los zapatos al llegar a casa.
english: She takes off her shoes when she gets home.

target: Se nos olvidó cerrar la puerta con llave.
english: We forgot to lock the door.
grammar: Olvidársele — unplanned event: se nos olvidó — we forgot (accidentally)

target: El jefe nos daba poco tiempo para los plazos.
english: The boss gave us little time for deadlines.

target: Me preparo para la excursión del día.
english: I get ready for the day's excursion.

target: Me preparo para la presentación de mañana.
english: I get ready for tomorrow's presentation.

target: Me concentro mejor por las mañanas.
english: I concentrate better in the mornings.

target: Nos acostamos temprano entre semana.
english: We go to bed early on weekdays.

target: Mi hija se enfada cuando no puede salir.
english: My daughter gets angry when she can't go out.

target: Mi padre se relaja viendo la televisión.
english: My father relaxes watching TV.

target: Los abuelos se acuestan a las diez.
english: The grandparents go to bed at ten.

target: Nos quedamos en un hostal muy barato.
english: We stayed at a very cheap hostel.
grammar: "Quedarse" is reflexive: nos quedamos (we stayed). Without "se," quedar means "to remain" or "to meet up."

target: Me bajé en la parada equivocada.
english: I got off at the wrong stop.
grammar: "Bajarse" is reflexive: me bajé (I got off). "Subirse" = to get on, "bajarse" = to get off.

target: Me levanté temprano para ver el amanecer.
english: I got up early to see the sunrise.

target: Se me cayó el teléfono en la piscina.
english: I dropped my phone in the pool.
grammar: "Se me cayó" — accidental reflexive construction. Literally: "it fell itself on me." Used when something happens unintentionally.

target: Se me olvidó el protector solar.
english: I forgot the sunscreen.
grammar: "Se me olvidó" — another accidental reflexive. "It forgot itself on me." Common way to say you forgot something unintentionally.

target: Nos levantamos a las seis para tomar el autobús.
english: We got up at six to catch the bus.

target: Me quedé dormido en el autobús.
english: I fell asleep on the bus.
grammar: "Dormirse" = to fall asleep (unintentionally). "Se me durmió la pierna" = My leg fell asleep. The reflexive adds the accidental nuance.

target: Me compré un sombrero para el sol.
english: I bought myself a sun hat.
grammar: "Me compré" — the reflexive here adds emphasis: I bought FOR MYSELF.

target: Me alegra que hayamos llegado a tiempo.
english: I'm glad we arrived on time.

target: Me preparé para la presentación.
english: I prepared for the presentation.
grammar: Reflexive past: me preparé (I prepared myself).

target: Nos mudamos a una oficina nueva.
english: We moved to a new office.
grammar: Reflexive mudarse = to move (locations). Nos mudamos = we moved.

target: Se me olvidó enviar el correo.
english: I forgot to send the email.
grammar: Se me olvidó: accidental se construction — 'it slipped my mind'.

target: El compañero nuevo se presentó al equipo.
english: The new colleague introduced himself to the team.
grammar: Reflexive presentarse = to introduce oneself.

target: Los niños se acostaron temprano.
english: The kids went to bed early.
grammar: Acostarse = reflexive verb: se acostaron (they went to bed)

target: Los niños se vistieron solos esta mañana.
english: The kids dressed themselves this morning.
grammar: Vestirse = to get dressed (reflexive): se vistieron

target: Se me olvidó recoger a los niños.
english: I forgot to pick up the kids.
grammar: Se me olvidó = it slipped my mind (accidental se construction)

target: Mi primo se rompió el brazo jugando.
english: My cousin broke his arm playing.
grammar: Romperse = reflexive for body parts: se rompió el brazo

target: Mi hermano me despertó muy temprano.
english: My brother woke me up very early.

target: Mi madre me aconseja que ahorre más.
english: My mother advises me to save more.

target: Mi hija se viste sola desde los cuatro años.
english: My daughter has been dressing herself since she was four.
grammar: Vestirse is reflexive — the person dresses themselves.

target: Me siento muy orgullosa de mis hijos.
english: I feel very proud of my children.

target: El nuevo proyecto es coser y cantar si nos organizamos bien.
english: The new project is a breeze if we organize well.
grammar: Ser coser y cantar = to be very easy / a piece of cake.

target: Creo que es por aquí.
english: I think it's this way.

target: Por la tarde tengo una reunión importante.
english: In the afternoon I have an important meeting.

target: Por las tardes suelo pasear al perro por el barrio.
english: In the evenings I usually walk the dog around the neighborhood.
grammar: "Soler" + infinitive = to usually do something. Present: suelo, sueles, suele, solemos, suelen. It's an o→ue stem-changer itself.

target: Los domingos por la mañana preparo tortitas para todos.
english: On Sunday mornings I make pancakes for everyone.

target: El aceite de oliva es muy bueno para la salud.
english: Olive oil is very good for your health.
grammar: "-ísimo/a" = absolute superlative (extremely): riquísimo (delicious), buenísimo (great). "Está" (not "es") because you're describing how it tastes right now.

target: Es importante tener un fondo de emergencia para gastos imprevistos.
english: It's important to have an emergency fund for unexpected expenses.

target: El puesto requiere disponibilidad para viajar y dominio del inglés.
english: The position requires availability to travel and fluency in English.

target: Gracias por tu ayuda.
english: Thanks for your help.
grammar: "Por" = because of: "Llegué tarde por el tráfico." Expresses the cause behind something.

target: Este regalo es para ti.
english: This gift is for you.
grammar: "Para" = for (recipient): "Es para ti." Para marks who receives something.

target: Trabajo por la mañana.
english: I work in the morning.
grammar: "Por" for approximate time: "por la mañana" (in the morning). General time of day, not a specific hour.

target: Lo cambié por otro.
english: I exchanged it for another.
grammar: "Por" = in exchange for: "Lo compré por diez euros." Por marks what you give to get something.

target: Estudio para aprender.
english: I study in order to learn.
grammar: "Para" = in order to: "Estudio para aprender." Para expresses the goal or purpose.

target: Pagué veinte euros por el libro.
english: I paid twenty euros for the book.
grammar: "Por" = in exchange for: "Lo compré por diez euros." Por for what you pay or trade.

target: Hago ejercicio por salud.
english: I exercise for health.
grammar: "Por" = because of / motivated by: "Lo hice por amor." Por for the underlying cause or motivation.

target: Estudio español para viajar por Latinoamérica.
english: I study Spanish to travel through Latin America.
grammar: "Para" = in order to (purpose). "Por" = through (place/means). Two different ideas.

target: Pagué veinte euros por la camiseta.
english: I paid twenty euros for the shirt.
grammar: "Por" = in exchange for: "Lo compré por diez euros." Por marks what you give to get something.

target: Vengo por la mañana.
english: I come in the morning.
grammar: "Por" for approximate time: "por la mañana" (in the morning), "por la noche" (at night). General time of day.

target: Por lo menos tenemos el fin de semana.
english: At least we have the weekend.

target: Fui al médico por el dolor de espalda.
english: I went to the doctor because of the back pain.
grammar: "Por" = because of / due to. Expresses the cause or motive behind something: "Llegué tarde por el tráfico."

target: Te cambio mi turno por el tuyo.
english: I'll trade you my shift for yours.
grammar: "Por" = in exchange for: "Cambié mi coche por uno nuevo." Por for swapping one thing for another.

target: Me quedé en casa por la lluvia.
english: I stayed home because of the rain.
grammar: "Por" = because of. Expresses what caused something: "Lo hice por ti" (I did it because of you).

target: No puedo ir por el trabajo.
english: I can't go because of work.
grammar: "Por" = because of. Expresses the cause or obstacle: "No salimos por la lluvia" (We didn't go out because of the rain).

target: Paseamos por el centro de la ciudad.
english: We strolled through the city center.

target: Ella vende ropa por internet.
english: She sells clothes online.

target: Trabajo por las mañanas y estudio por las tardes.
english: I work in the mornings and study in the afternoons.
grammar: Por + time period = during: por la mañana, por la tarde, por la noche

target: Gracias por ayudarme con la mudanza.
english: Thanks for helping me with the move.
grammar: Por after gracias = thanking for a reason

target: Ella estudia para ser abogada.
english: She's studying to be a lawyer.
grammar: Para + ser + profession = purpose/goal

target: Este software es para gestionar proyectos.
english: This software is for managing projects.
grammar: Para + infinitive = purpose/function

target: Cambié mi turno por el de la mañana.
english: I swapped my shift for the morning one.
grammar: Por = exchange/substitution: cambiar X por Y

target: Pagué trescientos euros por la reparación.
english: I paid three hundred euros for the repair.
grammar: Por = in exchange for a price

target: La reunión se canceló por falta de asistentes.
english: The meeting was canceled due to lack of attendees.
grammar: Por = because of, due to (cause)

target: Por lo general, termino de trabajar a las cinco.
english: Generally, I finish work at five.
grammar: "Por lo general" = generally / usually. Expresses what's typical: "Por lo general, como a las 2."

target: Por aquí hay muchos restaurantes buenos.
english: Around here there are many good restaurants.
grammar: "Por aquí" = around here / this way. "Por" for approximate location or direction.

target: Firme aquí abajo, por favor.
english: Sign down here, please.

target: Los niños corren por el parque.
english: The kids run through the park.

target: Pase por aquí, por favor.
english: Come this way, please.

target: El vuelo sale por la mañana.
english: The flight leaves in the morning.

target: Gano mil euros por mes.
english: I earn one thousand euros per month.

target: Quiero la carta, por favor.
english: I want the menu, please.
grammar: "La carta" is the menu in a restaurant. "El menú" often refers to a fixed-price meal (menú del día).

target: Reservé la habitación para tres noches.
english: I booked the room for three nights.
grammar: "Para" indicates purpose/destination. "For three nights" = the intended duration of the stay.

target: Tomamos el metro para ir al centro.
english: We took the metro to go downtown.
grammar: "Para ir" — "para" + infinitive expresses purpose: in order to go.

target: Alquilamos un carro por una semana.
english: We rented a car for a week.
grammar: "Por una semana" — "por" for duration of time. How long the rental lasted.

target: Compré regalos para toda la familia.
english: I bought gifts for the whole family.
grammar: "Para" indicates the recipient/beneficiary: gifts FOR the family.

target: Pagué treinta euros por la excursión.
english: I paid thirty euros for the excursion.
grammar: "Por" for exchange: money IN EXCHANGE FOR a service.

target: Cambié euros por pesos en el aeropuerto.
english: I exchanged euros for pesos at the airport.
grammar: "Por" for exchange: swapping one currency for another.

target: Habiendo viajado por toda Europa, puedo decir que España es única.
english: Having traveled all over Europe, I can say that Spain is unique.
grammar: the compound gerund, used to express a completed action before the main clause.

target: Compré un regalo para mi madre.
english: I bought a gift for my mother.
grammar: Para = for (intended recipient)

target: Compré estas flores para mi madre.
english: I bought these flowers for my mother.
grammar: Para + recipient = intended for someone.

target: Estudio español para poder comunicarme mejor.
english: I study Spanish to be able to communicate better.
grammar: Para + infinitive = in order to (purpose).

target: Suele llegar tarde los lunes por el trafico.
english: He usually arrives late on Mondays because of traffic.
grammar: Soler + infinitive = to usually do something (habitual).

target: ¿Me puedes ayudar?
english: Can you help me?
grammar: "Me" is the indirect object pronoun (to me). It can go before the conjugated verb: "me puedes ayudar" = "puedes ayudarme." Both are correct.

target: Camino hasta la parada del autobús.
english: I walk to the bus stop.

target: Enciendo la computadora y reviso mis correos.
english: I turn on the computer and check my emails.

target: Después de cenar veo un poco de televisión.
english: After dinner I watch some television.

target: Los sábados limpio la casa.
english: On Saturdays I clean the house.

target: Cierro la puerta con llave antes de irme.
english: I lock the door before leaving.
grammar: "Cerrar" is an e→ie stem-changer: cierro, cierras, cierra, cerramos, cierran. Common pattern shared by pensar, empezar, entender, querer.

target: Riego las plantas del balcón cada dos días.
english: I water the balcony plants every other day.

target: Es la tercera vez que me lo preguntas.
english: It's the third time you've asked me.

target: La tienda abre a las diez en punto.
english: The store opens at ten o'clock.

target: La primavera empieza en marzo.
english: Spring starts in March.

target: El apartamento mide sesenta y cinco metros cuadrados.
english: The apartment is sixty-five square meters.

target: La playa está al este de la ciudad.
english: The beach is east of the city.

target: El reloj marca las tres y diez de la tarde.
english: The clock reads three ten in the afternoon.
grammar: "Doble" (double) and "triple" (triple) are invariable before nouns: el doble de personas, el triple de cantidad.

target: La receta necesita trescientos gramos de harina.
english: The recipe needs three hundred grams of flour.

target: La torre tiene una altura de trescientos veinticuatro metros.
english: The tower has a height of three hundred and twenty-four meters.

target: Mi sobrina empieza la escuela el próximo septiembre.
english: My niece starts school next September.

target: Nos reunimos toda la familia en Navidad.
english: The whole family gets together at Christmas.

target: Mi marido y yo compartimos las tareas de la casa.
english: My husband and I share the household chores.

target: Mi mujer trabaja desde casa tres días a la semana.
english: My wife works from home three days a week.

target: La sala es la habitación más grande de la casa.
english: The living room is the biggest room in the house.

target: La cocina tiene una ventana que da al jardín.
english: The kitchen has a window that faces the garden.

target: El suelo de la cocina es de baldosas blancas.
english: The kitchen floor is made of white tiles.

target: Las llaves están colgadas en el gancho de la entrada.
english: The keys are hanging on the hook by the entrance.

target: El pan está fresco, lo compré esta mañana.
english: The bread is fresh, I bought it this morning.
grammar: "para mí" (for me) when ordering. Note the accent on "mí" (me) vs "mi" (my). Prepositional pronouns: para mí, para ti, para él/ella.

target: La fruta de temporada está más barata y más rica.
english: Seasonal fruit is cheaper and tastier.

target: Hay que dejar la masa reposar al menos una hora.
english: You need to let the dough rest for at least an hour.

target: Precalienta el horno a ciento ochenta grados.
english: Preheat the oven to one hundred and eighty degrees.

target: Perdona, ¿sabes dónde está la parada más cercana?
english: Excuse me, do you know where the nearest stop is?

target: El carril bici va por toda la avenida principal.
english: The bike lane runs along the whole main avenue.

target: Ponte el cinturón de seguridad antes de arrancar.
english: Put on your seatbelt before starting.

target: En la rotonda toma la segunda salida en dirección norte.
english: At the roundabout take the second exit heading north.

target: La estación de autobuses está justo enfrente de la plaza.
english: The bus station is right across from the square.

target: Pago las compras del supermercado en efectivo.
english: I pay for the supermarket shopping in cash.

target: Estoy ahorrando para dar la entrada de un departamento.
english: I'm saving up for a down payment on an apartment.

target: Las montañas están cubiertas de nieve hasta la primavera.
english: The mountains are covered in snow until spring.

target: La selva tropical tiene la mayor biodiversidad del planeta.
english: The tropical rainforest has the greatest biodiversity on the planet.

target: El corazón late más rápido cuando haces ejercicio.
english: The heart beats faster when you exercise.

target: Ella tiene alergia al polen y le lloran los ojos.
english: She is allergic to pollen and her eyes water.

target: El paciente necesita una operación de emergencia.
english: The patient needs an emergency operation.

target: No soporto que la gente hable durante la película.
english: I can't stand it when people talk during the movie.

target: Están planeando abrir un restaurante en el centro.
english: They are planning to open a restaurant downtown.

target: Nos proponemos reducir nuestro consumo de plástico.
english: We intend to reduce our plastic consumption.

target: La empresa tiene previsto contratar a cincuenta empleados más.
english: The company plans to hire fifty more employees.

target: Habremos terminado la construcción antes del invierno.
english: We will have finished the construction before winter.

target: Quedamos en vernos a las cinco, ¿no?
english: We agreed to meet at five, right?

target: El exceso de información puede dificultar la toma de decisiones.
english: The excess of information can make decision-making difficult.

target: El sistema sanitario público necesita una reforma urgente.
english: The public healthcare system needs urgent reform.

target: La corrupción política erosiona la confianza de los ciudadanos.
english: Political corruption erodes citizens' trust.

target: La participación ciudadana es esencial para fortalecer la democracia.
english: Citizen participation is essential for strengthening democracy.

target: Los medios independientes son fundamentales para la transparencia pública.
english: Independent media are essential for public transparency.

target: La convocatoria de empleo público exige un título universitario.
english: The public employment announcement requires a university degree.

target: La escalada en roca requiere fuerza, técnica y concentración.
english: Rock climbing requires strength, technique, and concentration.

target: El perro de mi vecino ladra toda la noche.
english: My neighbor's dog barks all night.

target: Dámelo ahora, por favor; te lo pido como un favor.
english: Give it to me now, please — I'm asking you as a favor.

target: ¿Se la has enviado ya? Lleva esperándola toda la semana.
english: Have you sent it to her already? She's been waiting for it all week.
grammar: formal usted: "dígame" (command) + "puedo ayudarle" (le = usted). Indirect question "en qué" = in what.

target: Ustedes son bienvenidos a nuestra casa cuando lo deseen.
english: You are welcome at our home whenever you wish.

target: El check-in es a las tres de la tarde.
english: Check-in is at three in the afternoon.

target: La casa de mis padres tiene un jardín grande.
english: My parents' house has a big garden.

target: Te lo devuelvo mañana.
english: I'll return it to you tomorrow.
grammar: Double object pronouns: "te lo" — to you + it.

target: Dale recuerdos a tu madre de mi parte.
english: Give my regards to your mother.

target: El diálogo abierto fortalece los lazos familiares.
english: Open dialogue strengthens family bonds.

target: El tiempo pone todo en su lugar.
english: Time puts everything in its place.

target: A mi parecer, la vida es demasiado corta para preocuparnos.
english: In my view, life is too short to worry.

target: Un currículum bien escrito abre muchas puertas.
english: A well-written resume opens many doors.

target: Probar la comida callejera es parte de la experiencia.
english: Trying street food is part of the experience.

target: Lo hice por ti.
english: I did it for you (because of you).
grammar: "Por" = on behalf of / because of: "Habló por todos nosotros." For speaking for someone or acting because of them.

target: Te lo dije ayer.
english: I told you yesterday.
grammar: Double object pronouns: te (you) + lo (it).

target: Se lo di a María.
english: I gave it to María.
grammar: When both pronouns start with l-, le becomes se.

target: Estoy haciéndolo ahora.
english: I'm doing it now.
grammar: Pronouns attach to the end of gerunds.

target: Me gusta la música.
english: I like music.
grammar: "Me" is indirect object — music pleases me.

target: La ciudad donde nací ya no es la misma.
english: The city where I was born is no longer the same.

target: Si no lloviera, iríamos a la playa.
english: If it weren't raining, we'd go to the beach.

target: Mi hermano menor mide un metro ochenta.
english: My younger brother is one meter eighty tall.

target: El museo abre de martes a domingo.
english: The museum is open Tuesday through Sunday.

target: Los vecinos de arriba hacen mucho ruido.
english: The upstairs neighbors make a lot of noise.

target: Le pasa algo, está muy callada.
english: Something's wrong with her, she's very quiet.
grammar: "Pasar" for what's happening: "¿Qué te pasa?" = What's wrong with you? / What's happening to you? Indirect object marks the affected person.

target: Se lo dije ayer.
english: I told him yesterday.
grammar: Double object pronouns: indirect (le/les) + direct (lo/la) → "se lo." Le becomes se before lo/la to avoid "le lo." "Se lo dije" = I told it to him.

target: Te lo explico ahora.
english: I'll explain it to you now.
grammar: Double pronouns: indirect + direct. "Te lo dije" = I told it to you. Indirect pronoun (te) goes first, then direct (lo).

target: ¿Les mandaste el correo?
english: Did you send them the email?
grammar: "Les" = to them (indirect object): "Les dije la verdad" = I told them the truth. Plural of le.

target: No me lo puedo creer.
english: I can't believe it.
grammar: Double pronouns with modals: "Me lo puede dar" or "Puede dármelo." Pronouns go before conjugated verb OR attached to infinitive.

target: Se lo prometí a mi hijo.
english: I promised it to my son.
grammar: When le/les comes before lo/la/los/las, le becomes "se" to avoid cacophony: "Le lo di" → "Se lo di."

target: Se las envié esta mañana.
english: I sent them to her this morning.
grammar: "Se las" = le + las → se replaces le before direct object. "Se las di" = I gave them (f.) to him/her.

target: Le compré un regalo a mi madre.
english: I bought my mother a gift.
grammar: "Le" = to him/her/you(formal). Often clarified with "a + person": "Le dije a mi madre" = I told my mother.

target: Me la presentó en una fiesta.
english: He introduced her to me at a party.
grammar: Double pronouns: "Me la trajo" = He brought it (f.) to me. Indirect (me) before direct (la).

target: Le cuesta levantarse temprano.
english: He has a hard time getting up early.
grammar: "Le cuesta" = it's hard for him/her. Costar uses indirect object like gustar: "Me cuesta entender" = It's hard for me to understand.

target: Le pedí el día libre al jefe.
english: I asked the boss for the day off.

target: Se lo compré en el mercado.
english: I bought it for her at the market.

target: ¿Nos lo confirmas antes del viernes?
english: Can you confirm it for us before Friday?

target: Dentro de un año habremos pagado todo.
english: In a year we'll have paid for everything.

target: La forma en que habla me recuerda a mi abuelo.
english: The way he speaks reminds me of my grandfather.
grammar: "En que" = in which/on which: "El día en que llegó" = The day on which he arrived. Preposition + que for things.

target: La persona a quien llamé no me contestó.
english: The person I called didn't answer me.
grammar: "A quien" = whom (with personal a): "La persona a quien vi" = The person whom I saw.

target: Le tomé el pelo y se lo creyó todo.
english: I pulled his leg and he believed everything.

target: Ella pide un café con leche cada mañana.
english: She orders a coffee with milk every morning.
grammar: "Pedir" has a e→i stem change: the "e" in the stem becomes "i" when stressed (all singular + 3rd plural). Nosotros/vosotros keep the original stem.

target: Yo preparo el desayuno para toda la familia.
english: I make breakfast for the whole family.

target: Yo empiezo a trabajar a las ocho.
english: I start working at eight.
grammar: "Empezar" has a e→ie stem change: the "e" in the stem becomes "ie" when stressed (all singular + 3rd plural). Nosotros/vosotros keep the original stem.

target: Yo llevo gafas desde los doce años.
english: I've worn glasses since I was twelve.

target: Mis amigos y yo quedamos los viernes.
english: My friends and I meet up on Fridays.

target: La reunión es a las tres en la sala grande.
english: The meeting is at three in the big room.
grammar: Ser for events: "La reunión es a las tres." Events use ser for when/where they take place.

target: La pantalla de mi portátil está rota.
english: My laptop screen is broken.

target: A mi hermano le dan miedo las arañas.
english: My brother is scared of spiders.
grammar: Dar miedo: le dan miedo — they scare him. 'Dan' agrees with 'arañas'

target: Conocí a mi mejor amigo en el colegio.
english: I met my best friend at school.

target: Nos vemos mañana en la oficina.
english: See you tomorrow at the office.
grammar: Verse (reciprocal): nos vemos — we see each other

target: Lo hice por ti, no por mí.
english: I did it for you, not for me.
grammar: Por + person = on behalf of, for the sake of

target: Paseamos por la playa al atardecer.
english: We walked along the beach at sunset.

target: Te lo dije ayer, ¿no te acuerdas?
english: I told you yesterday, don't you remember?
grammar: Double object pronouns: te (indirect) + lo (direct) before conjugated verb

target: Se lo voy a mandar por correo.
english: I'm going to send it to him by mail.
grammar: Sound rule: le + lo would sound awkward, so "le" becomes "se": "Se lo dije" (I told it to him), not "le lo dije."

target: Le dije que viniera a las tres.
english: I told him to come at three.
grammar: "Le" = indirect object (to him/her): "Le dije" = I told (to) him. Shows who receives the action.

target: Nos lo confirmaron esta mañana.
english: They confirmed it for us this morning.

target: Me la regaló mi abuela.
english: My grandmother gave it to me as a gift.

target: Les mandé los documentos ayer.
english: I sent them the documents yesterday.
grammar: "Les" = to them: "Les envié un mensaje" = I sent them a message. Plural indirect object pronoun.

target: Me los trajo mi padre del viaje.
english: My father brought them for me from the trip.

target: Le di las gracias por su ayuda.
english: I thanked him for his help.

target: Te lo cuento mañana con calma.
english: I'll tell you about it tomorrow calmly.

target: Me la enseñó con mucho orgullo.
english: She showed it to me with a lot of pride.

target: ¿Quieres que te lo lleve a casa?
english: Do you want me to bring it to you at home?

target: Confirme la reserva antes de las cinco.
english: Confirm the reservation before five.

target: Con mejor tiempo, iríamos a la montaña.
english: With better weather, we'd go to the mountains.

target: Hay que ver lo rápido que crecen los niños.
english: You wouldn't believe how fast kids grow.

target: Más le vale llegar puntual a la reunión de mañana.
english: He'd better be on time for tomorrow's meeting.

target: Este fin de semana nos toca limpiar la casa.
english: It's our turn to clean the house this weekend.

target: La vista desde este mirador es la más espectacular.
english: The view from this lookout is the most spectacular.

target: La sopa de mamá es la más rica del mundo.
english: Mom's soup is the most delicious in the world.

target: La cocina nueva es mucho más práctica que la anterior.
english: The new kitchen is much more practical than the old one.

target: Mi tía es la mejor repostera de la familia.
english: My aunt is the best baker in the family.

target: Envíe el informe antes de las cinco.
english: Send the report before five.

target: No se olvide de copiar al director.
english: Don't forget to copy the director.

target: Dile a papá que la cena está lista.
english: Tell dad dinner is ready.

target: Si pudiéramos reformar la cocina, cocinaríamos más.
english: If we could remodel the kitchen, we'd cook more.

target: Entro en el museo a las diez.
english: I enter the museum at ten.

target: Limpio la casa los sábados.
english: I clean the house on Saturdays.

target: Compro comida para toda la familia.
english: I buy food for the whole family.

target: Los aviones salen de la terminal dos.
english: The planes depart from terminal two.

target: Los museos abren a las diez de la mañana.
english: The museums open at ten in the morning.

target: Un documento con las cifras del mes.
english: A document with the month's figures.

target: El presupuesto incluye los gastos del viaje.
english: The budget includes the travel expenses.

target: La nómina sale el día quince.
english: Payroll comes out on the fifteenth.

target: Los abuelos cuentan las historias de antes.
english: The grandparents tell the old stories.

target: Una cena con toda la familia.
english: A dinner with the whole family.

target: Una receta de la abuela.
english: A recipe from grandmother.

target: La familia cena en el comedor.
english: The family has dinner in the dining room.

target: Me alegro de ver a toda la familia junta.
english: I'm glad to see the whole family together.

target: Gracias por la cena, mamá.
english: Thanks for dinner, mom.

target: Lo compré en la tienda del aeropuerto.
english: I bought it at the airport shop.

target: Le presenté la propuesta al gerente.
english: I presented the proposal to the manager.

target: Les explicamos las condiciones del contrato.
english: We explained the contract terms to them.

target: Lo revisé tres veces antes de enviarlo.
english: I reviewed it three times before sending it.

target: Te ayudo con la limpieza de la casa.
english: I'll help you with cleaning the house.

target: Les prometí a mis hijos ir al parque.
english: I promised my kids we'd go to the park.

target: La tienda cierra a las nueve.
english: The store closes at nine.

target: Cruzamos el puente para llegar a la otra orilla.
english: We crossed the bridge to get to the other side.

target: Me perdí en las callejuelas del centro.
english: I got lost in the narrow streets of the center.

target: Nos sacamos una foto delante de la fuente.
english: We took a photo in front of the fountain.

target: Le compré una camiseta a mi hermano.
english: I bought a t-shirt for my brother.
grammar: "Le" is an indirect object pronoun (to/for him). "Le compré" = I bought for him.

target: Se lo dije al recepcionista.
english: I told it to the receptionist.
grammar: "Se lo dije" — when "le" comes before "lo/la," it becomes "se": le + lo → se lo.

target: Nos sentamos en la terraza para cenar.
english: We sat on the terrace to have dinner.

target: No pierdas el billete, lo necesitas para volver.
english: Don't lose the ticket, you need it to return.

target: Le envié el documento por correo.
english: I sent him the document by email.
grammar: Le = to him/her (indirect object pronoun). Envié = I sent.

target: Le pedí un aumento a mi jefe.
english: I asked my boss for a raise.

target: Le presenté mi renuncia al director.
english: I submitted my resignation to the director.

target: Me inscribí en un curso de formación.
english: I enrolled in a training course.

target: Lo solucioné yo solo.
english: I solved it by myself.
grammar: Lo = it (direct object). Yo solo = by myself.

target: Le di mi opinión al director.
english: I gave my opinion to the director.

target: El proyecto me lo explicó María.
english: María explained the project to me.
grammar: Me lo: double object pronouns — me (to me) + lo (it).

target: Te lo mando por correo ahora.
english: I'll send it to you by email now.

target: Se lo dije al jefe, pero no me escuchó.
english: I told the boss, but he didn't listen to me.

target: La propuesta se la envié al director ayer.
english: I sent the proposal to the director yesterday.

target: Me di cuenta del error a tiempo.
english: I noticed the error in time.

target: Nos reunimos con el equipo cada martes.
english: We meet with the team every Tuesday.

target: Lo hice todo por la empresa.
english: I did everything for the company.

target: La casa huele a comida.
english: The house smells like food.
grammar: Oler: stem-changing (o→hue): huelo, hueles, huele (adds h before ue)

target: Le di un abrazo a mi abuela.
english: I gave my grandmother a hug.
grammar: Le = indirect object pronoun (to her). Dar: di (preterite yo form)

target: Le leí un cuento antes de dormir.
english: I read him a story before bed.

target: Mi padre nos recogió en el aeropuerto.
english: My father picked us up at the airport.
grammar: Nos = us (direct object pronoun placed before conjugated verb)

target: La vecina que cuida a mis hijos es encantadora.
english: The neighbor who watches my kids is lovely.

target: Tus hermanos vendrán a la barbacoa, ¿verdad?
english: Your siblings will come to the barbecue, right?

target: Si mi hijo fuera más responsable, le dejaríamos salir más.
english: If my son were more responsible, we'd let him go out more.

target: En caso de emergencia, llame al número indicado.
english: In case of emergency, call the number indicated.

target: Con respecto al presupuesto, necesitamos más fondos.
english: Regarding the budget, we need more funds.

target: Si me lo hubieras pedido, te habría acompañado sin dudarlo.
english: If you had asked me, I would have accompanied you without hesitation.

target: Lo importante no es cuánto ganas, sino cómo lo gastas.
english: The important thing isn't how much you earn, but how you spend it.

target: Necesito terminarlo para el viernes.
english: I need to finish it by Friday.

target: Es una lástima que no puedas venir a la boda.
english: It's a shame you can't come to the wedding.

target: Acabamos de mudarnos a un apartamento nuevo.
english: We just moved to a new apartment.

target: Dado el contexto actual, conviene actuar con prudencia.
english: Given the current context, it is advisable to act with caution.

target: Sea cual sea el resultado, lo importante es haberlo intentado.
english: Whatever the result may be, the important thing is having tried.

target: Mi madre prepara la cena.
english: My mother prepares dinner.

target: Mi hermano toca la guitarra.
english: My brother plays the guitar.

target: Ella enseña matemáticas en la escuela.
english: She teaches math at school.

target: Ella lleva a sus hijos al colegio.
english: She takes her children to school.

target: Yo aprendí a cocinar con mi madre.
english: I learned to cook with my mother.

target: Nosotros plantamos un árbol en el jardín.
english: We planted a tree in the garden.

target: Nosotros habremos llegado antes de las doce.
english: We will have arrived before twelve.

target: Es poco probable que cambien la politica de la empresa.
english: It's unlikely they'll change the company policy.

target: Estoy a punto de terminar el ultimo capitulo.
english: I'm about to finish the last chapter.
grammar: Estar a punto de + infinitive = to be about to do something.

target: Sigo sin encontrar mi tarjeta de embarque.
english: I still can't find my boarding pass.
grammar: Seguir sin + infinitive = still haven't / still can't.

target: Suelen cerrar las tiendas a la hora de la siesta.
english: They usually close the shops at siesta time.

target: Mis padres suelen cenar a las nueve de la noche.
english: My parents usually have dinner at nine in the evening.

target: Seguimos trabajando en la propuesta del nuevo proyecto.
english: We keep working on the proposal for the new project.

target: Suelo revisar el calendario a primera hora de la manana.
english: I usually check the calendar first thing in the morning.

target: Tendran unos treinta grados en la costa esta semana.
english: It must be about thirty degrees on the coast this week.

target: ¿Hay alguien que hable inglés?
english: Is there someone who speaks English?
grammar: Subjunctive preview! "Hable" (not "habla") because the person is hypothetical — you don't know if they exist. This is the subjunctive mood.

target: Abro la ventana para que entre aire fresco.
english: I open the window to let in fresh air.
grammar: "Para que" triggers the subjunctive: "para que entre." Use subjunctive when the subject changes after "para que." If same subject, just use "para + infinitive."

target: Saca la basura antes de que empiece a oler.
english: Take out the trash before it starts to smell.

target: Compré un abono mensual de transporte para ahorrar dinero.
english: I bought a monthly transport pass to save money.
grammar: "ojalá" always triggers subjunctive: "pueda" (not puede). Expresses hope/wish. From Arabic "inshallah" (God willing).

target: El taxista conocía un atajo que nos ahorró veinte minutos de viaje.
english: The taxi driver knew a shortcut that saved us twenty minutes of travel.
grammar: "esperar que" + subjunctive when there's a different subject. "Espero llegar" (same subject = infinitive). Subject change = subjunctive.

target: La temperatura ha bajado mucho esta noche.
english: The temperature has dropped a lot tonight.
grammar: "molestar" (like gustar) + "que" + subjunctive. Emotion verbs trigger subjunctive when the subject changes.

target: Hay alerta por tormenta en toda la costa este.
english: There's a storm warning along the entire east coast.
grammar: "creer que" + indicative for beliefs. But "no creo que" + subjunctive for doubt: "no creo que tengas razón." Negation triggers subjunctive.

target: Plantamos un huerto en el jardín con tomates, lechugas y hierbas aromáticas.
english: We planted a vegetable garden with tomatoes, lettuce, and aromatic herbs.
grammar: "soportar" = to stand/bear. "Que" + subjunctive (haya) because it's a negative emotional reaction triggering subjunctive.

target: La erosión del suelo es un problema grave en las zonas deforestadas.
english: Soil erosion is a serious problem in deforested areas.
grammar: "parecer" (like gustar): "it seems to me." "Me parece que" + indicative for opinions. "No me parece que" + subjunctive for disagreement.

target: Suspendí el examen de historia porque no estudié lo suficiente.
english: I failed the history exam because I didn't study enough.
grammar: "querer que" + subjunctive. The boss wants (someone else) to finish → different subject → subjunctive "termine."

target: Creo que es importante que hablemos de esto.
english: I think it's important that we talk about this.

target: Es probable que llueva durante el fin de semana.
english: It is likely that it will rain during the weekend.

target: A menos que tomemos medidas urgentes, las consecuencias serán irreversibles.
english: Unless we take urgent measures, the consequences will be irreversible.

target: La deforestación del Amazonas amenaza la biodiversidad del planeta.
english: The deforestation of the Amazon threatens the planet's biodiversity.
grammar: impersonal "se espera que" + subjunctive. "Mejore" = subjunctive of "mejorar."

target: La orquesta sinfónica interpretó obras de Beethoven y Mozart.
english: The symphony orchestra performed works by Beethoven and Mozart.
grammar: "agradecer que" + subjunctive. "Haya guiado" = present perfect subjunctive. Formal "le" for usted.

target: La reunión de seguimiento se celebrará el próximo lunes a las diez.
english: The follow-up meeting will be held next Monday at ten.
grammar: "rogar que" + subjunctive (formal request). "Le ruego" = I beg you (usted). "Lo antes posible" = as soon as possible.

target: Los datos masivos permiten a las empresas tomar decisiones más informadas.
english: Big data allows companies to make more informed decisions.
grammar: "cuantos más...mejores" = the more...the better. "Recopilemos" subjunctive in this construction.

target: Después del entrenamiento, estiro durante quince minutos para evitar lesiones.
english: After training, I stretch for fifteen minutes to avoid injuries.
grammar: "ojalá" + present subjunctive for a wish about a possible future event. "Gane" = subjunctive of "ganar."

target: Mi hermano es alto, pero hoy está enfermo y está en cama.
english: My brother is tall, but today he is sick and is in bed.
grammar: compound gerund "habiendo + participle" = having done. "Pasemos" = subjunctive/imperative nosotros (let's move on).

target: La creatividad surge cuando nos atrevemos a pensar de forma diferente.
english: Creativity arises when we dare to think differently.
grammar: "hágase" = let it be done (passive se + subjunctive). Formal/legal tone. "Que tengamos" = that we may have.

target: Ojalá hubiéramos reservado con antelación.
english: I wish we had booked in advance.
grammar: "Ojalá" + pluperfect subjunctive — expressing regret.

target: Aunque llueva, vamos a hacer la excursión.
english: Even if it rains, we're going to do the excursion.
grammar: "Aunque" + subjunctive — concessive clause for hypothetical.

target: Buscamos un restaurante donde sirvan comida local.
english: We're looking for a restaurant where they serve local food.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent — "un restaurante donde..."

target: Antes de que anochezca, deberíamos volver al hotel.
english: Before it gets dark, we should return to the hotel.

target: Es necesario que todos asistan a la reunión de mañana.
english: It's necessary that everyone attend tomorrow's meeting.
grammar: "Es necesario que" + subjunctive — expressing necessity.

target: A menos que reduzcamos los costos, no seremos rentables.
english: Unless we reduce costs, we won't be profitable.
grammar: "A menos que" + subjunctive — unless clause.

target: Para que el proyecto tenga éxito, necesitamos más inversión.
english: For the project to succeed, we need more investment.
grammar: "Para que" + subjunctive — expressing purpose.

target: Mi madre siempre me dice que tenga cuidado.
english: My mother always tells me to be careful.
grammar: "Que tenga" — subjunctive after "decir que" (telling someone to do something).

target: Me alegra que mis hijos sean tan responsables.
english: I'm glad that my children are so responsible.
grammar: "Me alegra que" + subjunctive — expressing emotion about something.

target: Quiero que mis hijos sepan de dónde vienen.
english: I want my children to know where they come from.

target: Es posible que llueva mañana.
english: It's possible that it will rain tomorrow.

target: Es importante que practiques todos los días.
english: It's important that you practice every day.

target: Me alegro de que hayas venido.
english: I'm glad you came.
grammar: "Me alegro de que" + present perfect subjunctive.

target: Por mucho que lo intente, no consigo entenderlo.
english: No matter how much I try, I can't manage to understand it.
grammar: "Por mucho que" + subjunctive — no matter how much.

target: Haga lo que haga, nunca parece ser suficiente.
english: Whatever he does, it never seems to be enough.
grammar: "Haga lo que haga" — reduplicative subjunctive for emphasis.

target: Te llamo para que sepas que llegaré tarde.
english: I'm calling so that you know I'll arrive late.

target: Antes de que me digas nada, déjame explicarte.
english: Before you say anything, let me explain.

target: ¿Para qué sirve esto?
english: What is this for?
grammar: "¿Para qué?" = What for? / For what purpose? Compare: "¿Por qué?" = Why? (cause). Para qué asks about the goal.

target: Espero que estés bien.
english: I hope you're well.
grammar: "Espero que" + subjunctive — expressing hope.

target: Es necesario que todos asistan.
english: It's necessary that everyone attend.

target: Busco un lugar donde pueda estudiar.
english: I'm looking for a place where I can study.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent.

target: Te llamo para que sepas.
english: I'm calling so that you know.
grammar: "Para que" + subjunctive — purpose.

target: Antes de que te vayas, escúchame.
english: Before you leave, listen to me.

target: Con tal de que llegues a tiempo.
english: As long as you arrive on time.

target: Es mejor que lleguemos temprano.
english: It's better that we arrive early.

target: Es fundamental que los empleados estén motivados.
english: It's fundamental that employees be motivated.

target: Haga lo que haga, nunca parece suficiente.
english: Whatever he does, it never seems enough.
grammar: Reduplicative subjunctive for emphasis.

target: Espero que llegues a tiempo.
english: I hope you arrive on time.
grammar: Espero que + subjunctive: hoping about someone else's action triggers subjunctive. "Espero que vengas" (I hope you come).

target: Es importante que hables con él.
english: It's important that you talk to him.
grammar: Es importante que + subjunctive: impersonal value judgments trigger subjunctive. "Es importante que estudies."

target: Ojalá haga buen tiempo mañana.
english: I hope the weather is good tomorrow.
grammar: Ojalá + subjunctive: expresses a wish. From Arabic "may God grant." "Ojalá llueva" (I hope it rains).

target: Mi madre quiere que la llame más.
english: My mother wants me to call her more.
grammar: Querer que + subjunctive: wanting someone else to do something. "Quiero que vengas" (I want you to come). Different subjects → subjunctive.

target: No es verdad que cueste tanto.
english: It's not true that it costs that much.
grammar: Negating a truth claim triggers subjunctive: "No creo que sea verdad." Denying certainty = uncertainty → subjunctive.

target: Necesito que me envíes el archivo.
english: I need you to send me the file.
grammar: Necesitar que + subjunctive: needing someone else to act. "Necesito que me ayudes" (I need you to help me).

target: Antes de que te vayas, firma esto.
