english: Before you leave, sign this.
grammar: Antes de que always takes subjunctive

target: Cuando llegues a casa, llámame.
english: When you get home, call me.
grammar: "Cuando" + subjunctive for future: "Cuando llegues, llámame." When referring to future events, cuando triggers subjunctive.

target: Es posible que llueva esta tarde.
english: It's possible it'll rain this afternoon.
grammar: Es posible que + subjunctive: expressions of possibility trigger subjunctive. "Es posible que llueva."

target: Es mejor que salgamos temprano.
english: It's better that we leave early.

target: Busco un hotel que tenga piscina.
english: I'm looking for a hotel that has a pool.
grammar: Subjunctive in adjective clauses: when describing something unknown or that might not exist. "Busco alguien que hable francés."

target: A menos que llueva, vamos a la playa.
english: Unless it rains, we're going to the beach.
grammar: A menos que always takes subjunctive

target: Me pide que llegue antes de las nueve.
english: He asks me to arrive before nine.
grammar: Pedir que + subjunctive: requesting someone do something. "Te pido que escuches." Requests about others' actions → subjunctive.

target: Me sorprende que no lo sepas.
english: I'm surprised you don't know.
grammar: Emotions about someone else's actions trigger subjunctive: "Me alegra que estés aquí." The emotion is in the main clause, the trigger is in the que-clause.

target: Aunque llueva, voy a salir.
english: Even if it rains, I'm going out.
grammar: Aunque + subjunctive = even if (hypothetical): "Aunque llueva, iré." Use subjunctive when the situation is hypothetical. Compare: aunque + indicative = although (fact).

target: Quiero que mis hijos sean felices.
english: I want my children to be happy.

target: Deja que te ayude con las maletas.
english: Let me help you with the suitcases.
grammar: Dejar que + subjunctive: allowing someone to do something. "Deja que hable" (Let him speak).

target: Es fundamental que lleguemos puntuales.
english: It's essential that we arrive on time.
grammar: Es fundamental que + subjunctive: value judgments about necessity trigger subjunctive. "Es fundamental que entiendas."

target: Para que entiendas, te lo voy a explicar.
english: So that you understand, I'll explain it to you.
grammar: Para que always takes subjunctive

target: Puede que tengamos que cancelar la reunión.
english: We might have to cancel the meeting.
grammar: Puede que + subjunctive = maybe/it's possible: "Puede que venga" (He might come). Possibility → subjunctive.

target: Sin que nadie se dé cuenta, salimos.
english: Without anyone noticing, we left.
grammar: Sin que + subjunctive = without (someone doing something): "Salió sin que nadie lo viera." Always triggers subjunctive.

target: No le digas nada todavía.
english: Don't tell him anything yet.
grammar: Negative tú command uses subjunctive: no digas

target: No creo que tarde mucho.
english: I don't think I'll take long.

target: Para que lo sepas, mañana no vengo.
english: Just so you know, I'm not coming tomorrow.

target: Espero que tengas un buen día.
english: I hope you have a good day.
grammar: Espero que triggers subjunctive: tengas (tener)

target: Es importante que lleguemos a tiempo a la reunión.
english: It's important that we arrive on time to the meeting.
grammar: Es importante que triggers subjunctive: lleguemos (llegar)

target: Dudo que haya sitio en el restaurante esta noche.
english: I doubt there's a spot at the restaurant tonight.

target: Ojalá haga buen tiempo este fin de semana.
english: I hope the weather is nice this weekend.
grammar: Ojalá always triggers subjunctive: haga (hacer)

target: No creo que sea buena idea salir tan tarde.
english: I don't think it's a good idea to go out so late.

target: Necesito que me envíes el documento hoy.
english: I need you to send me the document today.
grammar: Necesitar que triggers subjunctive: envíes (enviar)

target: Quiero que mis hijos estudien lo que les guste.
english: I want my children to study whatever they like.

target: Es una pena que no puedas venir a la boda.
english: It's a shame you can't come to the wedding.
grammar: Es una pena que triggers subjunctive: puedas (poder)

target: Es fundamental que todo el equipo esté informado.
english: It's essential that the whole team is informed.

target: Quiero que vengas a cenar a casa este domingo.
english: I want you to come to dinner at home this Sunday.

target: Es normal que estés nervioso antes de una presentación.
english: It's normal that you're nervous before a presentation.
grammar: Es normal que triggers subjunctive: estés (estar)

target: No creo que tenga tiempo de pasar por el supermercado.
english: I don't think I have time to stop by the supermarket.

target: Es importante que bebas suficiente agua.
english: It's important that you drink enough water.

target: Quiero que veas esta foto de cuando eras pequeño.
english: I want you to see this photo from when you were little.

target: Aunque suene raro, prefiero trabajar los fines de semana.
english: Even though it sounds weird, I prefer working on weekends.

target: Haga lo que haga, siempre encuentra algo que criticar.
english: Whatever I do, he always finds something to criticize.
grammar: "Haga lo que haga" = whatever I do. Subjunctive repeated: verb + lo que + same verb. Universal concession pattern.

target: Que yo sepa, no han cambiado las condiciones del contrato.
english: As far as I know, they haven't changed the contract terms.
grammar: "Que yo sepa" = as far as I know. Subjunctive because it signals uncertainty — I'm not sure this is complete information.

target: Sea como sea, tenemos que entregar el lunes.
english: Whatever happens, we have to deliver on Monday.
grammar: Sea como sea — whatever happens, come what may (reduplicative subjunctive)

target: Ni que fueras el único que tiene problemas.
english: It's not like you're the only one with problems.
grammar: Ni que + subjunctive — as if, like (incredulous tone)

target: Haga lo que haga, siempre le critican.
english: Whatever he does, they always criticize him.
grammar: Reduplicative subjunctive: haga lo que haga — whatever (he/she) does

target: La habitación doble es mejor que la individual.
english: The double room is better than the single.

target: Quiero que me recomiendes un buen restaurante.
english: I want you to recommend a good restaurant to me.

target: Es importante que lleguemos temprano al aeropuerto.
english: It's important that we arrive at the airport early.

target: Quiero que probemos la comida típica de aquí.
english: I want us to try the local cuisine here.

target: Es necesario que terminemos el informe hoy.
english: It's necessary that we finish the report today.

target: Necesito que alguien me cubra el turno de mañana.
english: I need someone to cover my shift tomorrow.

target: Es imprescindible que firmemos el acuerdo hoy.
english: It's essential that we sign the agreement today.

target: Es necesario que actualicemos la base de datos.
english: It's necessary that we update the database.

target: No creo que el plazo sea suficiente.
english: I don't think the deadline is enough.

target: Quiero que mi hija estudie más.
english: I want my daughter to study more.

target: Es necesario que ordenemos la casa antes de que vengan.
english: It's necessary that we tidy up the house before they come.

target: Ojalá consigamos entradas para el parque.
english: I hope we get tickets to the park.

target: Quiero que la cena de Navidad sea especial.
english: I want Christmas dinner to be special.

target: Ojalá los niños disfruten de las vacaciones.
english: I hope the kids enjoy the vacation.

target: Necesito que mi marido me ayude con la compra.
english: I need my husband to help me with the shopping.

target: Ojalá el clima fuera más cálido.
english: I wish the weather were warmer.

target: No creo que haya habitaciones disponibles.
english: I don't think there are rooms available.
grammar: "No creo que" triggers subjunctive because it expresses doubt. "Haya" is the subjunctive of "haber."

target: Dudo que el tren llegue a tiempo.
english: I doubt the train will arrive on time.

target: Necesito un hotel que tenga estacionamiento.
english: I need a hotel that has parking.
grammar: Subjunctive in relative clauses: "que tenga" because the hotel is hypothetical/unknown. If the hotel were known, you'd use indicative.

target: Confirme su vuelo veinticuatro horas antes.
english: Confirm your flight twenty-four hours in advance.
grammar: Formal command (usted): "confirme" — subjunctive form used as imperative.

target: Cuando llegues al hotel, avísame.
english: When you arrive at the hotel, let me know.
grammar: "Cuando" + future action uses subjunctive: "cuando llegues" (not "cuando llegas").

target: Antes de que salgas, revisa tu equipaje.
english: Before you leave, check your luggage.
grammar: "Antes de que" always triggers subjunctive.

target: Después de que cierre el museo, iremos a cenar.
english: After the museum closes, we'll go to dinner.

target: Ojalá no haga demasiado calor mañana.
english: I hope it's not too hot tomorrow.

target: Quiero que nos sentemos cerca de la ventana.
english: I want us to sit near the window.

target: Sugiero que cenemos en el restaurante del puerto.
english: I suggest we have dinner at the port restaurant.

target: Es fundamental que llevemos agua si vamos a hacer senderismo.
english: It's essential that we bring water if we're going hiking.

target: En caso de que pierdas el pasaporte, acude al consulado.
english: In case you lose your passport, go to the consulate.
grammar: "En caso de que" always takes subjunctive — it introduces a hypothetical/future possibility.

target: Sin que nadie nos lo advirtiera, terminamos en una zona peligrosa.
english: Without anyone warning us, we ended up in a dangerous area.
grammar: "Sin que" (without) + subjunctive. Always triggers subjunctive.

target: Espero que el jefe apruebe el proyecto.
english: I hope the boss approves the project.
grammar: Subjunctive after esperar que: apruebe (not aprueba).

target: Es importante que llegues a tiempo.
english: It's important that you arrive on time.
grammar: Subjunctive after es importante que: llegues.

target: Buscan a alguien que hable tres idiomas.
english: They're looking for someone who speaks three languages.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent: que hable.

target: No es necesario que vengas mañana.
english: It's not necessary for you to come tomorrow.

target: Quiero que todo esté listo para el lunes.
english: I want everything to be ready by Monday.

target: Espero que la entrevista vaya bien.
english: I hope the interview goes well.

target: Quiero que alguien revise este contrato.
english: I want someone to review this contract.

target: Dudo que acepten nuestra propuesta.
english: I doubt they'll accept our proposal.

target: Es mejor que esperemos a tener más información.
english: It's better for us to wait until we have more information.

target: Con tal de que acepten las condiciones, firmaremos mañana.
english: Provided they accept the conditions, we'll sign tomorrow.
grammar: Con tal de que + subjunctive: conditional conjunction.

target: A menos que bajen el precio, no compraremos.
english: Unless they lower the price, we won't buy.
grammar: A menos que + subjunctive: negative conditional.

target: Sea cual sea tu decisión, la respetaré.
english: Whatever your decision, I'll respect it.
grammar: Sea cual sea: subjunctive reduplication for 'whatever it may be'.

target: Tanto si aceptan como si no, seguiremos con el plan.
english: Whether they accept or not, we'll continue with the plan.
grammar: Tanto si... como si: whether... or. Uses indicative or subjunctive.

target: El perro ladra cuando alguien llega.
english: The dog barks when someone arrives.
grammar: Cuando + present indicative for habitual actions (not subjunctive)

target: Quiero que los niños coman más verdura.
english: I want the kids to eat more vegetables.
grammar: Subjunctive after quiero que: coman (not comen)

target: Espero que mi hijo apruebe el examen.
english: I hope my son passes the exam.

target: No creo que mi madre venga este fin de semana.
english: I don't think my mother is coming this weekend.

target: Me alegro de que estéis todos bien.
english: I'm glad you're all well.
grammar: Alegrarse de que + subjunctive: estéis (ustedes form)

target: No dejes los zapatos en la entrada.
english: Don't leave your shoes at the entrance.
grammar: Negative tú imperative uses subjunctive: no dejes (not no deja)

target: Es importante que hablemos con nuestros hijos.
english: It's important that we talk to our kids.

target: No creo que el niño tenga fiebre.
english: I don't think the boy has a fever.

target: Te lo digo para que no te preocupes.
english: I'm telling you so you don't worry.
grammar: "Para que" + subjunctive = so that: "Hablo despacio para que entiendas." Purpose involving another person → subjunctive.

target: Busco una canguro que tenga experiencia con bebés.
english: I'm looking for a babysitter who has experience with babies.
grammar: Subjunctive in relative clause: tenga (unknown/desired person)

target: No creo que haya suficiente comida para todos.
english: I don't think there's enough food for everyone.

target: Puede que mis padres vengan a vernos en Semana Santa.
english: My parents might come see us at Easter.
grammar: Puede que + subjunctive = maybe: "Puede que llueva mañana." Expressing possibility always triggers subjunctive.

target: Es fundamental que haya comunicación en la pareja.
english: It's essential that there be communication in a couple.

target: Espero que la tarta de cumpleaños salga bien.
english: I hope the birthday cake turns out well.

target: Por mucho que le insista, mi hijo no quiere comer brócoli.
english: No matter how much I insist, my son won't eat broccoli.
grammar: Por mucho que + subjunctive = no matter how much

target: Siempre y cuando los niños estén contentos, lo demás es secundario.
english: As long as the kids are happy, everything else is secondary.
grammar: Siempre y cuando + subjunctive = as long as, provided that

target: De haber tenido más hijos, habríamos necesitado una casa más grande.
english: Had we had more kids, we would have needed a bigger house.
grammar: De + infinitive = si + pluperfect subjunctive (formal alternative)

target: A menos que el niño mejore, no irá al colegio mañana.
english: Unless the boy gets better, he won't go to school tomorrow.
grammar: "A menos que" + subjunctive = unless: "Iré a menos que llueva." Conditions about uncertain events → subjunctive.

target: No es que no confíe en mis hijos, sino que me preocupo por ellos.
english: It's not that I don't trust my kids, but rather that I worry about them.
grammar: No es que + subjunctive, sino que + indicative = it's not that... but rather...

target: Antes de que los niños lleguen, quiero tener la casa limpia.
english: Before the kids arrive, I want to have the house clean.
grammar: Antes de que always takes subjunctive: lleguen

target: Aun cuando no estemos de acuerdo, nos respetamos.
english: Even when we don't agree, we respect each other.
grammar: Aun cuando + subjunctive = even when/even if

target: En caso de que el bebé se despierte, dale el biberón.
english: In case the baby wakes up, give him the bottle.
grammar: En caso de que + subjunctive = in case

target: Haga lo que haga mi hija, siempre la apoyaré.
english: Whatever my daughter does, I'll always support her.
grammar: Haga lo que haga = whatever she does (reduplicative subjunctive)

target: Sin que nadie se diera cuenta, el niño se comió todos los bombones.
english: Without anyone noticing, the boy ate all the chocolates.
grammar: "Sin que" always triggers subjunctive: "Se fue sin que yo lo supiera" (He left without me knowing).

target: Por más que lo intente, no logro entenderlo.
english: No matter how hard I try, I can't manage to understand it.
grammar: Por más que + subjunctive = no matter how much.

target: Lo que más me molesta es que no me lo hayan dicho antes.
english: What bothers me the most is that they didn't tell me before.
grammar: Lo que + subjunctive for emotional reactions about events.

target: No se preocupe, todo va a salir bien.
english: Don't worry, everything will turn out fine.
grammar: Negative usted commands also use the subjunctive form.

target: No creo que sea tan fácil como parece.
english: I don't think it's as easy as it seems.
grammar: No creo que + subjunctive — negated belief triggers subjunctive.

target: El hecho de que no haya venido me preocupa.
english: The fact that he hasn't come worries me.
grammar: El hecho de que + subjunctive for factual statements that trigger emotion.

target: Por más que estudie, no aprueba el examen.
english: No matter how much she studies, she doesn't pass the exam.
grammar: Por más que + subjunctive = no matter how much.

target: Que yo sepa, nadie ha confirmado la reserva.
english: As far as I know, nobody has confirmed the reservation.
grammar: Que yo sepa = as far as I know, set subjunctive phrase.

target: No creo que hayan entendido bien las instrucciones.
english: I don't think they understood the instructions well.

target: Por muy inteligente que sea, necesita estudiar más.
english: However intelligent he may be, he needs to study more.
grammar: Por muy + adjective + que + subjunctive = however much.

target: Pase lo que pase, estaremos juntos.
english: Whatever happens, we'll be together.
grammar: Pase lo que pase = reduplicative subjunctive for 'whatever happens'.

target: Es importante que estudies más.
english: It's important that you study more.

target: Quiero que vengas a mi fiesta.
english: I want you to come to my party.

target: No parece que vaya a mejorar el tiempo esta semana.
english: It doesn't seem like the weather is going to improve this week.
grammar: No parece que + subjunctive for negated perception.

target: Aunque llueva, vamos a salir de todas formas.
english: Even if it rains, we're going out anyway.
grammar: Aunque + subjunctive for hypothetical concessions (even if).

target: Por mucho que insistas, no voy a cambiar de opinion.
english: No matter how much you insist, I'm not going to change my mind.
grammar: Por mucho que + subjunctive for futile emphasis.

target: Busco a alguien que sepa hablar tres idiomas.
english: I'm looking for someone who can speak three languages.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent: alguien que + subjunctive.

target: Necesito un hotel que tenga piscina y este cerca del centro.
english: I need a hotel that has a pool and is close to the center.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent: un hotel que + subjunctive.

target: Dudo que el vuelo salga a tiempo con esta tormenta.
english: I doubt the flight will leave on time with this storm.

target: Es mejor que reserves con anticipacion para conseguir buen precio.
english: It's better that you book in advance to get a good price.

target: Es importante que los ninos aprendan a compartir desde pequenos.
english: It's important that children learn to share from a young age.

target: Es posible que mi primo venga con toda su familia.
english: It's possible that my cousin is coming with his whole family.

target: Es necesario que todos entreguen el informe antes del viernes.
english: It's necessary that everyone submit the report before Friday.

target: Por mas que revisemos los datos, siempre hay algo que se nos escapa.
english: No matter how much we review the data, there's always something we miss.
grammar: Por más que + subjunctive for emphasis on futility.

target: Vamos juntos.
english: Let's go together.
grammar: "Vamos" means both "we go" and "let's go!" It's one of the few verbs where the indicative doubles as a command.

target: Cuídate mucho y buena suerte.
english: Take care and good luck.
grammar: "Cuídate" is an informal command (tú imperative) of the reflexive verb cuidarse. The reflexive pronoun "-te" attaches to the end of imperative verbs.

target: Vivo en un apartamento pequeño.
english: I live in a small apartment.
grammar: "perdone" is usted imperative of "perdonar." Use usted form with strangers for politeness.

target: Puse las flores en un jarrón en la mesa del salón.
english: I put the flowers in a vase on the living room table.
grammar: "tome" is usted imperative. Formal commands: -ar verbs → -e (tome, gire), -er/-ir verbs → -a (siga, suba).

target: Hay que pasar la aspiradora debajo del sofá.
english: We need to vacuum under the sofa.
grammar: "siga" is usted imperative of "seguir" (e→i). Irregular command. "Todo recto" = straight ahead.

target: El edificio no tiene ascensor y vivimos en el quinto piso.
english: The building has no elevator and we live on the fifth floor.
grammar: "cruce" (usted command) + future "verá" to describe what will happen next. Future as prediction: "you'll see."

target: ¿Me pasas la sal y la pimienta, por favor?
english: Can you pass me the salt and pepper, please?
grammar: "traer" irregular: traigo (yo). "¿Me trae...?" = Can you bring me...? Polite request using present indicative as soft command.

target: Aprendí a hacer empanadas argentinas cuando viví en Buenos Aires un verano.
english: I learned to make Argentinian empanadas when I lived in Buenos Aires one summer.
grammar: "déjeme" = let me (usted command of "dejar" + me). "Pensarlo" — lo replaces the decision/choice. Pronoun attaches to infinitive.

target: Ponme una caña y una ración de croquetas de jamón.
english: Give me a small beer and a portion of ham croquettes.

target: La cola del supermercado era tan larga que esperé quince minutos para pagar.
english: The supermarket line was so long that I waited fifteen minutes to pay.
grammar: "pasarlo bien/mal/genial" = to have a good/bad/great time. "Lo" is a fixed neuter pronoun here, doesn't change.

target: Hay mucho viento hoy, ten cuidado con el sombrero.
english: It's very windy today, be careful with your hat.

target: No me parece justo que él tenga más oportunidades.
english: It doesn't seem fair to me that he has more opportunities.

target: Intenta ponerte en su lugar antes de juzgar.
english: Try to put yourself in their shoes before judging.

target: Disculpe, ¿sabe usted si el museo abre los domingos?
english: Excuse me, do you know if the museum is open on Sundays?

target: Dobla a la derecha en la esquina.
english: Turn right at the corner.
grammar: Imperative form of "girar" — used for giving directions.

target: Dime con quién andas y te diré quién eres.
english: Tell me who you walk with and I'll tell you who you are.

target: Para ser principiante, habla muy bien.
english: For a beginner, he speaks very well.
grammar: "Para" = considering: "Para ser nuevo, lo haces bien." Surprising given the circumstances.

target: Dámelo, por favor.
english: Give it to me, please.
grammar: Pronouns attach to affirmative commands.

target: No me lo digas.
english: Don't tell me.
grammar: Pronouns go before negative commands.

target: Ven aquí, por favor.
english: Come here, please.
grammar: Informal affirmative command of "venir."

target: No hables tan alto.
english: Don't speak so loud.
grammar: Negative informal command — uses subjunctive.

target: Siga todo recto.
english: Go straight ahead.
grammar: Formal command of "seguir."

target: No toque eso.
english: Don't touch that.
grammar: Formal negative command.

target: Sal de aquí ahora mismo.
english: Get out of here right now.

target: Dame el vaso, por favor.
english: Give me the glass, please.
grammar: Definite article "el" goes with masculine singular nouns. "La" for feminine. Article must agree in gender and number.

target: Un poco de sal, nada más.
english: A little salt, nothing else.

target: Me da pena que se vaya.
english: It makes me sad that she's leaving.
grammar: "Dar pena" uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me dar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Para ser extranjero, hablas muy bien.
english: For a foreigner, you speak very well.
grammar: "Para" = considering: "Para ser niño, cocina muy bien." Surprising given the circumstances.

target: Para llegar a tiempo, salimos temprano.
english: To arrive on time, we left early.
grammar: Para + infinitive for purpose / goal

target: Para el viernes necesito el informe.
english: I need the report by Friday.
grammar: "Para" = by/for (deadline): "Necesito esto para el lunes." Para sets the time limit.

target: Para mí es muy importante la familia.
english: For me, family is very important.
grammar: "Para mí" = in my opinion / for me: "Para mí, es demasiado caro." Para + person for personal perspective.

target: Pregunta por María cuando llegues.
english: Ask for María when you arrive.
grammar: "Preguntar por" = to ask about/for someone: "Preguntó por ti" = He asked about you. The preposition "por" is required.

target: Para el cumpleaños de mi madre, vamos a un restaurante.
english: For my mother's birthday, we're going to a restaurant.

target: Ponlo aquí, por favor.
english: Put it here, please.
grammar: Pronouns attach to affirmative commands: "Hazlo" (Do it). The pronoun goes at the end as one word.

target: Dime qué necesitas.
english: Tell me what you need.
grammar: Pronouns attach to affirmative tú commands: "Dime" = Tell me. "Di" (irregular command of decir) + "me."

target: No toques eso, por favor.
english: Don't touch that, please.

target: Siéntese, por favor.
english: Please, have a seat.
grammar: Formal usted command: "Siéntese" = Sit down (formal). Formed from subjunctive + pronoun attached: siente + se.

target: Sal de ahí ahora mismo.
english: Get out of there right now.

target: Envíeme el presupuesto por correo.
english: Send me the budget by email.
grammar: Formal usted command with pronoun: "Envíeme" = Send me. Subjunctive form + pronoun attached.

target: Haz la tarea antes de jugar.
english: Do your homework before playing.
grammar: Irregular tú command: "Haz" (do/make). Eight verbs have irregular tú commands: haz, di, sal, ven, ten, pon, sé, ve.

target: Ten cuidado con el escalón.
english: Be careful with the step.
grammar: Irregular tú command: "Ten" (have/hold). One of eight irregular tú commands: haz, di, sal, ven, ten, pon, sé, ve.

target: Abre la ventana, hace mucho calor.
english: Open the window, it's very hot.

target: Sé paciente, ya casi estamos.
english: Be patient, we're almost there.
grammar: Irregular tú command: "Sé" (be). One of eight irregular tú commands: haz, di, sal, ven, ten, pon, sé, ve.

target: Ponte el abrigo, que hace frío.
english: Put your coat on, it's cold.
grammar: Reflexive tú command: "Ponte" = Put on (yourself). Irregular command "pon" + reflexive "te" attached.

target: Siga todo recto y gire a la derecha.
english: Go straight ahead and turn right.
grammar: Formal usted commands for directions: "Siga recto, gire a la derecha." Subjunctive forms used as polite commands.

target: Acuéstate temprano, mañana hay que madrugar.
english: Go to bed early, we have to get up early tomorrow.

target: Cállate un segundo, estoy hablando.
english: Be quiet for a second, I'm talking.
grammar: Reflexive tú command: "Cállate" = Be quiet. Regular command "calla" + reflexive "te" attached.

target: Ve al supermercado y compra leche.
english: Go to the supermarket and buy milk.
grammar: Irregular tú command: "Ve" (go). From ir. One of eight irregular tú commands: haz, di, sal, ven, ten, pon, sé, ve.

target: No te andes con rodeos y dime qué pasó.
english: Don't beat around the bush and tell me what happened.

target: Para mí, lo más importante es la familia.
english: For me, the most important thing is family.
grammar: "Para mí" = in my opinion: "Para mí, es mejor así." Para + person for personal viewpoint.

target: Para ser su primer trabajo, lo hace muy bien.
english: For it being her first job, she does very well.
grammar: Para + ser = considering that

target: Para ser marzo, hace bastante calor.
english: For March, it's quite warm.
grammar: Para + ser + noun = considering, given

target: Para ser honesto, no me gustó el hotel.
english: To be honest, I didn't like the hotel.
grammar: Para ser honesto = to be honest (introducing an opinion)

target: Tráemelo cuando puedas.
english: Bring it to me when you can.
grammar: Command + double pronouns: "Tráemelo" = Bring it to me. Pronouns attach in order: indirect (me) + direct (lo). Note accent added.

target: Cierra la puerta, por favor.
english: Close the door, please.
grammar: Cierra — tú imperative of cerrar (e→ie stem change doesn't apply in imperative)

target: No te olvides de comprar pan.
english: Don't forget to buy bread.
grammar: No te olvides — negative tú command uses subjunctive: olvides (olvidar)

target: Pase, por favor, y siéntese.
english: Come in, please, and have a seat.
grammar: Pase/siéntese — usted imperatives, more formal

target: No abras la ventana, hace mucho frío.
english: Don't open the window, it's very cold.

target: Sal de casa temprano si no quieres llegar tarde.
english: Leave the house early if you don't want to be late.
grammar: Irregular tú command: "Sal" (go out/leave). From salir. One of eight irregular tú commands.

target: Disculpe, ¿podría indicarme dónde está el baño?
english: Excuse me, could you tell me where the bathroom is?

target: Trae los documentos a la oficina mañana.
english: Bring the documents to the office tomorrow.

target: Ponte el abrigo, que fuera hace frío.
english: Put on your coat, it's cold outside.
grammar: "Ponte" = Put it on / Put on (yourself). Irregular command "pon" + reflexive "te."

target: Ten paciencia, ya casi terminamos.
english: Be patient, we're almost done.
grammar: "Ten" = Have/Hold (command). Irregular tú command of tener. "Ten cuidado" = Be careful.

target: No te preocupes tanto, todo va a salir bien.
english: Don't worry so much, everything is going to turn out fine.

target: Di la verdad, ¿fuiste tú?
english: Tell the truth, was it you?
grammar: "Di" = Say/Tell (command). Irregular tú command of decir. "Dime la verdad" = Tell me the truth.

target: Dime dónde está la parada de autobús.
english: Tell me where the bus stop is.

target: Compra los souvenirs en el mercado local.
english: Buy the souvenirs at the local market.

target: Habla con tu supervisor sobre el problema.
english: Talk to your supervisor about the problem.

target: Pide presupuesto a tres proveedores.
english: Ask for quotes from three suppliers.

target: Pon la mesa para la cena.
english: Set the table for dinner.

target: Ven a cenar, que ya está listo.
english: Come to dinner, it's ready.

target: Para terminar el proyecto, necesitamos más tiempo.
english: To finish the project, we need more time.

target: Para los niños, lo más importante es jugar.
english: For kids, the most important thing is playing.

target: Para vivir bien, hay que estar cerca de la familia.
english: To live well, you need to be close to family.

target: Busco un vuelo que salga por la mañana.
english: I'm looking for a flight that leaves in the morning.

target: No te olvides el pasaporte.
english: Don't forget the passport.
grammar: Negative tú commands use subjunctive: "no olvides." With reflexive: "no te olvides."

target: Pregunta en recepción por los horarios.
english: Ask at the reception about the schedules.

target: Venid con nosotros a la excursión.
english: Come with us on the excursion.
grammar: "Venid" — affirmative ustedes command. Used in Spain for informal plural.

target: Envíame el presupuesto antes del viernes.
english: Send me the budget before Friday.
grammar: Imperative with pronoun: envía + me = envíame.

target: Haz una copia del contrato, por favor.
english: Make a copy of the contract, please.
grammar: Imperative tú form of hacer: haz.

target: Pon los archivos en la carpeta compartida.
english: Put the files in the shared folder.
grammar: Imperative tú form of poner: pon.

target: Sal de la oficina antes de las ocho.
english: Leave the office before eight.
grammar: Imperative tú of salir: sal.

target: No te vayas sin guardar los cambios.
english: Don't leave without saving the changes.
grammar: Negative tú command of irse: no te vayas.

target: Ten cuidado con esos datos confidenciales.
english: Be careful with that confidential data.
grammar: Imperative tú of tener: ten.

target: Trae los informes a mi despacho.
english: Bring the reports to my office.
grammar: Imperative tú of traer: trae.

target: Dile que venga a la reunión a las cuatro.
english: Tell him to come to the meeting at four.

target: Los niños ven la tele después de cenar.
english: The kids watch TV after dinner.

target: Cerrad la puerta con llave al salir.
english: Lock the door when you leave.
grammar: Vosotros command: "Cerrad" = Close (you all). Formed by replacing -r of infinitive with -d: cerrar → cerrad. Spain only.

target: Date prisa, que llegamos tarde al colegio.
english: Hurry up, we're late for school.
grammar: Date prisa — reflexive imperative: darse prisa (to hurry up)

target: Deja de pegarle a tu hermana.
english: Stop hitting your sister.

target: Para llegar al centro, toma la segunda a la derecha.
english: To get to the center, take the second right.

target: No toques eso, es peligroso.
english: Don't touch that, it's dangerous.
grammar: Negative tú commands use the subjunctive form.

target: Dime la verdad, ¿qué pasó?
english: Tell me the truth, what happened?

target: Prueba este plato; está delicioso.
english: Try this dish — it's delicious.

target: Ponga la maleta en el compartimento de arriba.
english: Put the suitcase in the overhead compartment.

target: No te olvides de traer los documentos.
english: Don't forget to bring the documents.

target: Espere un momento, por favor.
english: Wait a moment, please.

target: Sigue recto y gira a la izquierda.
english: Go straight and turn left.
grammar: Sigue is an affirmative tú command of seguir (irregular).

target: Trae una chaqueta por si hace frío.
english: Bring a jacket in case it's cold.

target: Busco una ruta que no tenga demasiado trafico.
english: I'm looking for a route that doesn't have too much traffic.

target: ¿Te gustaría tomar un café?
english: Would you like to have a coffee?
grammar: "Gustaría" is the conditional of gustar — would like. "Te gustaría" = it would please you = would you like. Politer than "¿quieres?"

target: Hoy almorcé en la cafetería con mis compañeros.
english: Today I had lunch in the cafeteria with my coworkers.

target: Me gustaría cambiar mi rutina para incluir más tiempo al aire libre.
english: I'd like to change my routine to include more time outdoors.
grammar: "Me gustaría" = I would like (conditional of gustar). Conditional = infinitive + ía/ías/ía/íamos/ían. Used for polite requests and wishes.

target: Montamos una estantería nueva que compramos el fin de semana.
english: We assembled a new bookshelf that we bought over the weekend.
grammar: "enfrente de" = in front of / across from. "Del" = de + el (contraction, always required). "Al" = a + el.

target: Vamos a pedir unas tapas para compartir.
english: Let's order some tapas to share.
grammar: conditional "podría" for polite requests. "Traernos" — object pronoun attaches to infinitive: traer + nos.

target: Hay mucho tráfico a esta hora, mejor vamos en metro.
english: There's a lot of traffic at this time, we'd better go by metro.
grammar: "deber" (should/must) + infinitive. Conditional "debería" = should (softer). "Debo" = I must (stronger obligation).

target: Envié mi currículum a varias empresas.
english: I sent my resume to several companies.

target: Me duele la oreja derecha desde ayer.
english: My right ear has hurt since yesterday.
grammar: "podría" (conditional) for polite requests. "Indicarme" — pronoun on infinitive. "Dónde está" = indirect question.

target: Deberías lavarte las manos antes de comer.
english: You should wash your hands before eating.

target: Estoy orgulloso de lo que hemos conseguido.
english: I am proud of what we have achieved.
grammar: "debería" (conditional of deber) = should. Softer than "debes" (you must). "Ir al médico" = to go to the doctor ("al" = a + el).

target: Pienso que deberíamos hablar sobre el problema.
english: I think we should talk about the problem.

target: Sería maravilloso poder hablar cinco idiomas.
english: It would be wonderful to be able to speak five languages.

target: Me aseguró que todo estaría preparado para la fecha.
english: She assured me that everything would be ready by the date.

target: Nadie imaginaba que la situación cambiaría tan rápido.
english: Nobody imagined that the situation would change so quickly.

target: Me gustaría suscribirme a ese boletín informativo.
english: I would like to subscribe to that newsletter.
grammar: conditional "podrías" for polite requests. "Más despacio" = more slowly. Comparative: más + adverb.

target: Sería conveniente que programáramos una videollamada para discutirlo.
english: It would be convenient for us to schedule a video call to discuss it.

target: Las organizaciones no gubernamentales desempeñan un papel fundamental en la ayuda humanitaria.
english: Non-governmental organizations play a fundamental role in humanitarian aid.
grammar: "según" = according to. "La mayoría" takes singular verb in Spanish: "la mayoría está" (not *están).

target: El acceso universal a internet debería considerarse un derecho básico.
english: Universal access to the internet should be considered a basic right.

target: El turismo rural es una forma estupenda de conocer la cultura local.
english: Rural tourism is a wonderful way to get to know the local culture.
grammar: "usted" form for formal address. Conditional "podría" for politeness. "Recomendarme" — pronoun on infinitive.

target: ¿Le importaría indicarme cómo llegar a la plaza mayor?
english: Would you mind telling me how to get to the main square?

target: ¿Sería usted tan amable de indicarme la salida más cercana?
english: Would you be so kind as to show me the nearest exit?

target: Los programadores utilizan bases de datos para almacenar la información.
english: Programmers use databases to store information.
grammar: "habría que" + infinitive = one should/would need to (conditional of hay que). Suggestion/recommendation.

target: Quedo a la espera de su respuesta. Atentamente, María García.
english: I await your reply. Sincerely, María García.

target: ¿Podría enviarme el acta de la reunión anterior, por favor?
english: Could you send me the minutes of the previous meeting, please?

target: ¿Has probado alguna vez la escalada en un rocódromo cubierto?
english: Have you ever tried climbing at an indoor climbing wall?
grammar: conditional "gustaría" for wishes. "Aprender a" + infinitive. "Al óleo" = in oils (art technique).

target: Me gustaría reservar una habitación.
english: I would like to book a room.
grammar: "Me gustaría" + infinitive — polite way to express a wish.

target: Me encantaría volver a este lugar algún día.
english: I'd love to come back to this place someday.

target: Deberías reducir el consumo de azúcar.
english: You should reduce your sugar intake.

target: En mi opinión, la educación debería ser gratuita.
english: In my opinion, education should be free.

target: Me gustaría un café.
english: I would like a coffee.
grammar: Conditional for polite requests.

target: Me iría de vacaciones ahora mismo.
english: I'd go on vacation right now.

target: Hay una panadería en la esquina.
english: There's a bakery on the corner.

target: Nos encontramos en la cafetería.
english: We met at the café.
grammar: "Encontrarse" = to meet/run into each other. "Nos encontramos en el café" = We met at the café.

target: Yo en tu lugar buscaría otro trabajo.
english: In your place, I would look for another job.
grammar: "Yo en tu lugar" + conditional: "Yo en tu lugar, hablaría con ella." Common pattern for giving advice — If I were you, I would...

target: Me gustaría viajar a Japón.
english: I would like to travel to Japan.
grammar: "Me gustaría" = I would like. Conditional of gustar for polite wishes. More polite than "quiero."

target: ¿Podrías cerrar la ventana?
english: Could you close the window?
grammar: "Podrías" = could you? Conditional of poder for polite requests. "¿Podrías ayudarme?" Softer than "¿Puedes...?"

target: ¿Te importaría bajar la música?
english: Would you mind turning down the music?
grammar: Conditional for polite requests: "¿Podría ver el menú?" Using conditional makes requests softer and more formal.

target: Me encantaría conocer a tu familia.
english: I would love to meet your family.

target: Con un poco de suerte, conseguiríamos el contrato.
english: With a bit of luck, we would get the contract.

target: Sería genial ir todos juntos.
english: It would be great to all go together.

target: No debería comer tanto dulce.
english: I shouldn't eat so many sweets.

target: Con más personal, terminaríamos antes.
english: With more staff, we would finish sooner.
grammar: Conditional for hypothetical result: "Si tuviera dinero, viajaría." The conditional clause answers "what would happen if..."

target: Creo que sería mejor esperar.
english: I think it would be better to wait.

target: Nos gustaría reservar una mesa para cuatro.
english: We would like to reserve a table for four.
grammar: "Nos gustaría" = We would like. Conditional of gustar for polite group wishes. More formal than "queremos."

target: Deberías probar la comida de aquí.
english: You should try the food here.

target: ¿Le importaría esperar un momento?
english: Would you mind waiting a moment?
grammar: "¿Le importaría...?" = Would you mind...? Very formal polite request using conditional of importar.

target: Yo que tú no aceptaría esa oferta.
english: If I were you, I wouldn't accept that offer.
grammar: Yo que tú + conditional — giving advice

target: Me mudaría a otra ciudad sin pensarlo.
english: I would move to another city without thinking twice.

target: Si no fuera por mi familia, no habría seguido adelante.
english: If it weren't for my family, I wouldn't have kept going.

target: A estas alturas, ya deberías saber cómo funciona.
english: At this point, you should already know how it works.

target: Los hermanos de María son gemelos.
english: Maria's brothers are twins.

target: Mandé el paquete por mensajería.
english: I sent the package by courier.
grammar: Por + means = by means of

target: ¿Te importaría bajar la música un poco?
english: Would you mind turning down the music a bit?

target: ¿Podrías ayudarme con esta maleta?
english: Could you help me with this suitcase?
grammar: "¿Podrías...?" = Could you...? Conditional of poder makes requests polite. "¿Podrías repetir?" = Could you repeat?

target: Me encantaría ir a la playa este fin de semana.
english: I'd love to go to the beach this weekend.

target: Con más tiempo, aprendería a tocar la guitarra.
english: With more time, I would learn to play guitar.

target: ¿Te gustaría venir a cenar a casa?
english: Would you like to come over for dinner?

target: Deberías descansar, tienes mala cara.
english: You should rest, you don't look well.
grammar: "Deberías" = You should. Conditional of deber for gentle advice. Softer than the direct "debes."

target: ¿Sería posible cambiar la fecha de la reunión?
english: Would it be possible to change the meeting date?

target: ¿Les importaría esperar un momento?
english: Would you mind waiting a moment?

target: Con un poco de suerte, llegaríamos a tiempo.
english: With a bit of luck, we would arrive on time.

target: ¿Te gustaría trabajar desde casa los viernes?
english: Would you like to work from home on Fridays?

target: Con más experiencia, conseguirías ese puesto.
english: With more experience, you'd get that position.
grammar: Conditional conseguirías for hypothetical outcome

target: No sería mala idea empezar a ahorrar.
english: It wouldn't be a bad idea to start saving.

target: ¿Te importaría echarme una mano con la mudanza?
english: Would you mind giving me a hand with the move?

target: Yo no me fiaría de esa empresa.
english: I wouldn't trust that company.
grammar: Conditional of reflexive verb fiarse: "No me fiaría" = I wouldn't trust. Reflexive pronoun stays before the conditional form.

target: A estas alturas, debería haberlo resuelto ya.
english: By this point, he should have solved it already.

target: Debería habérselo dicho antes en lugar de esperar.
english: He should have told him sooner instead of waiting.

target: No me habría enterado de no ser por tu mensaje.
english: I wouldn't have found out if it weren't for your message.
grammar: De no ser por — if it weren't for, formal conditional structure

target: Me encantaría conocer Japón algún día.
english: I'd love to visit Japan someday.

target: ¿Me recomendarías algún restaurante local?
english: Would you recommend a local restaurant to me?

target: ¿Te importaría revisar mi presentación?
english: Would you mind reviewing my presentation?

target: Si mi hijo no estuviera enfermo, iría al colegio.
english: If my son weren't sick, he'd go to school.

target: A mi hermana le fascina la repostería.
english: My sister is fascinated by baking.

target: Desayunamos en una cafetería cerca del hotel.
english: We had breakfast at a cafe near the hotel.

target: Me gustaría reservar una mesa para las ocho.
english: I would like to reserve a table for eight.
grammar: "Gustaría" is the conditional of "gustar." "Me gustaría" is the polite way to say "I would like."

target: ¿Le importaría hablar más despacio?
english: Would you mind speaking more slowly?

target: ¿Sería posible salir más temprano?
english: Would it be possible to leave earlier?

target: Deberíamos probar la comida callejera.
english: We should try the street food.

target: Deberías probar el vino de la región.
english: You should try the wine from the region.

target: Deberías llevar zapatos cómodos para la excursión.
english: You should wear comfortable shoes for the excursion.

target: Si hubiéramos reservado antes, habría sido más barato.
english: If we had booked earlier, it would have been cheaper.
grammar: Conditional III (past unreal): si + pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect. Expresses a hypothetical past that didn't happen.

target: Habría que tener en cuenta la diferencia horaria.
english: One should take the time difference into account.

target: Si hubiéramos tomado el avión de las seis, ya estaríamos allí.
english: If we had taken the six o'clock plane, we would already be there.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past unreal condition (pluperfect subjunctive) + present unreal result (conditional). "Already would be" refers to now.

target: ¿Podrías ayudarme con este informe?
english: Could you help me with this report?
grammar: Conditional for polite requests: podrías = could you.

target: Me gustaría saber cuándo anunciarán los resultados.
english: I'd like to know when they'll announce the results.

target: Deberías negociar un mejor horario.
english: You should negotiate a better schedule.

target: Si no fuera por la burocracia, todo iría más rápido.
english: If it weren't for the bureaucracy, everything would go faster.

target: Si la abuela pudiera viajar, vendría a visitarnos.
english: If grandma could travel, she'd come visit us.

target: Según él, el proyecto estaría listo para el viernes.
english: According to him, the project would be ready by Friday.

target: Nos dijeron que la cena estaría lista en media hora.
english: They told us dinner would be ready in half an hour.

target: Habría unos doscientos invitados en la ceremonia.
english: There must have been about two hundred guests at the ceremony.
grammar: Conditional for conjecture about the past.

target: De haberlo pensado mejor, no habría firmado el contrato.
english: Had I thought it over more carefully, I wouldn't have signed the contract.

target: No pierdas la cabeza por una tontería así.
english: Don't lose your head over something so silly.

target: Yo comería sushi esta noche.
english: I would eat sushi tonight.

target: ¿Te gustaría ir al cine?
english: Would you like to go to the movies?

target: Me encantaría vivir en la playa.
english: I would love to live at the beach.

target: Él diría que es imposible.
english: He would say it's impossible.

target: La directora anuncio que habria cambios importantes en la empresa.
english: The director announced there would be important changes in the company.

target: Habria que reconsiderar la estrategia antes de invertir mas recursos.
english: The strategy should be reconsidered before investing more resources.

target: Me desperté a medianoche porque oí un ruido fuerte.
english: I woke up at midnight because I heard a loud noise.

target: Nací el tres de octubre de mil novecientos noventa.
english: I was born on the third of October, nineteen ninety.

target: El vuelo dura dos horas y cuarenta y cinco minutos.
english: The flight lasts two hours and forty-five minutes.

target: El maratón se corre en aproximadamente cuarenta y dos kilómetros.
english: A marathon is run over approximately forty-two kilometers.

target: Calculo que necesitaremos entre sesenta y setenta kilos de material.
english: I estimate we'll need between sixty and seventy kilos of material.
grammar: "desde...hasta" = from...to/until. Works for time, places, and quantities.

target: Mi vecina es muy amable y siempre me saluda.
english: My neighbor is very kind and always greets me.
grammar: "tener" for possession/features, not "haber." "La casa tiene..." NOT *"La casa hay..."

target: Tiene un carácter fuerte pero un corazón muy grande.
english: He has a strong character but a very big heart.

target: Mi tía es la persona más cariñosa que conozco.
english: My aunt is the most affectionate person I know.
grammar: "criar" = to raise. Imperfect "trabajaban" for the ongoing background situation that explains why.

target: Las paredes del salón están pintadas de azul claro.
english: The living room walls are painted light blue.

target: La puerta del armario chirría cada vez que la abro.
english: The closet door creaks every time I open it.
grammar: "perderse" (reflexive) = to get lost. "Me he perdido" = present perfect (I've gotten lost). "¿Puede ayudarme?" — pronoun can attach to infinitive.

target: Plantamos un limonero en el jardín y ya da frutos.
english: We planted a lemon tree in the garden and it already bears fruit.

target: Tengo hambre, vamos a comer algo.
english: I'm hungry, let's eat something.
grammar: "ir a" + infinitive = going to (near future). Used constantly in restaurants. "Van" is ustedes form, polite for customers.

target: El menú del día incluye primer plato, segundo y postre.
english: The daily menu includes a first course, second course, and dessert.

target: Prefiero ir en carro compartido porque es más barato y más sostenible.
english: I prefer carpooling because it's cheaper and more sustainable.
grammar: "ir a" + infinitive for near future plans. "Intentar" + infinitive = to try to. No preposition needed.

target: ¿Tiene esta falda en otra talla?
english: Do you have this skirt in another size?
grammar: imperfect "era/vivía" for past descriptions and ongoing states. NOT completed events. Sets the scene.

target: En el centro comercial hay tiendas de todo tipo.
english: The shopping mall has all kinds of stores.

target: ¿Me puede cambiar un billete de cincuenta en monedas?
english: Can you break a fifty into coins for me?
grammar: imperfect (estaba durmiendo) for the ongoing action, preterite (sonó) for the interruption. Classic contrast.

target: La tarjeta no me funciona, ¿puedo pagar en efectivo?
english: My card isn't working, can I pay in cash?
grammar: past perfect (pluperfect) = había + past participle. For actions before another past action: "I had never seen anything like that."

target: ¿Tienen garantía estos electrodomésticos?
english: Do these appliances come with a warranty?
grammar: "de pequeño" = as a child. "Solía" (imperfect of soler) + infinitive = used to. For past habits no longer true.

target: Prefiero comprar en tiendas pequeñas porque el trato es más personal.
english: I prefer shopping at small stores because the service is more personal.
grammar: present perfect for life experiences: "Have you ever been?" "Alguna vez" = ever (in questions). "Nunca" = never.

target: Pedí un préstamo al banco para comprar un carro nuevo.
english: I applied for a loan from the bank to buy a new car.
grammar: imperfect "hacía" for ongoing weather in past description. "Aquel" = that (distant/nostalgic) vs "ese" (neutral that).

target: Comparamos las condiciones de tres bancos diferentes antes de elegir la hipoteca.
english: We compared the conditions of three different banks before choosing the mortgage.
grammar: present perfect "he terminado" for incomplete action still relevant. Preterite "empecé" for when it began.

target: El cielo está nublado y parece que va a llover.
english: The sky is cloudy and it looks like it's going to rain.

target: El pronóstico dice que mañana hará buen tiempo.
english: The forecast says tomorrow the weather will be good.

target: En el parque nacional hay ciervos, águilas y osos.
english: In the national park there are deer, eagles, and bears.

target: El jardín botánico tiene una colección de plantas tropicales.
english: The botanical garden has a collection of tropical plants.

target: Vamos a pedir un aumento de sueldo.
english: Let's ask for a raise.

target: Hay que ponerse protector solar para proteger la piel.
english: You need to put on sunscreen to protect your skin.
grammar: pluperfect "había salido" for the earlier action. "Cuando llegamos" (preterite) + "ya había" (already had).

target: El tratamiento dura seis meses e incluye sesiones de rehabilitación.
english: The treatment lasts six months and includes rehabilitation sessions.

target: Después de la operación, el paciente deberá guardar reposo absoluto.
english: After the operation, the patient must remain on complete bed rest.

target: Me entristece ver a los animales en el zoológico.
english: It saddens me to see the animals in the zoo.
grammar: "llevar" + time + "con" = to have had for. "Dolor de" + body part: dolor de cabeza, dolor de espalda, dolor de muelas.

target: Voy a estudiar mañana.
english: I am going to study tomorrow.
grammar: Future tense = full infinitive + endings: -é, -ás, -á, -emos, -án. "Viajaré" = I will travel. Same endings for all verb types (-ar, -er, -ir).

target: Ella va a viajar a México el próximo mes.
english: She is going to travel to Mexico next month.

target: Vamos a cenar juntos esta noche.
english: We are going to have dinner together tonight.

target: ¿Vendrás a la fiesta del sábado?
english: Will you come to Saturday's party?

target: No podremos terminar el proyecto a tiempo.
english: We will not be able to finish the project on time.
grammar: "hacer" has irregular future stem: har- (not *hacer-). Other irregular stems: tendr-, pondr-, saldr-, vendr-, sabr-, podr-, querr-.

target: Algún día viviré cerca del mar.
english: Someday I will live near the sea.

target: Planeo ahorrar dinero para comprar una casa.
english: I plan to save money to buy a house.
grammar: future perfect: habré + past participle = will have done. "Para cuando" + subjunctive = by the time.

target: El año que viene empezaré a aprender francés.
english: Next year I will start learning French.

target: Tengo la intención de correr un maratón este año.
english: I intend to run a marathon this year.
grammar: "tener" irregular future: tendré. "Pensarlo" — lo replaces the whole idea/proposal. Pronoun attaches to infinitive.

target: Hasta que no encuentre algo mejor, seguiré en este puesto.
english: Until I find something better, I will stay in this position.
grammar: "dentro de" + time = in (future time). "Habremos terminado" = we will have finished (future perfect).

target: De aquí a cinco años, espero tener mi propia empresa.
english: Five years from now, I hope to have my own company.

target: Tarde o temprano, encontrarás la respuesta que buscas.
english: Sooner or later, you will find the answer you are looking for.
grammar: future for predictions. "Probablemente" can also take subjunctive for uncertainty: "probablemente llueva."

target: Si estudias cada día, aprobarás el examen sin dificultad.
english: If you study every day, you will pass the exam without difficulty.

target: Los científicos advierten que el nivel del mar seguirá subiendo.
english: Scientists warn that sea levels will continue to rise.

target: ¿Puedo llamarte más tarde? Estoy ocupado ahora.
english: Can I call you later? I'm busy right now.
grammar: present perfect "he llamado" for recent actions with current relevance. "Varias veces" = several times.

target: Es importante verificar las fuentes antes de compartir una noticia.
english: It is important to verify sources before sharing a news story.

target: Hice una copia de seguridad de todos mis archivos en la nube.
english: I made a backup of all my files in the cloud.
grammar: passive present perfect: "ha sido enviado" = has been sent. "Por" for the means/method.

target: Le comunicaremos nuestra decisión en un plazo de dos semanas.
english: We will communicate our decision to you within two weeks.

target: Los criterios de selección incluyen formación académica, experiencia y competencias.
english: The selection criteria include academic training, experience, and competencies.

target: Mi pasatiempo favorito es ver documentales sobre ciencia y naturaleza.
english: My favorite hobby is watching documentaries about science and nature.

target: Sea cual sea tu decisión, te apoyaremos sin condiciones.
english: Whatever your decision may be, we will support you unconditionally.
grammar: gerund "siendo" = being (honesty marker). "Del todo" = completely. "Estar convencido" — estar for state.

target: El destino no es cuestión de suerte, sino de decisión.
english: Destiny is not a matter of luck, but of decision.

target: Cuando lleguemos al aeropuerto, te enviaré un mensaje.
english: When we arrive at the airport, I'll send you a message.
grammar: "Cuando" + subjunctive for future events.

target: Voy a preparar la presentación.
english: I'm going to prepare the presentation.

target: El sueldo base es de dos mil euros al mes.
english: The base salary is two thousand euros per month.

target: Mi abuela me contaba cuentos antes de dormir.
english: My grandmother used to tell me stories before bed.
grammar: Imperfect tense "contaba" — habitual past action.

target: Sea cual sea tu decisión, te apoyaré.
english: Whatever your decision may be, I'll support you.
grammar: "Sea cual sea" — whatever (it) may be.

target: Haré lo que sea necesario.
english: I'll do whatever is necessary.

target: Tengo la sensación de que algo va a pasar.
english: I have a feeling something is going to happen.

target: A mediados de julio, iré a la costa.
english: In mid-July, I'll go to the coast.

target: Los mercados locales son el alma de una ciudad.
english: Local markets are the soul of a city.

target: Llovía mucho cuando llegamos.
english: It was raining a lot when we arrived.
grammar: Imperfect for past weather: "Llovía mucho." Weather descriptions in the past use imperfect — they set the scene.

target: Eran las tres de la mañana cuando llamó.
english: It was three in the morning when he called.
grammar: Imperfect for past time: "Eran las tres." Telling time in the past always uses imperfect.

target: Vamos a caminar por el parque.
english: Let's walk through the park.
grammar: "Por" = through/along: "Caminamos por el parque." Por for movement through or past a place.

target: Mañana te llamaré por la tarde.
english: I'll call you tomorrow afternoon.

target: Creo que lloverá este fin de semana.
english: I think it'll rain this weekend.

target: ¿Quién será a esta hora?
english: Who could that be at this hour?
grammar: Future for speculation: "¿Dónde estará?" (Where could he be?). Spanish uses future tense to guess about the present.

target: El próximo año estudiaré un máster.
english: Next year I'll study for a master's degree.

target: Serán las tres de la tarde.
english: It must be about three in the afternoon.
grammar: Future for conjecture: "Serán las tres" (It must be about 3). Spanish uses future tense to express guesses about the present.

target: El mes que viene nos mudaremos.
english: Next month we'll move.

target: Estará en una reunión, por eso no contesta.
english: He's probably in a meeting, that's why he's not answering.
grammar: Future for probability: "Tendrá unos 30 años" (He must be about 30). The future tense can express guesses about now.

target: Este verano iré a visitar a mis abuelos.
english: This summer I'll go visit my grandparents.

target: Supongo que tendrá unos cuarenta años.
english: I guess he's about forty years old.
grammar: Future for guessing age: "Tendrá unos 40 años" (He must be about 40). Future tense = speculation about the present.

target: ¿Vendrás a la cena del viernes?
english: Will you come to Friday's dinner?

target: ¿Habrá sitio en el restaurante?
english: Will there be room at the restaurant?
grammar: Future of haber for speculation

target: Seguro que ya lo habrás oído.
english: I'm sure you'll have heard about it already.
grammar: Future perfect for assumption: "Ya habrá llegado" (He must have arrived by now). Future perfect = guessing about the recent past.

target: Los colegas con quienes almuerzo son de otro departamento.
english: The colleagues I have lunch with are from another department.
grammar: "Con quienes" = with whom (plural): preposition + quienes for people in formal relative clauses.

target: La sopa está fría, la voy a calentar.
english: The soup is cold, I'm going to heat it up.

target: Ella iba al gimnasio tres veces por semana.
english: She used to go to the gym three times a week.
grammar: Ir (imperfect): iba, ibas, iba, íbamos — irregular

target: Mañana empezaré a buscar piso.
english: Tomorrow I'll start looking for an apartment.
grammar: Simple future: empezaré (empezar) — future formed with infinitive + -é

target: Este verano viajaremos a Portugal.
english: This summer we'll travel to Portugal.

target: Te enviaré el documento esta tarde.
english: I'll send you the document this afternoon.

target: Nos veremos la semana que viene.
english: We'll see each other next week.

target: Mañana sabremos los resultados del examen.
english: Tomorrow we'll know the exam results.

target: El proyecto nuevo es tan importante como el anterior.
english: The new project is as important as the previous one.

target: Mañana visitaremos el casco antiguo.
english: Tomorrow we'll visit the old town.

target: Pasaremos tres noches en Barcelona.
english: We'll spend three nights in Barcelona.

target: Si el tiempo mejora, alquilaremos una bicicleta.
english: If the weather improves, we'll rent a bicycle.

target: ¿Necesitaremos visa para entrar en ese país?
english: Will we need a visa to enter that country?

target: Si no encuentro hotel, dormiré en un albergue.
english: If I can't find a hotel, I'll sleep in a hostel.

target: Firmaré el contrato la próxima semana.
english: I'll sign the contract next week.

target: El departamento crecerá el próximo año.
english: The department will grow next year.

target: Entregaremos el proyecto a final de mes.
english: We'll deliver the project at the end of the month.

target: Si termino pronto, ayudaré con tu parte.
english: If I finish early, I'll help with your part.

target: ¿Le dirás a papá lo que pasó?
english: Will you tell dad what happened?

target: La semana que viene celebraremos el aniversario.
english: Next week we'll celebrate the anniversary.

target: ¿Necesitaremos una canguro para el sábado?
english: Will we need a babysitter for Saturday?

target: La presentación que preparé duró veinte minutos.
english: The presentation I prepared lasted twenty minutes.

target: Se oyen risas desde la habitación de los niños.
english: You can hear laughter from the kids' room.

target: El hijo juega con las pelotas en el parque.
english: The son plays with balls in the park.

target: Preparé un pastel para el aniversario.
english: I made a cake for the anniversary.

target: Les preparé la cena a los niños.
english: I made dinner for the kids.

target: Reservaré el hotel esta noche.
english: I will book the hotel tonight.

target: El guía nos recogerá en la puerta del hotel.
english: The guide will pick us up at the hotel door.

target: Saldremos temprano para evitar el tráfico.
english: We will leave early to avoid traffic.
grammar: "Saldremos" — irregular future of "salir": saldré, saldrás, saldrá, saldremos, saldréis, saldrán.

target: Habrá tiempo libre por la tarde.
english: There will be free time in the afternoon.
grammar: "Habrá" is the future of "haber" (there is/are). Irregular: habrá, not haberá.

target: Tendremos que madrugar para tomar el vuelo.
english: We will have to get up early to catch the flight.
grammar: "Tendremos" — irregular future of "tener": tendré, tendrás, tendrá, tendremos, tendréis, tendrán.

target: Tanto si llueve como si hace sol, iremos de excursión.
english: Whether it rains or shines, we'll go on the excursion.

target: A no ser que cancelemos ahora, perderemos el depósito.
english: Unless we cancel now, we'll lose the deposit.

target: Si tengo tiempo, terminaré el informe hoy.
english: If I have time, I'll finish the report today.

target: Lo haré en cuanto tenga un momento.
english: I'll do it as soon as I have a moment.
grammar: En cuanto + subjunctive for future events: tenga (not tengo).

target: Cuando termine mi contrato, me iré al extranjero.
english: When my contract ends, I'll go abroad.

target: Leeré tu propuesta esta tarde.
english: I'll read your proposal this afternoon.

target: A no ser que cambien las condiciones, no firmaré.
english: Unless the conditions change, I won't sign.

target: Si redujéramos los plazos de entrega, seríamos más competitivos.
english: If we reduced delivery times, we would be more competitive.

target: Mi hermano y yo nos peleábamos de pequeños.
english: My brother and I used to fight as kids.
grammar: Imperfect for habitual past: nos peleábamos = we used to fight

target: Cuando los niños crezcan, entenderán.
english: When the kids grow up, they'll understand.
grammar: Cuando + subjunctive for future events: crezcan (not crecen)

target: Si no llueve, comeremos en el jardín.
english: If it doesn't rain, we'll eat in the garden.

target: Habrá que comprar una cama más grande para el niño.
english: We'll need to buy a bigger bed for the boy.
grammar: Habrá que + infinitive = it will be necessary to (impersonal future)

target: Si mi hermana viene, prepararé su plato favorito.
english: If my sister comes, I'll make her favorite dish.

target: Sacaré al perro cuando pare de llover.
english: I'll take the dog out when it stops raining.

target: Cuando termines los deberes, podrás jugar.
english: When you finish your homework, you can play.

target: Cuando seas mayor, entenderás por qué lo hacemos.
english: When you're older, you'll understand why we do it.
grammar: Ser in subjunctive: seas (when talking about future states)

target: Pase lo que pase, siempre seré tu madre.
english: Whatever happens, I'll always be your mother.

target: A condición de que todos colaboren, la mudanza será fácil.
english: Provided everyone helps out, the move will be easy.
grammar: A condición de que + subjunctive = on the condition that

target: Sea cual sea la decisión, la tomaremos en familia.
english: Whatever the decision, we'll make it as a family.

target: Lejos de ser perfecta, nuestra familia tiene sus propios encantos.
english: Far from being perfect, our family has its own charm.

target: De haber tenido tiempo, habría ido a visitarte.
english: Had I had time, I would have gone to visit you.
grammar: De haber + participle is an alternative to si hubiera + participle.

target: Se comenta que van a abrir un nuevo restaurante.
english: It's being said they're going to open a new restaurant.

target: Tanto mi hermano como yo somos alérgicos al polen.
english: Both my brother and I are allergic to pollen.

target: Después de todo, lo más importante es la salud.
english: After all, the most important thing is health.

target: Cuanto antes lo terminemos, mejor será para todos.
english: The sooner we finish it, the better it will be for everyone.

target: Con tal de que seas feliz, yo estaré bien.
english: As long as you're happy, I'll be fine.

target: Volveremos a intentarlo mañana con más calma.
english: We'll try again tomorrow more calmly.

target: Seguiremos luchando hasta conseguir nuestro objetivo.
english: We'll keep fighting until we achieve our goal.

target: Cuantas más personas participen, más enriquecedor será el debate.
english: The more people participate, the more enriching the debate will be.

target: Nosotros viajaremos a Italia en julio.
english: We will travel to Italy in July.

target: Nosotros haremos una fiesta el sábado.
english: We'll have a party on Saturday.

target: El nuevo café abrirá pronto.
english: The new café will open soon.

target: Ella vivirá cerca del trabajo.
english: She will live near work.

target: Ella aprenderá a conducir este verano.
english: She'll learn to drive this summer.

target: Aunque trabaje horas extra, no voy a poder terminarlo hoy.
english: Even if I work overtime, I won't be able to finish it today.

target: Estamos a punto de perder el tren, date prisa.
english: We're about to miss the train, hurry up.

target: De no haber sido por tu ayuda, no lo habria conseguido.
english: Had it not been for your help, I wouldn't have made it.
grammar: De no haber + participle: formal alternative to si no hubiera.

target: Habra cumplido sesenta anos ya, si no me equivoco.
english: He must have turned sixty by now, if I'm not mistaken.

target: De tal palo, tal astilla; el hijo es identico al padre.
english: Like father, like son — the son is identical to the father.
grammar: De tal palo, tal astilla = the apple doesn't fall far from the tree.

target: Analizado el problema, el equipo propuso tres soluciones posibles.
english: Having analyzed the problem, the team proposed three possible solutions.

target: Cuanto antes entreguemos el prototipo, antes recibiremos la financiacion.
english: The sooner we deliver the prototype, the sooner we'll receive the funding.

target: Antes de salir compruebo que todas las ventanas están cerradas.
english: Before leaving I check that all the windows are closed.

target: El norte de España es más verde que el sur.
english: The north of Spain is greener than the south.

target: Necesito una tabla de cortar, un cuchillo afilado y una olla grande.
english: I need a cutting board, a sharp knife, and a large pot.
grammar: "¿cuánto es?" for the price/bill. "En total" = in total. Also: "¿cuánto cuesta?" (how much does it cost?).

target: Ella está muy enfadada conmigo por lo que pasó.
english: She is very angry with me about what happened.
grammar: formal "le" (usted). Doctor speaking: "¿le duele?" = does it hurt you? "Aquí" = here. Doler with formal address.

target: Me siento avergonzado por lo que dije ayer.
english: I feel embarrassed about what I said yesterday.

target: Me asusta pensar en lo que podría pasar.
english: It scares me to think about what could happen.

target: Estoy decepcionado de que no hayas cumplido tu promesa.
english: I am disappointed that you didn't keep your promise.

target: Es difícil expresar lo que siento con palabras.
english: It is difficult to express what I feel with words.

target: Quiero aclarar lo que dije antes para evitar confusiones.
english: I want to clarify what I said before to avoid confusion.

target: La gentrificación desplaza a los residentes de bajos ingresos de sus barrios tradicionales.
english: Gentrification displaces low-income residents from their traditional neighborhoods.
grammar: "cuanto más...más" = the more...the more. Correlative comparative construction.

target: Necesito cambiar dinero. ¿Dónde hay una casa de cambio?
english: I need to exchange money. Where is there a currency exchange?

target: El autor, cuya obra fue premiada, dará una conferencia mañana.
english: The author, whose work was awarded, will give a lecture tomorrow.

target: La gratitud transforma lo que tenemos en suficiente.
english: Gratitude transforms what we have into enough.

target: No importa lo lejos que estemos, el vínculo familiar permanece.
english: No matter how far we are, the family bond remains.

target: Aprender un idioma es mucho más que memorizar palabras.
english: Learning a language is much more than memorizing words.

target: Es urgente que tomemos medidas contra el cambio climático.
english: It's urgent that we take measures against climate change.

target: La realidad es peor de lo que imaginaba.
english: Reality is worse than what I imagined.

target: No dejes para mañana lo que puedas hacer hoy.
english: Don't leave for tomorrow what you can do today.

target: Agua que no has de beber, déjala correr.
english: If you can't use it, let it go.

target: No hay peor ciego que el que no quiere ver.
english: There's none so blind as those who won't see.

target: La persona que llamó no dejó su nombre.
english: The person who called didn't leave their name.

target: El amigo con quien viajé es muy divertido.
english: The friend I traveled with is very fun.

target: El profesor, cuya clase es difícil, es muy bueno.
english: The teacher, whose class is hard, is very good.
grammar: "Cuyo/a" = whose: a relative possessive that agrees with the possessed noun. "El autor cuyo libro leí" = The author whose book I read.

target: El país del que vengo es muy bonito.
english: The country I come from is very beautiful.

target: Las personas con las que trabajo son geniales.
english: The people I work with are great.

target: La chica que trabaja aquí es mi prima.
english: The girl who works here is my cousin.
grammar: "Que" as relative pronoun: "La chica que vino" = The girl who came. "Que" works for both people and things in Spanish.

target: La persona con quien hablé me dio buena información.
english: The person I spoke with gave me good information.
grammar: "Con quien" = with whom: "La persona con quien hablé." Preposition + quien for people.

target: El cliente con quien tengo la reunión llega a las tres.
english: The client I have the meeting with arrives at three.
grammar: "Con quien" = with whom: "La persona con quien trabajo." Use preposition + quien when referring to people.

target: La razón por la que llegué tarde fue el tráfico.
english: The reason I arrived late was the traffic.
grammar: "Por la que" = for which: "La razón por la que vine" = The reason for which I came.

target: La mujer cuyo hijo estudia conmigo es abogada.
english: The woman whose son studies with me is a lawyer.
grammar: "Cuyo/a/os/as" = whose. Agrees with the POSSESSED noun, not the owner: "El hombre cuya hija..." (whose daughter).

target: El parque donde corremos está muy bien cuidado.
english: The park where we run is very well maintained.

target: El proyecto en el que estoy trabajando es complicado.
english: The project I'm working on is complicated.
grammar: "En el que" = in which: "La casa en la que vivo" = The house in which I live. More specific than just "que."

target: Lo que me preocupa es que no tenemos suficiente tiempo.
english: What worries me is that we don't have enough time.

target: El tema del que hablamos ayer es importante.
english: The topic we talked about yesterday is important.
grammar: "Del que" = of/about which: "El tema del que hablamos." Contraction of de + el + que.

target: Lo que pasó ayer no puede volver a pasar.
english: What happened yesterday cannot happen again.

target: Así que al final decidimos quedarnos en casa.
english: So in the end we decided to stay home.

target: Me hizo gracia lo que dijo tu hermano.
english: What your brother said was funny to me.

target: ¿De quién es este abrigo?
english: Whose coat is this?
grammar: ¿De quién...? — Whose...? Asks about possession

target: Lo que me molesta es que nadie avisó.
english: What bothers me is that nobody gave notice.
grammar: "Lo que" = what / the thing that: "Lo que dices es verdad" = What you say is true. For abstract or unspecified things.

target: Es algo de lo que no me gusta hablar.
english: It's something I don't like to talk about.
grammar: "De lo que" = about which (abstract): "De lo que hablamos" = What we talked about. Neuter lo for ideas, not physical objects.

target: El problema al que nos enfrentamos es el presupuesto.
english: The problem we're facing is the budget.
grammar: "Al que" = to whom/which: "El amigo al que escribí" = The friend to whom I wrote.

target: La empresa para la que trabajo tiene sede en Barcelona.
english: The company I work for has its headquarters in Barcelona.

target: El profesor cuyas clases más me gustaban se jubiló.
english: The teacher whose classes I liked the most retired.
grammar: "Cuyas" = whose (f. pl.): Cuyo agrees in gender/number with the noun it precedes, not the owner.

target: El motivo por el que te llamo es importante.
english: The reason I'm calling you is important.

target: El barrio donde vive mi abuela es muy tranquilo.
english: The neighborhood where my grandmother lives is very quiet.

target: El día en que nos mudamos llovía a cántaros.
english: The day we moved it was pouring rain.
grammar: "En que" for time: "El año en que nací" = The year in which I was born. Used for temporal relative clauses.

target: Lo que me gusta de vivir aquí es la tranquilidad.
english: What I like about living here is the peacefulness.

target: El país del que vengo tiene una cultura muy rica.
english: The country I come from has a very rich culture.

target: La mesa en la que siempre nos sentamos está ocupada.
english: The table where we always sit is taken.

target: El equipo con el que trabajo es muy profesional.
english: The team I work with is very professional.

target: Lo que haces por tu familia no tiene precio.
english: What you do for your family is priceless.

target: El apartamento cuyo dueño es mi tío está libre.
english: The apartment whose owner is my uncle is available.

target: Lo que cocina mi abuela no lo supera nadie.
english: Nobody beats what my grandmother cooks.

target: Digan lo que digan, voy a seguir adelante.
english: Whatever they say, I'm going to keep going.

target: El mercado central es más auténtico que las tiendas turísticas.
english: The central market is more authentic than the tourist shops.

target: El equipaje de mano es más práctico que facturar maletas.
english: Carry-on luggage is more practical than checking bags.

target: La entrevista fue más difícil de lo que esperaba.
english: The interview was harder than I expected.

target: El pueblo al que llegamos estaba en la montaña.
english: The village we arrived at was in the mountains.

target: La carretera por la que fuimos tenía curvas peligrosas.
english: The road we drove on had dangerous curves.

target: La torre desde la que sacamos fotos era altísima.
english: The tower we took photos from was very tall.

target: El cliente al que llamé quiere cambiar el pedido.
english: The client I called wants to change the order.

target: El contrato que firmamos tiene tres años de duración.
english: The contract we signed lasts three years.

target: La compañera con la que almuerzo es de contabilidad.
english: The coworker I have lunch with is from accounting.

target: La empresa para la que trabajaba antes era más pequeña.
english: The company I used to work for was smaller.
