target: El proveedor con el que negociamos ofrece buen precio.
english: The supplier we negotiate with offers a good price.

target: El parque al que llevamos a los niños tiene columpios.
english: The park we take the kids to has swings.

target: Los primos con los que jugábamos viven lejos.
english: The cousins we used to play with live far away.

target: Le conté a mi hermano lo que pasó.
english: I told my brother what happened.

target: El hotel donde nos quedamos era increíble.
english: The hotel where we stayed was incredible.
grammar: "Donde" introduces a relative clause of place. "El hotel donde..." = the hotel where...

target: El restaurante al que fuimos tenía buenas vistas.
english: The restaurant we went to had good views.
grammar: "Al que" = to which. When the relative pronoun is preceded by a preposition: a + el que = al que.

target: La ciudad en la que vivimos un mes es preciosa.
english: The city where we lived for a month is beautiful.

target: La isla a la que queremos ir está muy lejos.
english: The island we want to go to is very far.

target: No pensé que el viaje fuera a ser tan agotador.
english: I didn't think the trip was going to be so exhausting.

target: Cada vez que vuelvo a ese lugar, descubro algo nuevo.
english: Every time I go back to that place, I discover something new.

target: La empresa donde trabajo tiene buen ambiente.
english: The company where I work has a good atmosphere.
grammar: Relative clause: donde = where.

target: Le conté a mi madre lo que pasó.
english: I told my mother what happened.

target: La casa en la que vivíamos era muy vieja.
english: The house we used to live in was very old.

target: El perro al que rescatamos es muy cariñoso.
english: The dog we rescued is very affectionate.

target: Es una lástima que no puedas venir a la comunión.
english: It's a shame you can't come to the communion.

target: El parque donde juegan los niños está muy cuidado.
english: The park where the kids play is well maintained.

target: Siendo madre, entiendo mejor lo que pasó mi madre.
english: Being a mother, I understand better what my mother went through.

target: El problema al que nos enfrentamos es muy complejo.
english: The problem we face is very complex.
grammar: Al que = a + el + que, preposition + relative pronoun.

target: El trabajo por el que tanto luché finalmente dio frutos.
english: The work I fought so hard for finally bore fruit.

target: El país al que quiero viajar es Japón.
english: The country I want to travel to is Japan.

target: Las razones por las que renunció no están claras.
english: The reasons he resigned aren't clear.

target: La persona de la que te hablé viene mañana.
english: The person I told you about is coming tomorrow.

target: El equipo con el que trabajo es excelente.
english: The team I work with is excellent.

target: El barco en el que viajamos era enorme.
english: The boat we traveled on was enormous.

target: Lo que quiero saber es si podemos contar contigo.
english: What I want to know is if we can count on you.

target: El curso al que me apunté empieza el lunes.
english: The course I signed up for starts on Monday.

target: Las calles por las que caminamos eran muy estrechas.
english: The streets we walked through were very narrow.

target: El aeropuerto desde el que salimos estaba muy lejos.
english: The airport we departed from was very far away.

target: Dudo mucho que lleguen a un acuerdo pronto.
english: I very much doubt they'll reach an agreement soon.

target: Vuelvo a repetir: es muy importante que lleguen a tiempo.
english: I repeat again: it's very important that they arrive on time.

target: No me tomes el pelo; sé que no es verdad.
english: Don't pull my leg — I know it's not true.

target: El amigo con quien hablé me ayudó.
english: The friend I spoke with helped me.

target: Necesitamos a alguien que domine las hojas de calculo.
english: We need someone who is proficient in spreadsheets.

target: No niego que haya habido errores, pero estamos mejorando.
english: I don't deny there have been mistakes, but we're improving.

target: Hagan lo que hagan, no borren los archivos del servidor.
english: Whatever you do, don't delete the files from the server.

target: No tiene ni pies ni cabeza lo que estas diciendo.
english: What you're saying makes no sense at all.
grammar: No tener ni pies ni cabeza = to make no sense (literally: no feet or head).

target: Ha sido un placer hablar contigo.
english: It's been a pleasure talking to you.
grammar: "Ha sido" = present perfect of ser (has been). Formed with haber + past participle. "Contigo" is a special form meaning "with you" (con + ti → contigo).

target: Esta semana he cambiado mi rutina para hacer ejercicio por la mañana.
english: This week I've changed my routine to exercise in the morning.
grammar: Present perfect = "haber" + past participle: he cambiado, has cambiado, ha cambiado. Used for recent past or past with present relevance.

target: Este mes me he propuesto dejar de ver la televisión antes de dormir.
english: This month I've decided to stop watching television before bed.
grammar: "Me he propuesto" — reflexive present perfect. "Proponerse" = to set out to / decide to. Irregular participle: propuesto (not *proponido).

target: Es mediodía y todavía no he comido.
english: It's noon and I still haven't eaten.

target: Mi compañera de trabajo se ha convertido en una buena amiga.
english: My coworker has become a good friend.
grammar: "contar" (o→ue) = to tell/count. Imperfect "era" for describing what things were like in the past.

target: El jabón se ha acabado, ¿puedes poner uno nuevo?
english: The soap has run out, can you put a new one?

target: He puesto plantas en el balcón para darle más vida.
english: I've put plants on the balcony to give it more life.
grammar: "conocer" = to know/be familiar with (places, people). vs "saber" = to know (facts, how to). "No conozco Madrid" vs "No sé dónde está."

target: Hemos cambiado el fregadero viejo por uno de acero inoxidable.
english: We've replaced the old sink with a stainless steel one.

target: Hemos instalado paneles solares en el tejado para ahorrar energía.
english: We've installed solar panels on the roof to save energy.

target: La mantequilla se ha derretido con el calor.
english: The butter has melted in the heat.

target: El carro no arranca porque la batería se ha agotado.
english: The car won't start because the battery is dead.
grammar: "preferir" (e→ie) + infinitive. "Quedarme" — reflexive pronoun attaches to infinitive: quedarme, quedarte, quedarse.

target: El recibo dice que me han cobrado de más.
english: The receipt says I've been overcharged.

target: Este mes he gastado demasiado y tengo que ahorrar.
english: This month I've spent too much and I need to save.

target: El comercio electrónico ha cambiado la forma en que compramos casi todo.
english: E-commerce has changed the way we buy almost everything.
grammar: "llevaba" + time + gerund = had been doing for. Imperfect "llevaba" for the ongoing situation before "decidí."

target: La herida en el brazo todavía no ha cicatrizado.
english: The wound on my arm still hasn't healed.

target: Apenas había amanecido cuando nos pusimos en camino.
english: It had barely dawned when we set out.

target: En diez años, esta ciudad habrá cambiado completamente.
english: In ten years, this city will have changed completely.

target: ¿Habrás aprendido suficiente español para el viaje?
english: Will you have learned enough Spanish for the trip?
grammar: "cuando" + subjunctive for future events that haven't happened yet. "Cuando sea" (not *cuando es) because it's about the future.

target: Hemos decidido invertir en energías renovables para el futuro.
english: We have decided to invest in renewable energy for the future.

target: Todavía no he decidido si aceptaré la oferta de trabajo.
english: I still haven't decided whether I will accept the job offer.

target: ¿Has visto las noticias de esta mañana?
english: Have you seen this morning's news?

target: La videoconferencia ha facilitado el trabajo a distancia.
english: Video conferencing has made remote work easier.

target: Me complace informarle que su solicitud ha sido aprobada.
english: I am pleased to inform you that your application has been approved.

target: La tasa de desempleo ha bajado por tercer mes consecutivo.
english: The unemployment rate has dropped for the third consecutive month.
grammar: present perfect for recent news. "Ha anunciado" = has announced. Common in news reporting.

target: La natalidad ha descendido significativamente en los países desarrollados.
english: The birth rate has dropped significantly in developed countries.
grammar: unreal conditional: si + imperfect subjunctive → conditional. Policy hypothetical.

target: Hemos firmado un acuerdo de colaboración con una empresa japonesa.
english: We have signed a collaboration agreement with a Japanese company.

target: El comercio electrónico ha crecido exponencialmente en los últimos años.
english: E-commerce has grown exponentially in recent years.

target: Me he apuntado a un curso de cocina italiana los miércoles por la tarde.
english: I've signed up for an Italian cooking course on Wednesday afternoons.
grammar: "mientras" + present for simultaneous habitual actions. "Unos...otros" = some...others.

target: ¿Has estado alguna vez en México?
english: Have you ever been to Mexico?
grammar: Present perfect with "alguna vez" — asking about life experiences.

target: Nunca he comido comida tailandesa.
english: I've never eaten Thai food.

target: Cuando llegué, ya se habían ido.
english: When I arrived, they had already left.
grammar: Pluperfect: "se habían ido" — they had left.

target: La tecnología ha revolucionado la forma en que nos comunicamos.
english: Technology has revolutionized the way we communicate.

target: Me han dado un plantón.
english: I was stood up.

target: El mercado laboral ha experimentado cambios sin precedentes.
english: The job market has experienced unprecedented changes.

target: ¿Has viajado al extranjero alguna vez?
english: Have you traveled abroad?

target: He comido demasiado.
english: I've eaten too much.
grammar: Present perfect: haber + past participle.

target: Nunca he visto nada igual.
english: I've never seen anything like it.

target: Nunca había comido paella antes.
english: I had never eaten paella before.

target: Para las seis, habré terminado.
english: By six, I will have finished.
grammar: Future perfect: habré + past participle.

target: ¿Habrás terminado para las cinco?
english: Will you have finished by five?
grammar: Future perfect for deadline: "Para junio habré terminado" (By June I'll have finished). Action completed before a future point.

target: He vivido aquí toda mi vida.
english: I've lived here my whole life.
grammar: Present perfect (pretérito perfecto) for life experience: "He viajado a Japón." Have you ever done X? Life experience up to now.

target: Nunca he estado en Sudamérica.
english: I've never been to South America.

target: Ya habíamos comido cuando llegaron.
english: We had already eaten when they arrived.
grammar: Past perfect (pluscuamperfecto): "Ya había comido cuando llegaste." An action completed BEFORE another past action.

target: ¿Has visto las noticias hoy?
english: Have you seen the news today?

target: Nunca había visto algo así.
english: I had never seen anything like that.
grammar: Past perfect: "Nunca había visto nieve." First-time experience from a past perspective — hadn't done X before that point.

target: Habré terminado el informe para el viernes.
english: I'll have finished the report by Friday.
grammar: Future perfect: "Para el viernes habré terminado." An action that will be complete before a future point.

target: ¿Has probado la nueva cafetería?
english: Have you tried the new café?

target: He pensado mucho en lo que dijiste.
english: I've thought a lot about what you said.

target: Ya había salido cuando me llamaste.
english: I had already left when you called me.
grammar: Past perfect: "Cuando llegué, ya se había ido." Action completed before another past event.

target: Hemos recibido muchas quejas esta semana.
english: We've received a lot of complaints this week.

target: Para entonces ya habré encontrado trabajo.
english: By then I'll have found a job.
grammar: Future perfect — anticipated completion

target: Nos habíamos perdido en el centro.
english: We had gotten lost in the city center.

target: Le he dicho mil veces que tenga cuidado.
english: I've told him a thousand times to be careful.

target: Todavía no hemos decidido adónde ir.
english: We still haven't decided where to go.

target: Ya habían cerrado cuando llegamos.
english: They had already closed when we arrived.
grammar: Past perfect: "Cuando llegamos, ya había empezado." The pluperfect shows one past action was already done before another.

target: He aprendido mucho en este trabajo.
english: I've learned a lot at this job.

target: Ya habíamos hablado de esto antes.
english: We had already talked about this before.

target: Se habrá olvidado de la reunión.
english: He must have forgotten about the meeting.
grammar: Future perfect for guessing about the past: "Se habrá perdido" (He must have gotten lost). Speculation about what already happened.

target: Para junio ya habremos acabado las obras.
english: By June we'll have finished the construction.
grammar: Future perfect for projecting completion: "Para entonces ya habré terminado." Expressing that something will be done by a future time.

target: Nunca había probado la comida tailandesa.
english: I had never tried Thai food.

target: He intentado llamarte varias veces.
english: I've tried calling you several times.

target: No sé qué habrá pasado.
english: I don't know what could have happened.
grammar: Future perfect for past speculation: "¿Qué habrá pasado?" (What could have happened?). Guessing about past events.

target: La ciudad donde crecí ha cambiado mucho.
english: The city where I grew up has changed a lot.

target: Se ha demostrado que dormir bien es importante.
english: It has been shown that sleeping well is important.

target: Estamos en las mismas; no ha cambiado nada.
english: We're in the same place — nothing has changed.

target: ¿Quién ha llamado esta mañana?
english: Who called this morning?

target: Por cierto, ¿has visto mi cargador?
english: By the way, have you seen my charger?
grammar: "Por cierto" = by the way. Introduces a related but tangential comment.

target: ¿Se lo has dicho ya?
english: Have you told him yet?

target: He perdido las llaves otra vez.
english: I've lost my keys again.
grammar: Present perfect: he perdido (perder) — haber + past participle

target: Nunca había visto una puesta de sol tan bonita.
english: I had never seen such a beautiful sunset.

target: Cuando llegué, ya se habían ido todos.
english: When I arrived, everyone had already left.
grammar: Past perfect: se habían ido — describes completed action before another past event

target: Ya había comido cuando me llamaste.
english: I had already eaten when you called me.

target: Todavía no he recibido tu correo.
english: I still haven't received your email.

target: Nunca había probado comida tan picante.
english: I had never tried food so spicy.

target: Ya habíamos cerrado cuando llegó el cliente.
english: We had already closed when the customer arrived.

target: Hemos decidido mudarnos al centro.
english: We've decided to move downtown.

target: ¿Habrá llegado ya el pedido?
english: Do you think the order has arrived yet?

target: He estado pensando en lo que me dijiste.
english: I've been thinking about what you told me.

target: Ya me había dado cuenta del error.
english: I had already noticed the error.

target: Ya habíamos hablado de eso antes.
english: We had already talked about that before.

target: Cuando llegamos al cine, la película ya había empezado.
english: When we got to the movies, the film had already started.

target: ¿Quién habrá dejado la puerta abierta?
english: Who could have left the door open?
grammar: Future perfect of probability: habrá dejado — guessing about a past event

target: No sé dónde estará, le he llamado y no contesta.
english: I don't know where he could be, I've called and he's not answering.

target: Habíamos quedado a las ocho, pero no vino.
english: We had agreed to meet at eight, but he didn't come.

target: Siempre he querido visitar Japón.
english: I've always wanted to visit Japan.

target: He comido demasiado, no puedo más.
english: I've eaten too much, I can't take any more.

target: La casa donde crecí la han derribado.
english: The house where I grew up, they've torn it down.

target: Si te hubieras presentado a la entrevista, te habrían contratado.
english: If you had shown up for the interview, they would have hired you.
grammar: "Si" + pluperfect subjunctive for unreal past: "Si hubiera sabido, habría ido." If I had known, I would have gone. The condition can never be fulfilled.

target: Se ha demostrado que esta técnica funciona.
english: It has been demonstrated that this technique works.
grammar: "Se ha demostrado" = It has been shown. Impersonal se works in all tenses: "Se ha dicho que..." = It has been said that...

target: Aunque parezca mentira, nunca he estado en París.
english: Believe it or not, I've never been to Paris.

target: Está claro que algo ha cambiado entre ellos.
english: It's clear that something has changed between them.

target: Este aeropuerto es el peor en el que he estado.
english: This airport is the worst I've been to.

target: El desayuno del hotel es el mejor que he probado.
english: The hotel breakfast is the best I've tried.

target: Este vuelo es el más largo que he tomado.
english: This flight is the longest I've taken.

target: El correo que envié ayer no ha llegado.
english: The email I sent yesterday hasn't arrived.

target: Todavía no hemos decidido adónde ir mañana.
english: We still haven't decided where to go tomorrow.

target: Habré terminado de hacer las maletas para las diez.
english: I will have finished packing by ten.
grammar: Future perfect: "habré terminado" = I will have finished. Expresses completion before a future point.

target: De haber tenido más tiempo, habríamos explorado la selva.
english: Had we had more time, we would have explored the jungle.
grammar: "De haber + participle" is a formal alternative to "si hubiéramos + participle" for past unreal conditions.

target: Nada más llegar, supimos que habíamos elegido bien.
english: As soon as we arrived, we knew we had chosen well.
grammar: "Nada más" + infinitive = as soon as. A concise way to express immediacy.

target: Habré terminado el informe para las cinco.
english: I will have finished the report by five.
grammar: Future perfect: habré terminado = I will have finished.

target: Es increíble que ya hayan pasado diez años desde la boda.
english: It's incredible that ten years have already passed since the wedding.
grammar: Present perfect subjunctive: hayan pasado (haber subjunctive + past participle)

target: Se nota que has trabajado mucho en esto.
english: You can tell you've worked a lot on this.

target: A propósito, ¿has visto a María últimamente?
english: By the way, have you seen María lately?

target: Hemos vuelto a cometer el mismo error.
english: We've made the same mistake again.

target: Le explicó que no había podido terminar a tiempo.
english: He explained to her that he hadn't been able to finish on time.

target: Le informaron de que su vuelo había sido cancelado.
english: They informed her that her flight had been cancelled.

target: Le pregunté si había estado alguna vez en Sudamérica.
english: I asked him if he had ever been to South America.

target: Le pregunté por qué no había venido a la reunión.
english: I asked him why he hadn't come to the meeting.

target: Le conté que había estado pensando en cambiar de carrera.
english: I told her I had been thinking about changing careers.

target: Le informé de que su solicitud había sido aprobada.
english: I informed him that his application had been approved.

target: De haber sabido las consecuencias, no habría aceptado.
english: Had I known the consequences, I would not have accepted.
grammar: De haber + participle = formal alternative to si hubiera + participle.

target: Habrá salido ya; siempre se va a esta hora.
english: He must have left already — he always leaves at this time.
grammar: Future tense for probability/conjecture about the present.

target: Ella habrá terminado para las cinco.
english: She will have finished by five.

target: ¿Has estado alguna vez en Japón?
english: Have you ever been to Japan?

target: Ella ha vivido en cinco países.
english: She has lived in five countries.

target: Yo ya he terminado mi tarea.
english: I've already finished my homework.

target: Nosotros hemos comprado una casa nueva.
english: We have bought a new house.

target: Yo he aprendido mucho este año.
english: I have learned a lot this year.

target: Ella nunca ha probado el sushi.
english: She has never tried sushi.

target: Mi madre ha cocinado una paella.
english: My mother has made a paella.

target: ¿Has hablado con tu hermano?
english: Have you talked to your brother?

target: Ella ha escrito tres novelas.
english: She has written three novels.

target: Tú has mejorado mucho este semestre.
english: You have improved a lot this semester.

target: Ella ya ha comprado los regalos.
english: She has already bought the gifts.

target: Nosotros hemos pintado la habitación.
english: We have painted the room.

target: Mi hijo ha aprobado el examen.
english: My son has passed the exam.

target: He pensado mucho en tu propuesta.
english: I've thought a lot about your proposal.

target: Explicó que el tren se había retrasado.
english: He explained the train had been delayed.

target: Me informaron de que la reunion se habia adelantado al martes.
english: They informed me the meeting had been moved up to Tuesday.

target: No en vano ha sido el departamento con mejores resultados este trimestre.
english: Not for nothing has it been the department with the best results this quarter.

target: En la aduana me pidieron que abriera la maleta para una inspección.
english: At customs they asked me to open my suitcase for an inspection.
grammar: "conseguir" (e→i) + infinitive = to manage to. Irregular yo: consigo. "Cuando" + present for habitual.

target: El musgo crecía en las rocas húmedas junto al arroyo.
english: Moss was growing on the damp rocks beside the stream.
grammar: "ojalá" + present subjunctive for wishes about the present/future. "Ojalá" + imperfect subjunctive for unlikely/impossible wishes.

target: El profesor nos mandó leer tres capítulos del libro para el próximo lunes.
english: The professor assigned us to read three chapters of the book for next Monday.
grammar: "El profesor nos pidió que hiciéramos el ejercicio en parejas" — "pedir que" + subjunctive. "Hiciéramos" = imperfect subjunctive (past request → past subjunctive).

target: Necesito ir al médico.
english: I need to go to the doctor.
grammar: "quisiera" (imperfect subjunctive of querer) = I would like. More polite than "quiero." Common for requests.

target: La doctora me aconsejó que descansara durante una semana.
english: The doctor advised me to rest for a week.
grammar: "tardar en" + infinitive = to take (time) to. "¿Cuánto tarda?" = How long does it take?

target: Me recomendaron que consultara con un especialista en traumatología.
english: They recommended that I consult with a trauma specialist.

target: Me enoja que las personas no reciclen.
english: It angers me that people don't recycle.
grammar: "decir que" + subjunctive for commands/advice. "Dejara" = imperfect subjunctive (past context). "Dejar de" = to stop.

target: Aspiro a convertirme en un profesional reconocido.
english: I aspire to become a recognized professional.
grammar: "gustaría que" + imperfect subjunctive. Conditional + que → subjunctive. "Vinieras" from "venir" (irregular subjunctive stem).

target: Ojalá pudiera volver a empezar con lo que sé ahora.
english: I wish I could start over with what I know now.

target: Confío en que el equipo logrará superar las expectativas.
english: I trust that the team will manage to exceed expectations.

target: Es fundamental mantener un tono profesional en los correos de trabajo.
english: It is essential to maintain a professional tone in work emails.
grammar: "pedir que" + subjunctive. "Pidió" (e→i preterite) + "ayudara" (imperfect subjunctive, matching past tense).

target: Adjunto encontrará el documento que me pidió la semana pasada.
english: Attached you will find the document you asked me for last week.

target: Las elecciones se celebrarán el próximo domingo.
english: The elections will be held next Sunday.

target: La arquitectura colonial de Cartagena es patrimonio de la humanidad.
english: The colonial architecture of Cartagena is a World Heritage Site.
grammar: unreal conditional: si + imperfect subjunctive → conditional. "Tuviera" from "tener."

target: Cada país hispanohablante tiene sus propias expresiones y modismos.
english: Each Spanish-speaking country has its own expressions and idioms.
grammar: "ojalá" + imperfect subjunctive for unlikely/impossible wishes. "Pudiera" = could (imperfect subj of poder).

target: La declaración de la renta se presenta entre abril y junio.
english: The income tax return is filed between April and June.
grammar: unreal conditional for business: si + imperfect subjunctive → conditional.

target: El campeonato mundial de atletismo se celebra cada dos años.
english: The world athletics championship is held every two years.
grammar: unreal conditional: si + imperfect subjunctive → conditional. "Saldríamos" from "salir" (irregular conditional stem: saldr-).

target: Ojalá hubiera estudiado más cuando tenía la oportunidad.
english: I wish I had studied more when I had the opportunity.

target: Quisiera una mesa junto a la ventana.
english: I'd like a table by the window.
grammar: "Quisiera" — very polite form of "I would like."

target: La azafata nos pidió que abrocháramos los cinturones.
english: The flight attendant asked us to fasten our seatbelts.
grammar: "Pidió que" + imperfect subjunctive — indirect command.

target: Les pedí que me despertaran a las seis.
english: I asked them to wake me up at six.

target: Me pidieron que redactara un informe detallado.
english: They asked me to write a detailed report.

target: El gerente sugirió que implementáramos un nuevo sistema.
english: The manager suggested that we implement a new system.

target: Ojalá mis padres pudieran venir a la boda.
english: I wish my parents could come to the wedding.

target: Me sorprendió que hablara tan bien español.
english: It surprised me that he spoke Spanish so well.

target: El hecho de que no respondiera me preocupó.
english: The fact that he didn't respond worried me.

target: Habría sido mejor que hubiéramos esperado.
english: It would have been better if we had waited.

target: Quienquiera que haya escrito este libro es un genio.
english: Whoever wrote this book is a genius.
grammar: "Quienquiera que" + subjunctive — whoever.

target: Dondequiera que vayas, lleva siempre un diccionario.
english: Wherever you go, always carry a dictionary.
grammar: "Dondequiera que" + subjunctive — wherever.

target: Hubiera preferido que me lo dijeras antes.
english: I would have preferred that you had told me sooner.

target: Nos aconsejaron que no bebiéramos agua del grifo.
english: They advised us not to drink tap water.

target: Es natural que los hijos quieran independizarse.
english: It's natural that children want to become independent.

target: No es que no quiera, es que no puedo.
english: It's not that I don't want to, it's that I can't.

target: Era importante que practicáramos todos los días.
english: It was important that we practice every day.

target: Les pedí que vinieran temprano.
english: I asked them to come early.

target: El jefe quería que termináramos el informe antes del viernes.
english: The boss wanted us to finish the report before Friday.
grammar: Quería que + imperfect subjunctive: a past wish about someone else's action. "Quería que vinieras" (I wanted you to come).

target: Ojalá tuviera más vacaciones.
english: I wish I had more vacation time.
grammar: Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive: a wish seen as unlikely. "Ojalá pudiera volar" (I wish I could fly). Past subjunctive = more remote/unlikely.

target: Me sorprendió que nadie dijera nada.
english: It surprised me that nobody said anything.
grammar: Past emotion + que + imperfect subjunctive: "Me alegré de que estuvieras allí" (I was glad you were there). Past emotions → imperfect subjunctive.

target: Era importante que todos estuvieran de acuerdo.
english: It was important that everyone agreed.
grammar: Past impersonal + que + imperfect subjunctive: "Era importante que estudiaras" (It was important that you studied). Past main clause → imperfect subjunctive.

target: Esperaba que me llamaran para la entrevista.
english: I was hoping they would call me for the interview.
grammar: Esperaba que + imperfect subjunctive: "Esperaba que llegaran" (I was hoping they'd arrive). Past hope about uncertain outcome.

target: Estoy seguro de que llegará a tiempo.
english: I'm sure he'll arrive on time.

target: Es lógico que quieras descansar después de tanto trabajo.
english: It's logical that you want to rest after so much work.

target: Quería que supieras la verdad.
english: I wanted you to know the truth.
grammar: Quería que + imperfect subjunctive: "Quería que lo supieras" (I wanted you to know). Past main clause + que → imperfect subjunctive.

target: Ojalá encontrara un trabajo cerca de casa.
english: I wish I could find a job close to home.

target: Era necesario que termináramos el proyecto antes del jueves.
english: It was necessary for us to finish the project before Thursday.

target: Le aconsejé que hablara con su jefe.
english: I advised him to talk to his boss.
grammar: Aconsejar que + imperfect subjunctive: "Me aconsejó que descansara" (She advised me to rest). Past advice → imperfect subjunctive.

target: Era importante que todos participaran en la decisión.
english: It was important for everyone to participate in the decision.

target: Ojalá mi casa estuviera más cerca del trabajo.
english: I wish my house were closer to work.

target: Ojalá hubiera aprovechado más el tiempo cuando era joven.
english: I wish I had made better use of my time when I was young.
grammar: Ojalá + pluperfect subjunctive for impossible past wishes

target: El nuevo software facilitará mucho el trabajo.
english: The new software will make work much easier.

target: El director presentará los cambios en la asamblea.
english: The director will present the changes at the assembly.

target: ¿Llamarás a tu hermana por su cumpleaños?
english: Will you call your sister for her birthday?

target: Era necesario que reserváramos con antelación.
english: It was necessary that we book in advance.

target: Era importante que cambiáramos dinero antes de salir.
english: It was important that we exchange money before leaving.

target: Dudaba que mi hermano viniera a la boda.
english: I doubted my brother would come to the wedding.

target: Era necesario que ordenáramos el garaje.
english: It was necessary for us to organize the garage.

target: Ojalá mi hermana viviera en la misma ciudad.
english: I wish my sister lived in the same city.

target: Buscaba una casa que tuviera jardín para los niños.
english: I was looking for a house with a garden for the kids.

target: De ahí que la familia decidiera mudarse al campo.
english: That's why the family decided to move to the countryside.

target: Ojalá hubiera más tiempo para visitar todo.
english: I wish there were more time to visit everything.

target: Me habría gustado que nos quedáramos más tiempo.
english: I would have liked us to stay longer.

target: Habría sido mejor que hubiéramos alquilado un carro.
english: It would have been better if we had rented a car.

target: En el supuesto de que cancelen el vuelo, nos reembolsarán.
english: In the event that they cancel the flight, they will reimburse us.
grammar: "En el supuesto de que" (in the event that) — formal conditional connector, always takes subjunctive.

target: Quienquiera que haya diseñado este itinerario hizo un trabajo increíble.
english: Whoever designed this itinerary did an incredible job.
grammar: "Quienquiera que" (whoever) + subjunctive. Always takes subjunctive because the person is unknown/unspecified.

target: Si pudieras elegir, ¿qué puesto querrías?
english: If you could choose, which position would you want?

target: Aunque me ofrecieran el doble, no volvería.
english: Even if they offered me double, I wouldn't go back.
grammar: Aunque + imperfect subjunctive for hypothetical concession.

target: Por mucho que se esfuerce, no logrará el ascenso.
english: No matter how hard he tries, he won't get the promotion.
grammar: Por mucho que + subjunctive: concessive with emphasis.

target: Aunque el mercado estuviera en crisis, seguiríamos adelante.
english: Even if the market were in crisis, we would carry on.

target: Comoquiera que sea, debemos presentar los resultados el lunes.
english: Be that as it may, we must present the results on Monday.

target: Me encantaría que vinieras a la cena de Navidad.
english: I'd love for you to come to Christmas dinner.

target: El pediatra recomendó que el niño descansara.
english: The pediatrician recommended that the boy rest.

target: La profesora pidió que los padres asistieran a la reunión.
english: The teacher asked that the parents attend the meeting.

target: Ni que fueras su madre para hablarle así.
english: It's not like you're his mother to talk to him like that.
grammar: Ni que + imperfect subjunctive = it's not as if / as though

target: Es como si mi madre tuviera un sexto sentido con los niños.
english: It's as if my mother had a sixth sense with kids.
grammar: "Como si" always takes imperfect subjunctive: "Habla como si supiera todo" (He talks as if he knew everything).

target: Quería que mi hijo estudiara medicina.
english: I wanted my son to study medicine.
grammar: Quería que + imperfect subjunctive for past wishes about others.

target: Era necesario que llegáramos temprano.
english: It was necessary that we arrived early.

target: Ojalá hubiera más tiempo para descansar.
english: I wish there were more time to rest.

target: Ojalá mi familia viviera más cerca.
english: I wish my family lived closer.

target: Le sugerí que hablara con su jefe directamente.
english: I suggested that he talk to his boss directly.

target: Ojalá pudiéramos vernos más a menudo.
english: I wish we could see each other more often.

target: Esperaba que el problema se resolviera pronto.
english: I was hoping the problem would be resolved soon.

target: Quería que todo el mundo se divirtiera en la fiesta.
english: I wanted everyone to have fun at the party.

target: Era necesario que firmáramos el contrato hoy.
english: It was necessary for us to sign the contract today.
grammar: Era necesario que + imperfect subjunctive for past impersonal obligations.

target: Habría preferido que me consultaran antes de tomar la decisión.
english: I would have preferred them to consult me before making the decision.

target: Como si fuera la primera vez que lo hace.
english: As if it were the first time he's doing it.
grammar: Como si always takes imperfect subjunctive.

target: Quienquiera que haya dicho eso, se equivoca.
english: Whoever said that is wrong.
grammar: Quienquiera que + subjunctive = whoever.

target: Dondequiera que vayas, lleva tu pasaporte.
english: Wherever you go, take your passport.
grammar: Dondequiera que + subjunctive = wherever.

target: Comoquiera que se mire, fue una mala decisión.
english: However you look at it, it was a bad decision.

target: Insistió en que nos quedáramos a cenar.
english: She insisted we stay for dinner.

target: Le exigieron que presentara los documentos de inmediato.
english: They demanded he present the documents immediately.

target: Me reprochó que no le hubiera llamado antes.
english: She reproached me for not having called her sooner.

target: Cabe señalar que esta medida beneficiará a miles de personas.
english: It should be noted that this measure will benefit thousands of people.

target: Estará cansado después de un viaje tan largo.
english: He must be tired after such a long trip.

target: Los invitados llegarán a las ocho.
english: The guests will arrive at eight.

target: La tienda cerrará a las diez.
english: The store will close at ten.

target: Ojalá tuviéramos más vacaciones este año.
english: I wish we had more vacation this year.

target: Ojalá mis padres pudieran quedarse más tiempo.
english: I wish my parents could stay longer.

target: Me advirtio que tuviera cuidado con la carretera de noche.
english: He warned me to be careful on the road at night.
grammar: Indirect command: advirtió que + imperfect subjunctive.

target: Nos recomendo que probaramos los tacos de ese puesto.
english: He recommended we try the tacos from that stall.

target: Le sugerimos que revisara los numeros antes de presentarlos.
english: We suggested he review the numbers before presenting them.

target: Hice una presentación delante de toda la clase y me puse muy nervioso.
english: I gave a presentation in front of the whole class and I got very nervous.
grammar: real conditional: "si" + present, future. "Sacaré" = I will get. "Sacar notas" = to get grades.

target: El índice de desempleo juvenil es uno de los mayores problemas económicos del país.
english: The youth unemployment rate is one of the biggest economic problems in the country.
grammar: unreal past conditional: "si" + pluperfect subjunctive (hubiera) + conditional perfect (habría). "If I had studied, I would have passed."

target: Ellos van a mudarse a otra ciudad.
english: They are going to move to another city.
grammar: real conditional: si + present → future. "Ahorrar" = to save. "Lo suficiente" = enough (neuter lo).

target: ¿Qué harías si ganaras la lotería?
english: What would you do if you won the lottery?

target: Si tuviera más dinero, viajaría por todo el mundo.
english: If I had more money, I would travel around the world.
grammar: unreal present conditional: si + imperfect subjunctive → conditional. "Pudiera" = if I could. "Me tomaría" = I would take.

target: Es poco probable que cambien las reglas este año.
english: It is unlikely that they will change the rules this year.
grammar: unreal past: si + pluperfect subjunctive → conditional perfect. "If I had known, I would have come earlier."

target: Si hubiera sabido antes, habría tomado otra decisión.
english: If I had known earlier, I would have made a different decision.
grammar: "pensar" + infinitive = to plan/intend to. "El mes que viene" = next month (literally: the month that comes).

target: Si hubiera sabido que el hotel estaba tan lejos, habría alquilado un carro.
english: If I had known the hotel was so far, I would have rented a car.
grammar: Pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect — third conditional.

target: Si tuviera más experiencia, me ascenderían.
english: If I had more experience, they would promote me.
grammar: Imperfect subjunctive + conditional — second conditional.

target: Si pudiera volver atrás, pasaría más tiempo con mi familia.
english: If I could go back, I'd spend more time with my family.

target: Si tuviera más tiempo, aprendería a tocar la guitarra.
english: If I had more time, I would learn to play the guitar.
grammar: Second conditional: imperfect subjunctive + conditional.

target: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado el examen.
english: If I had studied more, I would have passed the exam.
grammar: Third conditional: pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect.

target: Si tuviera más tiempo, viajaría más.
english: If I had more time, I'd travel more.
grammar: Imperfect subjunctive + conditional — second conditional.

target: Si pudiera elegir, viviría en la costa.
english: If I could choose, I'd live on the coast.

target: Si pudieras cambiar algo, ¿qué sería?
english: If you could change something, what would it be?

target: Si hubiera sabido, habría venido antes.
english: If I had known, I would have come earlier.
grammar: Third conditional: pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect.

target: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, ahora sería doctor.
english: If I had studied medicine, I'd be a doctor now.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past condition, present result.

target: Si hubiera aceptado ese trabajo, viviría en Madrid.
english: If I had accepted that job, I'd be living in Madrid.

target: Si pudiera elegir, viviría cerca del mar.
english: If I could choose, I would live near the sea.

target: Si no trabajara tanto, pasaría más tiempo con mis hijos.
english: If I didn't work so much, I would spend more time with my kids.

target: Si estuviera en tu situación, hablaría con ella.
english: If I were in your situation, I would talk to her.

target: Si fuera más joven, viajaría por todo el mundo.
english: If I were younger, I would travel around the world.

target: Si tuviera carro, no tardaría tanto en llegar.
english: If I had a car, I wouldn't take so long to get there.

target: Si fuera tú, aceptaría esa oferta de trabajo.
english: If I were you, I would accept that job offer.
grammar: "Si fuera tú..." = If I were you... Imperfect subjunctive of ser + conditional for advice: "Si fuera tú, lo haría."

target: Si pudiera cambiar algo de mi vida, estudiaría otra carrera.
english: If I could change something about my life, I would study a different degree.

target: Si hubiera tenido tu número, te habría llamado.
english: If I had had your number, I would have called you.

target: Si conociera a alguien en esa ciudad, me mudaría allí.
english: If I knew someone in that city, I would move there.

target: Si tuviera un día libre, dormiría hasta tarde.
english: If I had a day off, I would sleep in.

target: Si viviera en otro país, extrañaría la comida de aquí.
english: If I lived in another country, I would miss the food from here.

target: Si hubiera aceptado ese trabajo, ahora viviría en Barcelona.
english: If I had accepted that job, I would now be living in Barcelona.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past condition → present result. "Si hubiera estudiado, ahora sabría." Pluperfect subjunctive + conditional present.

target: Si hubiera hecho caso a mi padre, no tendría estas deudas.
english: If I had listened to my father, I wouldn't have these debts.
grammar: Mixed conditional: "Si no hubiera llovido, estaríamos en la playa." Past condition (pluperfect subjunctive) with present consequence (conditional).

target: Si no hubiera comido tanto, no me sentiría mal ahora.
english: If I hadn't eaten so much, I wouldn't feel bad now.

target: Si hubiera aprendido inglés de pequeño, ahora lo hablaría perfectamente.
english: If I had learned English as a child, I would speak it perfectly now.

target: Si hubiera preparado mejor la presentación, habría salido mejor.
english: If I had prepared the presentation better, it would have gone better.

target: Si hubiera invertido en esa empresa, ahora sería rico.
english: If I had invested in that company, I would be rich now.
grammar: Mixed conditional — past unrealized + present hypothetical result

target: Si hubiera tenido tu apoyo, todo habría sido más fácil.
english: If I had had your support, everything would have been easier.

target: Si hubiera practicado más, habría jugado mejor en el partido.
english: If I had practiced more, I would have played better in the match.

target: Me mudaría a otra ciudad si encontrara trabajo.
english: I'd move to another city if I found work.

target: Yo llamaría antes de ir, por si acaso.
english: I'd call before going, just in case.

target: Si tuviera más tiempo, leería más libros.
english: If I had more time, I would read more books.
grammar: Si + imperfect subjunctive (tuviera) for hypothetical conditions

target: Si fuera más joven, viajaría sin parar.
english: If I were younger, I would travel nonstop.

target: Si supiera su número, le llamaría.
english: If I knew his number, I'd call him.

target: Si fuera posible, me tomaría el día libre.
english: If it were possible, I'd take the day off.

target: Si trabajara menos horas, tendría más tiempo para mi familia.
english: If I worked fewer hours, I'd have more time for my family.

target: Si el jefe me diera más responsabilidad, rendiría mejor.
english: If the boss gave me more responsibility, I'd perform better.

target: Si los niños se portaran bien, les compraría un helado.
english: If the kids behaved, I'd buy them ice cream.

target: Si hubiera sabido que venías, habría preparado algo de comer.
english: If I had known you were coming, I would have prepared something to eat.
grammar: Type III conditional: si + pluperfect subjunctive (hubiera sabido) + conditional perfect (habría preparado)

target: Habríamos ganado el contrato si la presentación hubiera sido mejor.
english: We would have won the contract if the presentation had been better.
grammar: Reversed order: conditional perfect + si + pluperfect subjunctive

target: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, ahora sería doctora.
english: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
grammar: Mixed conditional: si + pluperfect subjunctive + simple conditional (past cause, present result)

target: Si por mí fuera, trabajaría desde casa todos los días.
english: If it were up to me, I'd work from home every day.

target: Si el vuelo no fuera tan caro, lo compraría.
english: If the flight weren't so expensive, I'd buy it.

target: Si mi madre viviera más cerca, la vería más.
english: If my mother lived closer, I'd see her more.

target: Si mi abuelo estuviera aquí, estaría muy orgulloso.
english: If my grandfather were here, he'd be very proud.

target: Habría visitado más museos si hubiera tenido más tiempo.
english: I would have visited more museums if I had had more time.

target: Si el hotel no fuera tan ruidoso, dormiría mejor.
english: If the hotel weren't so noisy, I'd sleep better.

target: Si supiera hablar japonés, viajaría a Tokio.
english: If I knew how to speak Japanese, I'd travel to Tokyo.

target: Si el vuelo no costara mil euros, lo compraría hoy.
english: If the flight didn't cost a thousand euros, I'd buy it today.

target: Si hubiera sabido que cerraba, habría ido antes al monumento.
english: If I had known it was closing, I would have gone to the monument earlier.

target: Habría cocinado algo especial si hubiera sabido que venías.
english: I would have cooked something special if I had known you were coming.

target: Habría preparado más comida si hubiera sabido que venían tantos.
english: I would have prepared more food if I had known so many were coming.

target: Si mi padre no trabajara tanto, descansaría más.
english: If my father didn't work so much, he'd rest more.

target: Si hubiera tren directo, llegaríamos antes.
english: If there were a direct train, we would arrive sooner.

target: Si tuviera más vacaciones, viajaría por toda Sudamérica.
english: If I had more vacation, I would travel all over South America.
grammar: Conditional II (present unreal): si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional. A hypothetical that's unlikely but possible.

target: Si no fuera por los turistas, esa zona estaría desierta.
english: If it weren't for the tourists, that area would be deserted.

target: Si hubiera preparado mejor la entrevista, me habrían contratado.
english: If I had prepared better for the interview, they would have hired me.
grammar: Conditional III: Si + pluperfect subjunctive, conditional perfect.

target: De haber sabido, no habría aceptado el puesto.
english: Had I known, I wouldn't have accepted the position.
grammar: De + infinitive compound as alternative to si clause.

target: Si hubiera negociado mejor, tendría mejores condiciones.
english: If I had negotiated better, I would have better conditions.
grammar: Mixed conditional: Si + pluperfect subj + conditional (present result).

target: Si hubiera delegado mejor, no estaría tan agotado.
english: If I had delegated better, I wouldn't be so exhausted.

target: Si mi abuelo viviera, estaría orgulloso.
english: If my grandfather were alive, he'd be proud.
grammar: Type II conditional: si + imperfect subjunctive (viviera) + conditional (estaría)

target: Si yo fuera tú, aceptaría la oferta.
english: If I were you, I would accept the offer.

target: Si pudiera elegir, viviría junto al mar.
english: If I could choose, I would live by the sea.

target: Si no tuviera que trabajar, estaría en la playa.
english: If I didn't have to work, I would be at the beach.

target: Si viviera más cerca, vendría todos los días.
english: If I lived closer, I would come every day.

target: Si tuviera un jardín, plantaría tomates.
english: If I had a garden, I would plant tomatoes.

target: Si hablara mejor español, conseguiría ese puesto.
english: If I spoke better Spanish, I would get that position.
grammar: Si + imperfect subjunctive for hypothetical present situations.

target: Si no existiera internet, la vida sería muy diferente.
english: If the internet didn't exist, life would be very different.

target: Si tuviera más experiencia, solicitaría ese empleo.
english: If I had more experience, I would apply for that job.

target: Si tuviera vacaciones, visitaría a mi abuela.
english: If I had vacation, I would visit my grandmother.

target: Si hiciera buen tiempo, comeríamos en el jardín.
english: If the weather were nice, we would eat in the garden.

target: Si conociera a alguien allí, me sentiría más cómodo.
english: If I knew someone there, I would feel more comfortable.

target: Si pudiera cocinar mejor, invitaría a más gente.
english: If I could cook better, I would invite more people.

target: Si trabajara menos, pasaría más tiempo con mi familia.
english: If I worked less, I would spend more time with my family.

target: Si fuera más joven, aprendería otro idioma.
english: If I were younger, I would learn another language.

target: Si supiera conducir, alquilaría un coche.
english: If I knew how to drive, I would rent a car.

target: Si tuviera un hermano, no me sentiría tan solo.
english: If I had a brother, I wouldn't feel so alone.

target: Si me pagaran más, no buscaría otro trabajo.
english: If they paid me more, I wouldn't look for another job.

target: Si estuviera de vacaciones, estaría durmiendo ahora.
english: If I were on vacation, I would be sleeping right now.

target: Si hiciera más ejercicio, me sentiría mejor.
english: If I exercised more, I would feel better.

target: Si pudiera cambiar algo de mi vida, no cambiaría nada.
english: If I could change something about my life, I wouldn't change anything.

target: Si hubiera llovido, nos habríamos quedado en casa.
english: If it had rained, we would have stayed home.

target: Si hubiera hecho la reserva, tendríamos mesa ahora.
english: If I had made the reservation, we would have a table now.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past condition + present result.

target: Si hubiera ahorrado más, ahora podría comprar un coche.
english: If I had saved more, I could buy a car now.

target: Si no me hubiera mudado, seguiría viviendo con mis padres.
english: If I hadn't moved, I would still be living with my parents.

target: Si hubiera tenido más confianza, habría hablado en la reunión.
english: If I had had more confidence, I would have spoken in the meeting.

target: Si yo hablara chino, viajaría a Pekín.
english: If I spoke Chinese, I'd travel to Beijing.

target: Si tuviera tiempo, viajaría más.
english: If I had time, I would travel more.

target: Si tuviera más dinero, compraría una casa.
english: If I had more money, I'd buy a house.

target: Si me dieran la oportunidad, lo intentaría.
english: If they gave me the chance, I'd try it.

target: Si hubiera más tiempo, visitaríamos el museo.
english: If there were more time, we'd visit the museum.

target: Si pudiera cocinar como mi madre, sería feliz.
english: If I could cook like my mom, I'd be happy.

target: Mi dormitorio es tranquilo porque da a un patio interior.
english: My bedroom is quiet because it faces an inner courtyard.
grammar: "hay que" + infinitive = one must / you have to (impersonal). Different from "tener que" (personal): "tengo que girar."

target: La almohada es demasiado dura para mí.
english: The pillow is too hard for me.
grammar: "tardar en" + infinitive = to take time to. "Tarda" is impersonal here (it takes). "Tardo" = I take (personal).

target: Hay una gotera en el techo del salón cuando llueve mucho.
english: There's a leak in the living room ceiling when it rains a lot.
grammar: "se puede" = one can (impersonal se). "Ir andando" = to go walking/on foot. "En autobús/metro/carro" — "en" for transport.

target: Nos ofrecieron un vuelo alternativo porque el nuestro fue cancelado por condiciones meteorológicas adversas.
english: They offered us an alternative flight because ours was canceled due to adverse weather conditions.
grammar: "atreverse a" + infinitive = to dare to. Reflexive verb. "No me atrevo" = I don't dare.

target: El mercado laboral es muy competitivo y tener idiomas abre muchas puertas.
english: The job market is very competitive and knowing languages opens many doors.
grammar: "se busca(n)" = is/are sought (passive se). The verb agrees with the thing sought: "se buscan empleados" (plural).

target: Tengo tos y me duele la garganta.
english: I have a cough and my throat hurts.
grammar: passive participle as adjective: "incluido" agrees with "desayuno" (masc). "Estar" + past participle for states resulting from actions.

target: El hospital está a diez minutos de aquí.
english: The hospital is ten minutes from here.
grammar: passive voice: ser + past participle + por. "Fue cancelado" = was cancelled. "Por" introduces the cause/agent.

target: ¿Puedes darme algo para el dolor de cabeza?
english: Can you give me something for the headache?
grammar: "hay que" (impersonal must) + infinitive. "Antes de" + infinitive = before doing.

target: El paciente fue trasladado en ambulancia a urgencias.
english: The patient was transported by ambulance to the emergency room.

target: Me importa mucho lo que piensen mis amigos de mí.
english: I care a lot about what my friends think of me.
grammar: "recetar" = to prescribe. 3rd person plural "recetaron" with no stated subject = impersonal "they prescribed me."

target: Me desperté sobresaltado porque había tenido una pesadilla terrible.
english: I woke up startled because I had had a terrible nightmare.
grammar: "hay que" + infinitive = one must (impersonal obligation). "El medio ambiente" = the environment.

target: ¿Cómo se pronuncia esta palabra correctamente?
english: How is this word pronounced correctly?
grammar: "¿Cómo se pronuncia esta palabra correctamente?" — "se pronuncia" = impersonal se (one pronounces / it's pronounced). "Correctamente" — adverb from "correcto."

target: El terrorismo sigue siendo una amenaza para la seguridad mundial.
english: Terrorism continues to be a threat to global security.
grammar: passive: "fue aprobada" (ser + past participle, agreeing with "ley" = feminine). "Por unanimidad" = unanimously.

target: Compramos las entradas por internet para evitar la cola.
english: We bought the tickets online to avoid the line.
grammar: passive voice: ser + past participle + por. "Fue pintada" agrees with "obra" (feminine). Historical fact in preterite.

target: Las iglesias barrocas de Oaxaca están decoradas con pan de oro.
english: The Baroque churches of Oaxaca are decorated with gold leaf.
grammar: passive with ser: "fue escrita" (feminine agreeing with "novela"). "Por" + agent. Common for attributing artistic works.

target: El antivirus detectó una amenaza y la eliminó automáticamente.
english: The antivirus detected a threat and removed it automatically.
grammar: "se requiere" = is required (passive se). Impersonal construction common in job listings.

target: Dedico al menos una hora al día a leer antes de dormir.
english: I dedicate at least one hour a day to reading before bed.
grammar: "se necesitan" (passive se, plural agreement). "Cada uno" = each one (invariable).

target: Se habla español en veinte países.
english: Spanish is spoken in twenty countries.
grammar: "Se" + verb — passive/impersonal construction.

target: Se dice que va a llover.
english: They say it's going to rain.

target: El edificio fue construido en 1900.
english: The building was built in 1900.
grammar: Passive with "ser" + past participle.

target: Hay que estudiar más.
english: One must study more.
grammar: "Hay que" — impersonal obligation.

target: Se llevan muy bien.
english: They get along very well.
grammar: "Llevarse bien" = to get along. Reciprocal: "Nos llevamos bien" = We get along well.

target: Le hace falta descansar.
english: He needs to rest.
grammar: "Hacerle falta" = to need (gustar pattern): "Le hace falta descansar" = He needs to rest. Indirect object + hacer falta.

target: Aquí se habla español.
english: Spanish is spoken here.
grammar: "Se" impersonal: no specific subject. "Se vive bien aquí" = One lives well here / People live well here. Like English "one" or "people."

target: Se necesitan camareros con experiencia.
english: Experienced waiters are needed.
grammar: Passive "se" with plural noun: "Se venden coches" = Cars are sold. The verb agrees with the noun (plural). Very common in signs and announcements.

target: Se dice que va a llover mañana.
english: They say it's going to rain tomorrow.
grammar: "Se dice que..." = It is said that / They say that. Impersonal se for general statements. No specific person saying it.

target: El proyecto fue aprobado por el director.
english: The project was approved by the director.
grammar: Passive with ser: "Fue escrito por Cervantes" = It was written by Cervantes. Ser + participle + por (agent). Less common than passive se.

target: Se recomienda llegar quince minutos antes.
english: It is recommended to arrive fifteen minutes early.

target: Se puede pagar con tarjeta aquí.
english: You can pay by card here.
grammar: "Se puede" + infinitive = One can / You can: "Aquí se puede fumar." Impersonal way to state what's allowed.

target: La decisión fue tomada sin consultarnos.
english: The decision was made without consulting us.
grammar: Passive with ser emphasizes the action: "Fuimos invitados" = We were invited. Focus on what happened, not who did it.

target: Se cree que habrá cambios en la empresa.
english: It is believed there will be changes in the company.
grammar: "Se cree que..." = It is believed that. Impersonal se for widely-held beliefs without attributing to anyone specific.

target: Se pueden comprar entradas por internet.
english: Tickets can be bought online.

target: Se vive bien en esta ciudad.
english: Life is good in this city.
grammar: "Se vive" = one lives / people live. Impersonal se with intransitive verbs: "Se vive bien aquí" = People live well here.

target: Se trabaja mucho en este país.
english: People work a lot in this country.
grammar: "Se trabaja" = one works / people work. Impersonal se for general truths: "Se trabaja mucho aquí."

target: Se aconseja no viajar de noche por esa zona.
english: It is advised not to travel at night through that area.

target: Se hablan varios idiomas en esta oficina.
english: Several languages are spoken in this office.

target: Se tarda una hora en llegar al aeropuerto.
english: It takes one hour to get to the airport.
grammar: "Se tarda" = it takes (time). Impersonal: "Se tarda dos horas" = It takes two hours. No specific person.

target: Se deben entregar los informes antes del viernes.
english: Reports must be submitted before Friday.

target: Se aprende mucho viajando.
english: You learn a lot by traveling.
grammar: "Se aprende" = one learns. Impersonal se: "Se aprende practicando" = You learn by practicing.

target: Ellos se hablan por teléfono cada noche.
english: They talk to each other on the phone every night.
grammar: Hablarse (reciprocal): se hablan — they talk to each other

target: Se habla español en más de veinte países.
english: Spanish is spoken in more than twenty countries.
grammar: Se impersonal — used for general statements without a specific subject

target: La reunión fue cancelada por el director.
english: The meeting was canceled by the director.
grammar: Passive voice with ser: fue cancelada — past participle agrees with subject

target: Estas camisetas se venden muy bien.
english: These shirts sell very well.
grammar: Passive se: the verb agrees with the grammatical subject. "Se venden camisetas" (camisetas = subject, plural → venden).

target: Se dice que van a abrir un supermercado nuevo.
english: They say they're going to open a new supermarket.
grammar: "Se dice que..." = It is said that. Impersonal se for common knowledge or rumors.

target: Se recomienda llegar con antelación al aeropuerto.
english: It is recommended to arrive at the airport early.

target: La decisión fue tomada sin consultar al equipo.
english: The decision was made without consulting the team.
grammar: Passive with ser + past participle (tomada agrees with decisión)

target: El producto fue retirado del mercado.
english: The product was pulled from the market.

target: El proyecto fue financiado por inversores privados.
english: The project was funded by private investors.

target: Se aconseja reservar con antelación.
english: It is advisable to book in advance.

target: Se come a las dos en España.
english: People eat lunch at two in Spain.

target: Se nota a la legua que está enamorado.
english: You can tell from a mile away that he's in love.

target: Se puede comer muy bien en este barrio.
english: You can eat very well in this neighborhood.

target: El hotel fue renovado completamente.
english: The hotel was completely renovated.

target: Se vive bien en este barrio.
english: Life is good in this neighborhood.

target: Se sientan en la arena a ver el atardecer.
english: They sit on the sand to watch the sunset.

target: Se organiza una fiesta de despedida.
english: A farewell party is organized.

target: Me tocó el asiento del medio.
english: I got the middle seat.
grammar: "Tocar" used impersonally: "me tocó" = it was my turn/I ended up with.

target: Si reservamos ahora, nos saldrá más barato.
english: If we book now, it will be cheaper for us.
grammar: "Salir" used impersonally: "nos saldrá" = it will come out to / it will cost us.

target: El vuelo fue cancelado debido a la niebla.
english: The flight was canceled due to the fog.
grammar: Passive with "ser" + past participle: "fue cancelado." Used for specific completed actions.

target: Se recomienda reservar con antelación.
english: It is recommended to book in advance.
grammar: Passive "se" construction: "se recomienda" = it is recommended. Very common in formal/impersonal travel language.

target: El informe fue revisado y aprobado por tres departamentos.
english: The report was reviewed and approved by three departments.

target: El salario fue negociado directamente con la dirección.
english: The salary was negotiated directly with management.

target: La cena fue preparada por mi cuñada.
english: Dinner was prepared by my sister-in-law.
grammar: Passive with ser: fue preparada (past participle agrees with subject)

target: La decisión fue tomada por toda la familia.
english: The decision was made by the whole family.
grammar: Ser + past participle passive: fue tomada (agrees with la decisión)

target: El álbum fue organizado por mi madre con mucho cariño.
english: The album was organized by my mother with a lot of love.

target: El edificio fue construido en el siglo diecinueve.
english: The building was constructed in the nineteenth century.

target: Se prohíbe fumar en esta zona.
english: Smoking is prohibited in this area.

target: Se busca un apartamento cerca del centro.
english: An apartment near the center is being sought.

target: El proyecto fue aprobado por el comité.
english: The project was approved by the committee.
grammar: Ser + participle = true passive, often with por + agent.

target: El acuerdo fue firmado por ambas partes.
english: The agreement was signed by both parties.

target: Se dice que va a nevar esta semana.
english: It is said it's going to snow this week.

target: Se aceptan tarjetas de crédito y débito.
english: Credit and debit cards are accepted.

target: Se espera que la situación mejore pronto.
english: It is expected that the situation will improve soon.

target: El informe fue redactado por todo el equipo.
english: The report was drafted by the entire team.

target: Se requiere experiencia previa para este puesto.
english: Previous experience is required for this position.

target: El contrato fue renovado por un año más.
english: The contract was renewed for one more year.

target: Se espera que las negociaciones concluyan esta semana.
english: It is expected that negotiations will conclude this week.

target: Se necesitan más voluntarios para el evento.
english: More volunteers are needed for the event.

target: Fue publicado en una revista internacional.
english: It was published in an international journal.

target: Habria que analizar el asunto con mas detenimiento.
english: The matter should be analyzed more carefully.
grammar: Habría que + infinitive = one should (impersonal, formal).

target: Siempre y cuando mantengamos el compromiso, lograremos los resultados deseados.
english: As long as we maintain our commitment, we will achieve the desired results.

target: En resumen, necesitamos más recursos y más tiempo.
english: In summary, we need more resources and more time.

target: En primer lugar, debemos analizar el problema a fondo.
english: First of all, we must analyze the problem thoroughly.

target: En conclusión, los datos confirman nuestra hipótesis inicial.
english: In conclusion, the data confirms our initial hypothesis.

target: El libro es interesante; sin embargo, el final me decepcionó un poco.
english: The book is interesting — however, the ending disappointed me a little.
grammar: double object pronouns: "se lo" = it to him/her. "Se" replaces "le" before "lo/la." "Hacer caso" = to pay attention.

target: Dado que la situación ha cambiado, debemos adaptar nuestra estrategia.
english: Given that the situation has changed, we must adapt our strategy.

target: En cambio, su hermana es muy extrovertida y sociable.
english: On the other hand, his sister is very outgoing and sociable.

target: Asimismo, es importante respetar los plazos de entrega establecidos.
english: Likewise, it is important to respect the established delivery deadlines.

target: A pesar de los obstáculos, logró cumplir su sueño.
english: Despite the obstacles, she managed to fulfill her dream.

target: Siempre que tenga la oportunidad, viajo a nuevos lugares.
english: Whenever I have the opportunity, I travel to new places.

target: En cuanto aterricemos, pasaremos por inmigración.
english: As soon as we land, we'll go through immigration.

target: La biodiversidad está en peligro debido a la deforestación.
english: Biodiversity is in danger due to deforestation.

target: Sin embargo, no todo fue negativo.
english: However, not everything was negative.

target: A pesar de todo, salió adelante.
english: Despite everything, she moved forward.

target: No obstante, hay que considerar otros factores.
english: Nevertheless, other factors must be considered.

target: Por lo tanto, la conclusión es evidente.
english: Therefore, the conclusion is evident.

target: Dado que no tenemos más opciones, aceptaremos la oferta.
english: Given that we have no more options, we'll accept the offer.

target: En cuanto al tema que mencionaste, estoy de acuerdo.
english: Regarding the topic you mentioned, I agree.

target: De hecho, es más complicado de lo que parece.
english: In fact, it's more complicated than it seems.

target: Si bien es cierto que cometí errores, aprendí.
english: While it's true I made mistakes, I learned.

target: A medida que pasa el tiempo, las cosas cambian.
english: As time passes, things change.

target: Siempre que tenga la oportunidad, viajo.
english: Whenever I have the opportunity, I travel.

target: A fin de que mejoren los resultados, debemos actuar.
english: In order for results to improve, we must act.

target: Con respecto a tu pregunta, no tengo la respuesta.
english: Regarding your question, I don't have the answer.

target: A fin de cuentas, todos somos humanos.
english: At the end of the day, we're all human.

target: Estaba cansado; sin embargo, siguió trabajando.
english: He was tired — however, he kept working.
grammar: "Sin embargo" = however. Formal connector that introduces a contrasting idea. Placed at the start of the clause.

target: No tengo mucho tiempo; no obstante, intentaré ayudarte.
english: I don't have much time — nevertheless, I'll try to help you.
grammar: "No obstante" = nevertheless. Very formal connector for contrast, common in written/academic Spanish.

target: A pesar de la lluvia, salimos a caminar.
english: Despite the rain, we went out for a walk.
grammar: A pesar de + noun — despite/in spite of

target: Llegué tarde; por lo tanto, me perdí el principio.
english: I arrived late — therefore, I missed the beginning.
grammar: "Por lo tanto" = therefore. Introduces a logical consequence of what was just said.

target: A pesar de que estudió mucho, no aprobó el examen.
english: Even though he studied a lot, he didn't pass the exam.
grammar: A pesar de que + verb — even though

target: No tenemos presupuesto; por consiguiente, el viaje se cancela.
english: We don't have budget — consequently, the trip is canceled.

target: A pesar de que no hablaba español, se hizo entender.
english: Even though he didn't speak Spanish, he made himself understood.

target: El vuelo se retrasó dos horas; por consiguiente, perdimos la conexión.
english: The flight was delayed two hours — consequently, we missed the connection.
grammar: "Por consiguiente" = consequently. Formal connector linking cause to effect.

target: No solo llegó tarde, sino que además no pidió disculpas.
english: Not only did he arrive late, but he also didn't apologize.
grammar: No solo... sino que además — not only... but also

target: Por un lado quiero ir; por otro lado, estoy muy cansado.
english: On the one hand I want to go — on the other hand, I'm very tired.
grammar: Por un lado... por otro lado — on one hand... on the other

target: No me llamó; es más, ni siquiera me mandó un mensaje.
english: He didn't call me — what's more, he didn't even send me a message.
grammar: "Es más" = what's more / moreover. Escalates or adds to a previous point.

target: Dado que no hay quórum, aplazamos la reunión.
english: Given that there's no quorum, we'll postpone the meeting.
grammar: "Dado que" = given that / since. Formal causal connector: "Dado que llueve, nos quedamos."

target: En cuanto al presupuesto, todavía estamos esperando aprobación.
english: Regarding the budget, we're still waiting for approval.
grammar: "En cuanto a" = regarding / as for. Introduces a new topic or shifts focus.

target: De hecho, es la mejor propuesta que hemos recibido.
english: In fact, it's the best proposal we've received.
grammar: "De hecho" = in fact / actually. Reinforces or contrasts with what was expected.

target: En definitiva, fue un viaje que mereció la pena.
english: All in all, it was a trip that was worth it.
grammar: "En definitiva" = all in all / in short. Used to summarize or conclude.

target: De todos modos, gracias por intentarlo.
english: Anyway, thanks for trying.

target: En primer lugar, quiero agradecer a todos por venir.
english: First of all, I want to thank everyone for coming.
grammar: "En primer lugar" = first of all. Sequence connector for organizing arguments.

target: A medida que pasa el tiempo, todo mejora.
english: As time goes by, everything gets better.
grammar: "A medida que" = as (gradual): "A medida que crecía, aprendía más." Shows two things progressing together.

target: Al fin y al cabo, lo que cuenta es la intención.
english: At the end of the day, what counts is the intention.

target: Estudié mucho; no obstante, el examen me pareció difícil.
english: I studied a lot — nevertheless, the exam seemed hard to me.

target: El restaurante es pequeño; no obstante, la comida es excelente.
english: The restaurant is small — nevertheless, the food is excellent.

target: A pesar de que hacía frío, fuimos a la playa.
english: Even though it was cold, we went to the beach.
grammar: A pesar de que + verb clause — despite the fact that

target: No obstante, los resultados del último mes fueron positivos.
english: Nevertheless, last month's results were positive.

target: A pesar de todo, seguimos adelante con el plan.
english: Despite everything, we're moving forward with the plan.

target: Era tarde; sin embargo, decidimos quedarnos un rato más.
english: It was late — however, we decided to stay a while longer.

target: Al fin y al cabo, lo importante es que estamos todos bien.
english: At the end of the day, the important thing is that we're all okay.

target: A pesar de la lluvia, disfrutamos de la excursión.
english: Despite the rain, we enjoyed the excursion.

target: Tan pronto como crucemos la frontera, cambiaremos dinero.
english: As soon as we cross the border, we'll exchange money.

target: A pesar de vivir lejos, nos vemos a menudo.
english: Despite living far away, we see each other often.

target: Dado que el bebé tiene fiebre, no iremos al parque.
english: Given that the baby has a fever, we won't go to the park.

target: Si bien la casa es pequeña, es suficiente para nosotros.
english: Although the house is small, it's enough for us.

target: Pese a las discusiones, siempre nos reconciliamos.
english: Despite the arguments, we always make up.

target: Siempre que viajamos en avión, reservamos con antelación.
english: Whenever we fly, we book in advance.

target: A pesar de la lluvia, visitamos todos los monumentos.
english: Despite the rain, we visited all the monuments.
grammar: "A pesar de" (despite/in spite of) — an advanced concessive connector.

target: No obstante las malas críticas, el hotel estaba bien.
english: Notwithstanding the bad reviews, the hotel was fine.
grammar: "No obstante" (nevertheless/notwithstanding) — formal concessive connector.

target: Si bien el hotel era caro, la ubicación lo compensaba.
english: Although the hotel was expensive, the location made up for it.
grammar: "Si bien" (although/even though) — a formal concessive that introduces a contrasting idea.

target: No solo visitamos la capital, sino también el interior.
english: We not only visited the capital, but also the interior.
grammar: "No solo... sino también" = not only... but also. Correlative connector for adding information.

target: A fin de ahorrar, decidimos viajar en temporada baja.
english: In order to save money, we decided to travel in the off-season.

target: Cabe destacar que la hospitalidad fue lo mejor del viaje.
english: It's worth noting that the hospitality was the best part of the trip.
grammar: "Cabe destacar que" (it's worth noting that) — formal connector used in written/spoken narratives.

target: Habida cuenta de la situación, decidimos acortar el viaje.
english: Given the situation, we decided to shorten the trip.
grammar: "Habida cuenta de" (given/considering) — very formal causal connector.

target: El equipo que lidera Juan está en primer lugar.
english: The team Juan leads is in first place.

target: Dado que no cumplieron el contrato, tomaremos medidas legales.
english: Given that they didn't fulfill the contract, we'll take legal action.

target: Siempre y cuando cumplan los objetivos, recibirán el bonus.
english: As long as they meet the targets, they'll receive the bonus.

target: No solo subieron los precios, sino que también redujeron la calidad.
english: Not only did they raise prices, but they also reduced quality.
grammar: No solo... sino que también: correlative connectors.

target: Si bien el informe es completo, falta el análisis financiero.
english: Although the report is complete, the financial analysis is missing.
grammar: Si bien: formal concessive connector = although.

target: Habida cuenta de la situación, se tomaron medidas extraordinarias.
english: Given the situation, extraordinary measures were taken.
grammar: Habida cuenta de: formal connector meaning 'given' or 'considering'.

target: A pesar de la distancia, llamo a mis padres cada día.
english: Despite the distance, I call my parents every day.
grammar: A pesar de = despite, in spite of (+ noun or infinitive)

target: Dado que los niños están de vacaciones, pasamos más tiempo juntos.
english: Given that the kids are on vacation, we spend more time together.
grammar: Dado que = given that, since (formal connector + indicative)

target: A pesar de las dificultades, sacamos adelante a la familia.
english: Despite the difficulties, we raised the family.
grammar: Sacar adelante = to raise/support (through hardship)

target: A pesar de la lluvia, decidimos salir a caminar.
english: Despite the rain, we decided to go for a walk.
grammar: A pesar de + noun = despite/in spite of.

target: Siempre que pueda, iré a visitarte.
english: Whenever I can, I will go visit you.

target: A medida que pasa el tiempo, las cosas mejoran.
english: As time goes by, things improve.

target: Dado que no hay suficiente presupuesto, el viaje se canceló.
english: Given that there isn't enough budget, the trip was cancelled.

target: A fin de cuentas, lo importante es estar juntos.
english: At the end of the day, the important thing is being together.

target: Sin embargo, creo que deberíamos intentarlo.
english: However, I think we should try.

target: Es decir, tendremos que buscar otra opción.
english: That is to say, we'll have to look for another option.

target: Aun así, no me arrepiento de mi decisión.
english: Even so, I don't regret my decision.

target: Siempre y cuando cumplas tu parte, yo cumpliré la mía.
english: As long as you do your part, I'll do mine.

target: En primer lugar, debemos analizar los datos.
english: First of all, we must analyze the data.

target: Debido a la falta de personal, cerraron temprano.
english: Due to the lack of staff, they closed early.

target: Por lo tanto, la reunión se ha pospuesto.
english: Therefore, the meeting has been postponed.
grammar: Por lo tanto = therefore, formal consequence connector.

target: No obstante, los resultados fueron positivos.
english: Nevertheless, the results were positive.

target: En conclusión, el proyecto fue un éxito total.
english: In conclusion, the project was a total success.

target: Por consiguiente, debemos tomar medidas inmediatas.
english: Consequently, we must take immediate measures.

target: Si bien el precio es alto, la calidad lo justifica.
english: Although the price is high, the quality justifies it.

target: Pese a las dificultades, lograron completar la misión.
english: Despite the difficulties, they managed to complete the mission.

target: A pesar de que llueva, iremos a la excursión.
english: Even though it may rain, we will go on the excursion.

target: Siempre que necesites algo, llámame.
english: Whenever you need something, call me.

target: No obstante, seguimos adelante con el plan.
english: However, we're moving forward with the plan.

target: Sin embargo, la decisión fue difícil.
english: However, the decision was difficult.
