target: No obstante, el resultado fue positivo.
english: Nevertheless, the result was positive.

target: Sin embargo, decidieron continuar con el proyecto.
english: However, they decided to continue with the project.

target: No obstante, el equipo logró sus metas.
english: Nevertheless, the team achieved its goals.

target: A pesar de la distancia, nos vemos mucho.
english: Despite the distance, we see each other often.

target: Sin embargo, prefiero quedarme en casa hoy.
english: However, I prefer to stay home today.

target: Salí corriendo porque el autobús estaba a punto de irse.
english: I ran out because the bus was about to leave.
grammar: "Salí corriendo" — the gerund (-ando/-iendo) after a verb of motion describes HOW the action was done. Like English "I ran out" but literally "I left running."

target: El bebé acaba de aprender a caminar.
english: The baby just learned to walk.

target: Estoy orgulloso de mis hijos porque se esfuerzan mucho en los estudios.
english: I'm proud of my children because they try very hard in their studies.
grammar: "llevar" + time + gerund = to have been doing for (duration). No present perfect needed: "We've been living here for ten years."

target: ¿Este tren va a la estación central?
english: Does this train go to the central station?
grammar: "aprender a" + infinitive = to learn to. Many verbs need specific prepositions before infinitives: empezar a, dejar de, tratar de.

target: El semáforo está en rojo, espera antes de cruzar.
english: The traffic light is red, wait before crossing.
grammar: "llevar" + time + gerund = have been doing for. "Llevo dos horas" = I've been at it for two hours. Very common alternative to present perfect.

target: La puerta de embarque cierra treinta minutos antes de la salida.
english: The boarding gate closes thirty minutes before departure.
grammar: "acabar de" + infinitive = to have just (done). "Acabo de comer" = I just ate. Only works in present/imperfect.

target: La beca me cubre la matrícula y parte del alojamiento.
english: The scholarship covers my tuition and part of my accommodation.
grammar: "acabar de" + infinitive = to have just. "Ascender" (e→ie) = to promote. "Ascenderme" — pronoun attached to infinitive.

target: Me resulta difícil concentrarme cuando hay mucho ruido en la oficina.
english: I find it hard to concentrate when there's a lot of noise in the office.
grammar: "llevar" + gerund + "desde" = have been doing since. "Llevar" for duration is a key alternative to present perfect.

target: Acabo de recibir un correo electrónico muy importante.
english: I just received a very important email.

target: La brecha salarial entre hombres y mujeres sigue siendo preocupante.
english: The wage gap between men and women remains concerning.

target: Los atletas entrenan durante años para competir en los Juegos Olímpicos.
english: Athletes train for years to compete in the Olympic Games.
grammar: "llevar" + time + gerund = have been doing for. "Practicar" — no preposition before the activity.

target: Lleva lloviendo desde hace tres días sin parar.
english: It has been raining for three days without stopping.

target: Llevo trabajando aquí desde hace cinco años.
english: I've been working here for five years.
grammar: "Llevar" + gerund — duration of ongoing action.

target: Llevo dos años estudiando español.
english: I've been studying Spanish for two years.
grammar: "Llevar" + time + gerund — duration of ongoing action.

target: Llevo toda la mañana intentando arreglar la lavadora.
english: I've spent all morning trying to fix the washing machine.

target: Acabo de enterarme de la noticia.
english: I just found out about the news.
grammar: "Acabar de" + infinitive — to have just done something.

target: La brecha digital sigue siendo un problema en muchos países.
english: The digital divide continues to be a problem in many countries.

target: Saldremos cuando deje de llover.
english: We'll leave when it stops raining.

target: La conciliación laboral y familiar sigue siendo un reto.
english: Work-life balance continues to be a challenge.

target: Llevo tres años estudiando español.
english: I've been studying Spanish for three years.
grammar: "Llevar" + time + gerund — ongoing duration.

target: ¿Cuánto tiempo llevas viviendo aquí?
english: How long have you been living here?

target: Acabo de llegar.
english: I just arrived.
grammar: "Acabar de" + infinitive — to have just done.

target: Acaba de llamar tu madre.
english: Your mother just called.

target: El tren está a punto de salir.
english: The train is about to leave.

target: Te prometo que no volverá a pasar.
english: I promise it won't happen again.

target: Llevo años intentando que me asciendan.
english: I've been trying to get promoted for years.
grammar: Llevar + time + gerund — ongoing duration

target: Descansa un poco, llevas horas trabajando.
english: Rest a bit, you've been working for hours.

target: A ver si quedamos un día de estos y nos ponemos al día.
english: Let's see if we meet up one of these days and catch up.

target: Llevo meses dándole vueltas al asunto sin llegar a una conclusión.
english: I've been going back and forth on the issue for months without reaching a conclusion.
grammar: Darle vueltas a algo — to mull something over, to keep thinking about it

target: Llevo años queriendo aprender a tocar la guitarra.
english: I've been wanting to learn to play the guitar for years.

target: Acabo de mandar el correo con los documentos.
english: I just sent the email with the documents.

target: Llevo viajando por España tres semanas.
english: I've been traveling around Spain for three weeks.
grammar: "Llevar" + gerund expresses duration: "I've been doing something for X time."

target: Deja de hablar y escucha.
english: Stop talking and listen.

target: Acabo de llegar; dame un minuto.
english: I just arrived — give me a minute.
grammar: Acabar de + infinitive = to have just done something.

target: Se puso a llorar cuando recibió la noticia.
english: She started to cry when she received the news.
grammar: Ponerse a + infinitive = to suddenly start doing something.

target: Dejó de fumar hace cinco años.
english: He stopped smoking five years ago.
grammar: Dejar de + infinitive = to stop doing something.

target: Volvió a llamarme a las diez de la noche.
english: He called me again at ten at night.
grammar: Volver a + infinitive = to do something again.

target: Sigue lloviendo desde esta mañana.
english: It's been raining since this morning.

target: Anda buscando trabajo desde que lo despidieron.
english: He's been looking for a job since they fired him.
grammar: Andar + gerund = to go around doing something (habitual/ongoing).

target: Llevo dos horas esperando el autobús.
english: I've been waiting for the bus for two hours.

target: Se puso a gritar sin ninguna razón.
english: He started shouting for no reason.

target: Sigo pensando que fue una buena decisión.
english: I still think it was a good decision.
grammar: Seguir + gerund = to keep/continue doing something.

target: Anda diciendo por ahí que va a renunciar.
english: He's going around saying he's going to resign.

target: Dejaron de fabricar ese modelo hace años.
english: They stopped manufacturing that model years ago.

target: Sigue trabajando en la misma empresa desde que era joven.
english: He's still working at the same company since he was young.

target: Llevo toda la semana sin dormir bien.
english: I've gone the whole week without sleeping well.

target: Acaban de anunciar que el vuelo está retrasado.
english: They just announced that the flight is delayed.

target: Cuando vio el resultado, se puso a saltar de alegría.
english: When he saw the result, he started jumping for joy.

target: No dejo de pensar en lo que me dijiste.
english: I can't stop thinking about what you told me.

target: Llevo meses intentando aprender a tocar la guitarra.
english: I've been trying to learn to play the guitar for months.

target: Se pusieron a discutir en medio del restaurante.
english: They started arguing in the middle of the restaurant.

target: Está a punto de empezar la película.
english: The movie is about to start.
grammar: Estar a punto de + infinitive = to be about to do something.

target: No deja de hablar ni un segundo.
english: He doesn't stop talking for even a second.

target: Sigue nevando; no podremos salir hoy.
english: It keeps snowing — we won't be able to go out today.

target: Llevo un rato intentando llamarte.
english: I've been trying to call you for a while.

target: Acabo de hablar con el médico y todo está bien.
english: I just spoke with the doctor and everything is fine.

target: Lleva toda la vida dedicándose a la enseñanza.
english: He has spent his whole life dedicated to teaching.

target: Estaba a punto de salir cuando empezó a llover.
english: I was about to leave when it started raining.

target: Andan diciendo que van a cerrar la fábrica.
english: They're going around saying they're going to close the factory.

target: No dejes de practicar o perderás lo aprendido.
english: Don't stop practicing or you'll lose what you've learned.

target: El perro sigue ladrando aunque ya no hay nadie.
english: The dog keeps barking even though there's nobody there.

target: Llevamos horas conduciendo sin parar.
english: We've been driving for hours without stopping.

target: Mencionó que su hermana acababa de tener un bebé.
english: She mentioned that her sister had just had a baby.

target: Llevo tres años viviendo en Madrid.
english: I've been living in Madrid for three years.

target: Acabo de terminar el informe.
english: I just finished the report.

target: Ella se puso a llorar de repente.
english: She suddenly started crying.

target: Dejé de fumar hace un año.
english: I quit smoking a year ago.

target: Sigue lloviendo desde la mañana.
english: It's still raining since morning.

target: Llevo meses intentando encontrar un buen dentista.
english: I've been trying to find a good dentist for months.
grammar: Llevar + time + gerund = have been doing for [duration].

target: Se puso a cantar en medio del supermercado.
english: He started singing in the middle of the supermarket.

target: Ya lleva un rato esperando en la puerta.
english: He's been waiting at the door for a while now.

target: Acabamos de aterrizar y ya estamos haciendo cola.
english: We just landed and we're already waiting in line.

target: Llevo tres horas conduciendo sin parar.
english: I've been driving for three hours without stopping.

target: Dejamos de buscar hotel y decidimos acampar.
english: We stopped looking for a hotel and decided to camp.

target: Acabo de hablar con mi madre y esta muy contenta.
english: I just spoke to my mother and she's very happy.

target: Mi abuela sigue tejiendo bufandas para toda la familia.
english: My grandmother keeps knitting scarves for the whole family.

target: Acabo de enviar el correo con los documentos adjuntos.
english: I just sent the email with the attached documents.

target: Se puso a gritar cuando vio los resultados trimestrales.
english: He started shouting when he saw the quarterly results.

target: Dejo de responder correos a las seis para desconectar.
english: I stop answering emails at six to disconnect.

target: Andan buscando un programador con experiencia en Python.
english: They're looking for a programmer with experience in Python.
grammar: Andar + gerund = to go around doing (ongoing search/activity).

target: Según el calendario, la próxima luna llena es el dieciocho de abril.
english: According to the calendar, the next full moon is on the eighteenth of April.
grammar: "cuánto" for amount/price. Agrees with noun: "¿cuántos años?" (masc pl), "¿cuántas personas?" (fem pl).

target: El conductor del autobús nos avisó de que era la última parada.
english: The bus driver told us it was the last stop.

target: El asesor financiero me recomendó diversificar mis inversiones para reducir el riesgo.
english: The financial advisor recommended I diversify my investments to reduce risk.

target: El médico me dijo que tengo la gripe.
english: The doctor told me I have the flu.

target: El dentista me dijo que tengo que sacarme un diente.
english: The dentist told me I have to get a tooth pulled.

target: La doctora me dijo que el dolor de estómago podría ser estrés.
english: The doctor told me the stomachache could be stress.

target: El cardiólogo le recomendó que redujera el consumo de sal y grasas.
english: The cardiologist recommended that he reduce his salt and fat intake.

target: El especialista le advirtió que sin tratamiento la enfermedad podría empeorar.
english: The specialist warned him that without treatment the illness could worsen.

target: El arrepentimiento me persigue desde que tomé aquella decisión sin pensar en las consecuencias.
english: Regret has haunted me since I made that decision without thinking about the consequences.

target: Mi abuelo me contó que durante la guerra no había suficiente comida.
english: My grandfather told me that during the war there wasn't enough food.

target: Aquella noche llovía mucho y decidimos quedarnos en casa.
english: That night it was raining a lot and we decided to stay home.

target: Ella me dijo que había vivido en tres países diferentes antes de cumplir treinta.
english: She told me she had lived in three different countries before turning thirty.

target: Prometió que llegaría antes de las seis.
english: He promised that he would arrive before six.

target: Dijo que nos avisaría cuando estuviera listo.
english: He said he would let us know when he was ready.

target: Es mejor llegar a un acuerdo que seguir discutiendo.
english: It is better to reach an agreement than to keep arguing.
grammar: indirect speech: "le pregunté si" = I asked him/her if. "Quería" — tense shifts back (wants → wanted) in reported speech.

target: El bilingüismo ofrece ventajas cognitivas según los estudios recientes.
english: Bilingualism offers cognitive advantages according to recent studies.

target: Según los datos disponibles, la situación ha mejorado significativamente.
english: According to available data, the situation has improved significantly.

target: La cortesía verbal varía enormemente según la cultura y el contexto.
english: Verbal politeness varies enormously depending on culture and context.

target: La Navidad se celebra de formas muy distintas según la región.
english: Christmas is celebrated in very different ways depending on the region.

target: Me tomó el pelo cuando dijo que había ganado la lotería.
english: He was pulling my leg when he said he had won the lottery.

target: Me dijo que venía mañana, pero luego me confirmó que no podía.
english: He told me he was coming tomorrow, but then he confirmed he couldn't.

target: Me dijeron que el vuelo había sido cancelado.
english: They told me the flight had been canceled.
grammar: Reported speech with pluperfect — "había sido" (had been).

target: En aquella empresa no pagaban bien.
english: At that company, they didn't pay well.

target: No nos avisaron del cambio.
english: They didn't tell us about the change.
grammar: "Nos" = to us (indirect object): "Nos dijo la verdad" = He told us the truth.

target: Mi madre me dijo que no saliera tan tarde.
english: My mother told me not to go out so late.

target: El profesor nos recomendó que leyéramos ese libro.
english: The teacher recommended that we read that book.

target: Me dijo que no me moviera de allí.
english: He told me not to move from there.

target: Me pidió que le leyera un cuento antes de dormir.
english: He/she asked me to read a story before bed.

target: Me dijeron que el museo cierra a las cinco.
english: They told me the museum closes at five.

target: Nos informaron del cambio de horario.
english: They informed us of the schedule change.

target: Me comunicaron la decisión por correo.
english: They communicated the decision to me by email.

target: El camarero nos recomendó el pescado.
english: The waiter recommended the fish to us.

target: El conserje me recomendó un restaurante.
english: The concierge recommended a restaurant to me.

target: El conserje sugirió que fuéramos al castillo.
english: The concierge suggested we go to the castle.

target: Me dijeron que el mejor restaurante está en la plaza.
english: They told me the best restaurant is in the square.

target: La agencia nos dijo que las excursiones se llenarían pronto.
english: The agency told us the excursions would fill up soon.

target: A raíz de aquella experiencia, siempre contrato seguro de viaje.
english: As a result of that experience, I always get travel insurance.

target: No me avisaron del cambio de horario.
english: They didn't tell me about the schedule change.

target: Esa semana tuve tres entrevistas seguidas.
english: That week I had three interviews in a row.

target: La indemnización fue calculada según los años de servicio.
english: The severance was calculated based on years of service.

target: Los informes fueron archivados según el protocolo establecido.
english: The reports were filed according to the established protocol.

target: Mi padre me dijo que estudiara más.
english: My father told me to study more.

target: Se enfadó porque nadie le avisó del cambio.
english: He got angry because nobody warned him about the change.

target: Dijo que vendría a las ocho.
english: He said he would come at eight.
grammar: Direct → indirect: 'vendré' becomes 'vendría' (tense backshift).

target: Nos contó que había vivido en Japón durante tres años.
english: He told us he had lived in Japan for three years.
grammar: Vivió → había vivido (preterite backshifts to pluperfect).

target: Me aseguró que todo estaba bajo control.
english: He assured me that everything was under control.

target: Comentó que su madre había estado enferma esa semana.
english: He mentioned that his mother had been sick that week.

target: Me advirtió que tuviera cuidado con el hielo.
english: He warned me to be careful with the ice.
grammar: Advirtió que + subjunctive for indirect commands/warnings.

target: Dijo que se iba de vacaciones al día siguiente.
english: She said she was going on vacation the next day.
grammar: 'Mañana' becomes 'al día siguiente' in reported speech.

target: Me prometió que no volvería a llegar tarde.
english: He promised me he wouldn't be late again.

target: Me recordó que tenía que entregar el informe esa semana.
english: She reminded me I had to submit the report that week.
grammar: 'Esta semana' becomes 'esa semana' in reported speech (demonstrative shift).

target: Dijeron que el problema se resolvería en unos días.
english: They said the problem would be resolved in a few days.

target: Nos comunicaron que las oficinas cerrarían a las tres.
english: They informed us that the offices would close at three.

target: Según los vecinos, el ruido venía del tercer piso.
english: According to the neighbors, the noise was coming from the third floor.

target: Me explicaron que el proceso tardaría varios meses.
english: They explained to me that the process would take several months.

target: Nos avisó de que habría retrasos en el servicio.
english: He warned us there would be delays in the service.

target: Dijo que lo pensaría y me daría una respuesta.
english: She said she would think about it and give me an answer.

target: Según mis padres, antes aquí todo era campo.
english: According to my parents, before, all this was countryside.

target: Me aseguró que el paquete llegaría antes del viernes.
english: He assured me the package would arrive before Friday.

target: Comentaron que la obra del teatro había sido magnífica.
english: They commented that the theater play had been magnificent.

target: Me recomendó que leyera ese libro cuanto antes.
english: She recommended I read that book as soon as possible.

target: Según el periódico, la economía mejoraría el próximo trimestre.
english: According to the newspaper, the economy would improve next quarter.

target: Me dijo que aquella había sido la mejor decisión de su vida.
english: She told me that had been the best decision of her life.
grammar: 'Esa/esta' becomes 'aquella' in reported speech (distal demonstrative).

target: Aseguró que no sabía nada del asunto.
english: He assured that he knew nothing about the matter.

target: Dijo que vendría mañana por la tarde.
english: He said he'd come tomorrow afternoon.

target: Me dijo que no se encontraba bien y que preferia quedarse en casa.
english: He told me he wasn't feeling well and preferred to stay home.
grammar: Reported speech: present → imperfect (se encuentra → se encontraba).

target: Le pregunte si habia visto la pelicula y me dijo que no.
english: I asked him if he had seen the movie and he said no.

target: Dijo que habia intentado llamarme pero que no le contestaba.
english: He said he had tried to call me but I wasn't answering.

target: Le conte que ya no vivia en aquella ciudad.
english: I told him I no longer lived in that city.
grammar: Demonstrative shift: esta ciudad → aquella ciudad in reported speech.

target: Segun ella, el restaurante era el mejor de la zona.
english: According to her, the restaurant was the best in the area.

target: El guia nos dijo que el museo cerraba a las cinco.
english: The guide told us the museum closed at five.

target: Preguntamos si habia habitaciones disponibles y nos dijeron que no.
english: We asked if there were rooms available and they told us no.

target: Segun el camarero, aquel plato era la especialidad de la casa.
english: According to the waiter, that dish was the house specialty.

target: Nos avisaron de que ibamos a llegar tarde si no saliamos ya.
english: They warned us we would arrive late if we didn't leave right away.

target: Mi madre me dijo que mi hermano habia llamado mientras estaba fuera.
english: My mother told me my brother had called while I was out.

target: El cliente nos dijo que no estaba satisfecho con el servicio.
english: The client told us he wasn't satisfied with the service.

target: Se quedo de piedra cuando le dijeron que lo habian ascendido.
english: He was stunned when they told him he had been promoted.

target: Mi madre dijo que vendría a visitarnos el domingo.
english: My mother said she would come visit us on Sunday.
grammar: Reported speech: present "vendrá" → imperfect "vendría" after "dijo que."

target: El profesor preguntó si habíamos entendido la lección.
english: The teacher asked if we had understood the lesson.
grammar: Reported question with "si" (if/whether). Preterite perfect → pluperfect.

target: Mi jefe explicó que el proyecto cambiaría de fecha.
english: My boss explained that the project would change dates.
grammar: Reported speech: "cambiará" → "cambiaría" (future → conditional) after "explicó que."

target: Laura comentó que estaba pensando en mudarse.
english: Laura mentioned that she was thinking about moving.
grammar: Reported speech: "estoy pensando" → "estaba pensando" (present → imperfect).

target: El médico respondió que los resultados eran normales.
english: The doctor replied that the results were normal.
grammar: Reported speech: "son normales" → "eran normales" (present → imperfect).

target: Mi hermano afirmó que no sabía nada del tema.
english: My brother claimed he didn't know anything about the topic.
grammar: Reported speech with "afirmó que." "No sé" → "no sabía" (present → imperfect).

target: La vecina aseguró que había oído un ruido extraño.
english: The neighbor assured us she had heard a strange noise.
grammar: Reported speech: "he oído" → "había oído" (present perfect → pluperfect).

target: El guía mencionó que el museo cerraba a las cinco.
english: The guide mentioned that the museum closed at five.
grammar: Reported speech: "cierra" → "cerraba" (present → imperfect) after "mencionó que."

target: Mi amiga contó que había viajado sola por Asia.
english: My friend told us she had traveled alone through Asia.
grammar: Reported speech: "viajé" → "había viajado" (preterite → pluperfect) after "contó que."

target: El recepcionista dijo que no quedaban habitaciones.
english: The receptionist said there were no rooms left.
grammar: Reported speech: "no quedan" → "no quedaban" (present → imperfect).

target: Mi padre preguntó qué hora era.
english: My father asked what time it was.
grammar: Reported question with "qué." "¿Qué hora es?" → "qué hora era."

target: La profesora preguntó cómo se escribía esa palabra.
english: The teacher asked how that word was spelled.
grammar: Reported question with "cómo." "¿Cómo se escribe?" → "cómo se escribía."

target: El piloto explicó que aterrizaríamos con retraso.
english: The pilot explained that we would land late.
grammar: Reported speech: "aterrizaremos" → "aterrizaríamos" (future → conditional).

target: Mi compañero comentó que necesitaba más tiempo para el informe.
english: My colleague mentioned that he needed more time for the report.
grammar: Reported speech: "necesito" → "necesitaba" (present → imperfect).

target: La directora afirmó que los sueldos subirían el próximo año.
english: The director stated that salaries would go up next year.
grammar: Reported speech: "subirán" → "subirían" (future → conditional) after "afirmó que."

target: El camarero preguntó si queríamos postre.
english: The waiter asked if we wanted dessert.
grammar: Reported question with "si": "¿Quieren postre?" → "si queríamos postre."

target: Mi abuela contó que de joven trabajaba en una fábrica.
english: My grandmother told us that when she was young she worked in a factory.
grammar: Reported speech: both verbs stay imperfect for habitual past narration.

target: El mecánico aseguró que el coche estaría listo el viernes.
english: The mechanic assured us the car would be ready on Friday.
grammar: Reported speech: "estará" → "estaría" (future → conditional) after "aseguró que."

target: Mi hijo dijo que quería un perro para su cumpleaños.
english: My son said he wanted a dog for his birthday.
grammar: Reported speech: "quiero" → "quería" (present → imperfect) after "dijo que."

target: La enfermera respondió que el doctor llegaría en diez minutos.
english: The nurse replied that the doctor would arrive in ten minutes.
grammar: Reported speech: "llegará" → "llegaría" (future → conditional).

target: El taxista mencionó que conocía un atajo para evitar el tráfico.
english: The taxi driver mentioned he knew a shortcut to avoid traffic.
grammar: Reported speech: "conozco" → "conocía" (present → imperfect) after "mencionó que."

target: Mi esposa preguntó si yo había comprado la leche.
english: My wife asked if I had bought the milk.
grammar: Reported question: "¿Has comprado?" → "si había comprado" (pres. perf. → pluperfect).

target: El entrenador explicó que debíamos calentar antes de correr.
english: The coach explained that we had to warm up before running.
grammar: Reported speech: "deben" → "debíamos" (present → imperfect) after "explicó que."

target: Lavo la ropa una vez a la semana.
english: I do laundry once a week.

target: Me acostumbré a madrugar cuando empecé este trabajo.
english: I got used to waking up early when I started this job.
grammar: "Acostumbrarse a" + infinitive = to get used to. The preterite "me acostumbré" marks a completed change. Compare with "estar acostumbrado a" = to be used to.

target: Compré un millón de cosas en la tienda.
english: I bought a million things at the store.
grammar: literally "What hour is it?" Use "es" for 1:00 ("es la una") and "son" for all others ("son las cinco").

target: La mitad de los estudiantes aprobó el examen.
english: Half of the students passed the exam.

target: Mis padres están casados desde hace treinta años.
english: My parents have been married for thirty years.
grammar: "ser" for personality traits and inherent qualities. "Están cariñosos" would mean they're being affectionate right now (unusual behavior).

target: Mi novio me regaló flores por nuestro aniversario.
english: My boyfriend gave me flowers for our anniversary.

target: Heredé los ojos azules de mi abuela materna.
english: I inherited my blue eyes from my maternal grandmother.

target: El grifo de la cocina gotea desde ayer.
english: The kitchen faucet has been dripping since yesterday.

target: Limpié los cristales de las ventanas con vinagre.
english: I cleaned the window glass with vinegar.

target: Después de la lluvia salió un arcoíris precioso.
english: After the rain a beautiful rainbow came out.

target: El médico me escribió una receta para la medicina.
english: The doctor wrote me a prescription for the medicine.

target: Se me hinchó la pierna después de la picadura.
english: My leg swelled up after the sting.

target: Me recetaron un antibiótico para la infección de oído.
english: They prescribed me an antibiotic for the ear infection.

target: La enfermera le cambió el vendaje de la herida.
english: The nurse changed the bandage on his wound.

target: Me mareé al levantarme demasiado rápido de la silla.
english: I got dizzy from standing up too quickly from the chair.

target: El accidente le dejó una cicatriz en la frente.
english: The accident left a scar on his forehead.

target: Las protestas estudiantiles exigieron una reforma del sistema educativo.
english: Student protests demanded a reform of the education system.

target: El muralismo mexicano reflejó los ideales de la revolución.
english: Mexican muralism reflected the ideals of the revolution.

target: El equipo femenino de balonmano ganó el campeonato europeo.
english: The women's handball team won the European championship.

target: Por mucho que lo intentes, no conseguirás cambiar su forma de pensar.
english: No matter how much you try, you won't manage to change their way of thinking.

target: Mi madre insiste en que comamos juntos los domingos.
english: My mother insists that we eat together on Sundays.

target: Por más que estudie, siempre hay algo nuevo que aprender.
english: No matter how much I study, there's always something new to learn.

target: Por mucho que discutamos, al final siempre nos reconciliamos.
english: No matter how much we argue, in the end we always reconcile.

target: Por mucho que insistas, no voy a cambiar de opinión.
english: No matter how much you insist, I'm not going to change my mind.

target: Que yo sepa, la tienda cierra a las ocho.
english: As far as I know, the store closes at eight.

target: La receta que encontré en internet salió muy bien.
english: The recipe I found online turned out very well.

target: Por más que intenté, no pude convencerlo.
english: No matter how much I tried, I couldn't convince him.
grammar: "Por más que" + subjunctive = no matter how much: "Por más que intento, no puedo." Concessive construction.

target: Por mucho que lo intente, no consigo entender este programa.
english: No matter how much I try, I can't understand this program.

target: Que yo sepa, la reunión sigue en pie.
english: As far as I know, the meeting is still on.
grammar: "Que yo sepa" = as far as I know. Uses subjunctive of saber because it marks uncertainty.

target: Me quedé de piedra cuando me dieron la noticia.
english: I was stunned when they gave me the news.

target: Con las manos en la masa lo pillaron copiando.
english: They caught him red-handed cheating (lit. with hands in the dough).

target: Comimos en un sitio nuevo cerca de la oficina.
english: We ate at a new place near the office.

target: Mi tío nos invitó a su casa de campo.
english: My uncle invited us to his country house.

target: Pasamos un fin de semana increíble en la playa.
english: We had an incredible weekend at the beach.

target: El equipo ganó el partido por tres a uno.
english: The team won the game three to one.

target: Antes yo fumaba, pero lo dejé hace cinco años.
english: I used to smoke, but I quit five years ago.

target: Ella dejó el trabajo para cuidar a su madre.
english: She left work to take care of her mother.

target: Las cosas que me contó me dejaron sin palabras.
english: The things he told me left me speechless.

target: Pase lo que pase, cuenta conmigo.
english: Whatever happens, count on me.

target: El cliente está más satisfecho que el mes pasado.
english: The client is more satisfied than last month.

target: El jardín está más bonito que el año pasado.
english: The garden looks nicer than last year.

target: Los juguetes que le regalaron al niño son educativos.
english: The toys they gave the child are educational.

target: El jardín que plantó mi madre tiene muchas flores.
english: The garden my mother planted has many flowers.

target: Por mucho que busque, no encuentro un hotel barato.
english: No matter how much I search, I can't find a cheap hotel.

target: Por mucho que insista, mi hijo no quiere comer verduras.
english: No matter how much I insist, my son won't eat vegetables.

target: No es que no me guste viajar, sino que prefiero planificarlo bien.
english: It's not that I don't like traveling, but I prefer to plan it well.

target: Por más que busqué, no encontré un vuelo directo a esa isla.
english: No matter how much I searched, I couldn't find a direct flight to that island.

target: Por mucho que me cueste admitirlo, ese hotel era un desastre.
english: As much as it pains me to admit it, that hotel was a disaster.

target: Pase lo que pase, no nos vamos sin ver el atardecer desde el faro.
english: Whatever happens, we're not leaving without seeing the sunset from the lighthouse.

target: Sea lo que sea que decidan los niños, les apoyaremos.
english: Whatever the kids decide, we'll support them.

target: Por mucho que cueste, la educación de los hijos es prioritaria.
english: No matter how much it costs, children's education is a priority.

target: Sea como sea, resolveremos esto en familia.
english: No matter what, we'll resolve this as a family.

target: Llegué al aeropuerto a las seis de la mañana.
english: I arrived at the airport at six in the morning.

target: Los niños jugaron en el jardín toda la tarde.
english: The kids played in the garden all afternoon.

target: El GPS nos llevó por una ruta más larga.
english: The GPS took us on a longer route.

target: El guía nos llevó por las calles más bonitas.
english: The guide took us through the prettiest streets.

target: Salimos del hotel a las siete de la mañana.
english: We left the hotel at seven in the morning.

target: Pedí un plato sin gluten y lo prepararon enseguida.
english: I ordered a gluten-free dish and they prepared it right away.

target: Por más que intentamos, no conseguimos entradas.
english: No matter how much we tried, we couldn't get tickets.

target: Por mucho que investigue, no encuentro vuelos baratos.
english: No matter how much I research, I can't find cheap flights.

target: Por mucho que le insistí, el recepcionista se negó a devolverme el dinero.
english: No matter how much I insisted, the receptionist refused to refund me.

target: Por mucho que planifiques, siempre surgirá algo inesperado.
english: No matter how much you plan, something unexpected will always come up.

target: Por más que intenten convencerme, no cambiaré de opinión.
english: No matter how much they try to convince me, I won't change my mind.

target: Así las cosas, la empresa decidió cerrar la filial.
english: As things stood, the company decided to close the subsidiary.

target: Los primos jugaron en el jardín toda la tarde.
english: The cousins played in the garden all afternoon.

target: Mi abuelo me contó cómo conoció a mi abuela.
english: My grandfather told me how he met my grandmother.

target: Los niños se lavaron los dientes antes de dormir.
english: The kids brushed their teeth before bed.

target: Mi madre se enfadó porque no lavé los platos.
english: My mother got angry because I didn't wash the dishes.

target: Mi padre nos enseñó a pescar en el río.
english: My father taught us to fish in the river.

target: Mis padres se mudaron al campo cuando se jubilaron.
english: My parents moved to the countryside when they retired.

target: El hecho de que tengamos hijos no significa que no podamos salir.
english: The fact that we have kids doesn't mean we can't go out.

target: Por más que lo intento, no consigo que mi hijo ordene su cuarto.
english: No matter how much I try, I can't get my son to tidy his room.

target: Sea como sea la situación, la familia tiene que estar unida.
english: Whatever the situation, the family has to stick together.

target: Por más que mi madre se empeñe, los tiempos han cambiado.
english: No matter how much my mother insists, times have changed.

target: Por mucho que crezcan, siempre serán mis niños pequeños.
english: No matter how much they grow, they'll always be my little kids.

target: Por mucho que se esfuerze, la situación no mejora.
english: No matter how much he tries, the situation doesn't improve.

target: Sea como sea, debemos encontrar una solución antes del viernes.
english: Be that as it may, we must find a solution before Friday.

target: Por más que intenté convencerla, no cambió de opinión.
english: No matter how much I tried to convince her, she didn't change her mind.

target: Sea como sea, tenemos que terminar hoy.
english: Be that as it may, we have to finish today.

target: Hagas lo que hagas, te voy a apoyar.
english: Whatever you do, I'm going to support you.

target: Por mucho que lo niegue, todos sabemos la verdad.
english: No matter how much he denies it, we all know the truth.

target: No es que sea difícil, es que requiere tiempo.
english: It's not that it's difficult, it's that it requires time.

target: Que yo recuerde, nunca hemos tenido ese problema.
english: As far as I remember, we've never had that problem.

target: Por poco que duerma, siempre se levanta de buen humor.
english: However little he sleeps, he always wakes up in a good mood.

target: Dejamos de vernos cuando ella se mudó al extranjero.
english: We stopped seeing each other when she moved abroad.

target: Como si nada hubiera pasado, sonrió.
english: As if nothing had happened, she smiled.

target: Sea como sea, tenemos que tomar una decision.
english: However it may be, we have to make a decision.

target: Pase lo que pase, no pierdas el pasaporte.
english: Whatever happens, don't lose the passport.

target: No es que no me guste tu novio, es que apenas lo conozco.
english: It's not that I don't like your boyfriend, it's that I barely know him.

target: Sea lo que sea lo que decidan, los vamos a apoyar.
english: Whatever they decide, we're going to support them.

target: Esta receta es de mi abuela, la preparo igual que ella.
english: This recipe is my grandmother's, I make it just like she did.

target: ¿Te apetece que pidamos comida a domicilio esta noche? No me apetece cocinar.
english: Do you feel like ordering delivery tonight? I don't feel like cooking.

target: ¿Me puedes indicar cómo llego a la plaza mayor? Soy nuevo aquí.
english: Can you tell me how to get to the main square? I'm new here.

target: Hace mucho calor en verano, a veces más de cuarenta grados.
english: It's very hot in summer, sometimes over forty degrees.
grammar: "estar" + adjective for current emotional states. "Preocupado por" = worried about. "Ser preocupado" would mean being a worrier by nature.

target: La niebla es muy espesa esta mañana, apenas se ve la carretera.
english: The fog is very thick this morning, you can barely see the road.

target: La energía eólica es una de las fuentes renovables que más está creciendo.
english: Wind energy is one of the fastest growing renewable sources.

target: El periodo de prueba en esta empresa es de seis meses.
english: The probation period at this company is six months.

target: Me arrepentí de no haber dicho lo que sentía cuando tuve la oportunidad.
english: I regretted not saying what I felt when I had the chance.

target: Estoy convencido de que la educación es la clave del progreso.
english: I am convinced that education is the key to progress.

target: ¿Sabes cómo se adjunta un documento a un correo en esta aplicación?
english: Do you know how to attach a document to an email in this app?

target: La pared del salón es blanca pero estamos pensando en pintarla de color crema.
english: The living room wall is white but we are thinking of painting it cream.

target: Por lo tanto, la reunión se pospone hasta la semana que viene.
english: Therefore, the meeting is postponed until next week.
grammar: "cuyo/a/os/as" = whose. Agrees with the thing owned (libro = masc), not the owner. Formal/literary.

target: Ella es muy lista, pero está nerviosa por el examen de mañana.
english: She is very smart, but she is nervous about tomorrow's exam.

target: De haber sabido la verdad, habría actuado de otra manera.
english: Had I known the truth, I would have acted differently.

target: Me arrepiento de no haber ido.
english: I regret not having gone.

target: De haber sabido la verdad, habría actuado diferente.
english: Had I known the truth, I would have acted differently.
grammar: "De haber" + participle — literary third conditional.

target: Habría venido antes de haber podido.
english: I would have come earlier if I could have.

target: Ya habrán llegado a esta hora.
english: They must have arrived by now.
grammar: Future perfect for past guessing: "Habrá sido difícil" (It must have been hard). Speculation about a past event.

target: Me arrepiento de no haber estudiado más.
english: I regret not having studied more.

target: ¿Serán las ocho ya?
english: Could it already be eight o'clock?

target: Tendrá unos cuarenta años.
english: He must be about forty years old.
grammar: Future of probability: tendrá — making guesses about the present

target: Habrá unas doscientas personas en la sala.
english: There must be about two hundred people in the room.

target: Dicho sea de paso, tu presentación estuvo muy bien.
english: By the way, your presentation was really good.
grammar: Dicho sea de paso — by the way, incidentally (formal register)

target: De haber sabido que iba a llover, me habría llevado el paraguas.
english: Had I known it was going to rain, I would have taken the umbrella.
grammar: De + infinitive perfecto — literary/formal alternative to si + pluperfect subjunctive

target: De haber sabido que el museo cerraba, habríamos ido antes.
english: Had I known the museum was closing, we would have gone earlier.

target: De no ser por el mapa, nos habríamos perdido completamente.
english: If it weren't for the map, we would have gotten completely lost.

target: En lo que respecta al transporte, el metro es la mejor opción.
english: As far as transportation goes, the subway is the best option.

target: De haber sabido que vendríais, habría preparado más comida.
english: Had I known you were coming, I would have prepared more food.

target: De no ser por mis padres, no habría podido estudiar.
english: If it weren't for my parents, I wouldn't have been able to study.

target: En lo que respecta a la educación, somos bastante estrictos.
english: As far as education goes, we're quite strict.

target: En lo que respecta al presupuesto, nos ajustamos bastante bien.
english: As far as the budget is concerned, we managed quite well.

target: El hecho de haber viajado solo me hizo más independiente.
english: The fact of having traveled alone made me more independent.

target: Me arrepiento de no haber visitado el norte cuando tuve la oportunidad.
english: I regret not having visited the north when I had the chance.

target: Habríamos disfrutado más de no haber estado tan cansados.
english: We would have enjoyed it more had we not been so tired.

target: De no ser por la huelga de pilotos, habríamos viajado la semana pasada.
english: Were it not for the pilots' strike, we would have traveled last week.

target: De haber podido, nos habríamos quedado todo el verano.
english: Had we been able to, we would have stayed the whole summer.

target: El proyecto habría sido un éxito de no ser por los recortes.
english: The project would have been a success if not for the cuts.

target: La empresa habría quebrado de no ser por la inversión.
english: The company would have gone bankrupt if not for the investment.

target: De no haber sido por el plazo, habríamos hecho un mejor trabajo.
english: If it hadn't been for the deadline, we would have done a better job.

target: Habría renunciado de no ser por las responsabilidades familiares.
english: I would have resigned if it weren't for family responsibilities.

target: En lo que respecta a los plazos, estamos al día.
english: As far as deadlines are concerned, we're on track.

target: De no ser por el seguro, la empresa habría asumido pérdidas enormes.
english: If it weren't for insurance, the company would have taken enormous losses.

target: En lo que respecta a la crianza, cada familia es diferente.
english: When it comes to parenting, every family is different.

target: De no ser por el apoyo de mi familia, no lo habría conseguido.
english: Were it not for my family's support, I wouldn't have made it.

target: De haber sabido que era niña, habríamos decorado la habitación de rosa.
english: Had we known it was a girl, we would have decorated the room in pink.

target: De no haber sido por ti, no lo habría conseguido.
english: If it hadn't been for you, I wouldn't have made it.

target: De haber podido, me habría quedado una semana más.
english: If I could have, I would have stayed one more week.

target: De haber llegado antes, habríamos encontrado sitio.
english: Had we arrived earlier, we would have found a seat.

target: De no haber sido por su ayuda, nunca habría terminado a tiempo.
english: Had it not been for his help, I never would have finished on time.

target: Cabe destacar que los resultados superaron las expectativas.
english: It is worth noting that the results exceeded expectations.
grammar: Cabe + infinitive = it is fitting/possible to (formal register).

target: En virtud de lo expuesto, se solicita la aprobación del proyecto.
english: By virtue of the foregoing, approval of the project is requested.

target: No bien hubo terminado de hablar, estalló un aplauso.
english: No sooner had he finished speaking than applause broke out.
grammar: Hubo + participle = pretérito anterior, very formal/literary.

target: En lo que respecta a la financiación, aún hay cuestiones pendientes.
english: With regard to financing, there are still outstanding issues.

target: De no ser por su intervención, el acuerdo habría fracasado.
english: Were it not for his intervention, the agreement would have failed.

target: Serán las cinco de la tarde, más o menos.
english: It must be about five in the afternoon.

target: A tenor de los resultados, cabe esperar mejoras significativas.
english: In light of the results, significant improvements can be expected.

target: En lo sucesivo, las solicitudes deberán presentarse por escrito.
english: Henceforth, applications must be submitted in writing.

target: Cabe la posibilidad de que el evento se celebre al aire libre.
english: There is a possibility that the event will be held outdoors.

target: En aras de la transparencia, publicaremos todos los datos.
english: In the interest of transparency, we will publish all the data.

target: Tendría unos treinta años cuando empezó la empresa.
english: He must have been about thirty when he started the company.

target: Huelga decir que su contribución fue fundamental.
english: Needless to say, his contribution was fundamental.
grammar: Huelga decir que = needless to say (very formal register).

target: De no mediar circunstancias excepcionales, el plazo es inamovible.
english: Barring exceptional circumstances, the deadline is firm.

target: En lo tocante a la seguridad, se han tomado todas las medidas necesarias.
english: Regarding safety, all necessary measures have been taken.

target: Cabe preguntarse si esta política es realmente efectiva.
english: One might ask whether this policy is truly effective.

target: A mi juicio, la propuesta carece de fundamento.
english: In my judgment, the proposal lacks foundation.

target: Conviene recordar que las normas son de obligado cumplimiento.
english: It is worth remembering that the rules are mandatory.

target: En resumidas cuentas, la inversión resultó rentable.
english: In short, the investment turned out to be profitable.

target: A la luz de estos datos, debemos reconsiderar nuestra estrategia.
english: In light of this data, we must reconsider our strategy.

target: Procede, pues, analizar las causas del problema.
english: It is therefore appropriate to analyze the causes of the problem.

target: Con arreglo a la normativa vigente, se procederá al archivo del expediente.
english: In accordance with current regulations, the file will be archived.

target: De no haber mediado su intervención, las consecuencias habrían sido graves.
english: Had he not intervened, the consequences would have been serious.

target: Valga como ejemplo el caso de las energías renovables.
english: The case of renewable energy serves as an example.

target: Cabe destacar su aporte a la ciencia.
english: It is worth noting his contribution to science.

target: Cabe senalar que estos datos no han sido verificados.
english: It should be noted that this data has not been verified.
grammar: Cabe + infinitive = it is fitting/appropriate to (formal register).

target: Seran las tres de la tarde, mas o menos.
english: It must be around three in the afternoon.
grammar: Future tense for probability/conjecture about the present.

target: Conviene recordar que no siempre fue asi.
english: It's worth remembering that it wasn't always like this.

target: En lo que respecta a la calidad, no hay queja alguna.
english: As far as quality is concerned, there is no complaint whatsoever.

target: Huelga decir que la puntualidad es fundamental.
english: It goes without saying that punctuality is essential.
grammar: Huelga decir que = it goes without saying that (very formal).

target: Cabe mencionar que la catedral data del siglo quince.
english: It's worth mentioning that the cathedral dates from the fifteenth century.

target: De haber llegado antes, habriamos conseguido mejores asientos.
english: Had we arrived earlier, we would have gotten better seats.

target: Conviene senalar que el acceso al monumento es gratuito los domingos.
english: It's worth noting that access to the monument is free on Sundays.

target: De no haber reservado con antelacion, nos habriamos quedado sin hotel.
english: Had we not booked in advance, we would have been left without a hotel.

target: De haber sabido que venias, habria preparado mas comida.
english: Had I known you were coming, I would have prepared more food.

target: Bien es cierto que mi abuelo siempre tuvo razon en estas cosas.
english: It's certainly true that my grandfather was always right about these things.

target: Cabe destacar el esfuerzo que hizo toda la familia durante aquellos anos.
english: The effort the whole family made during those years is worth highlighting.

target: Cabe senalar que el presupuesto fue aprobado por unanimidad.
english: It should be noted that the budget was approved unanimously.

target: En lo que respecta al rendimiento del equipo, los datos son alentadores.
english: As far as team performance is concerned, the data is encouraging.

target: De haber actuado antes, habriamos evitado estas perdidas.
english: Had we acted sooner, we would have avoided these losses.

target: Huelga decir que la confidencialidad es prioritaria en este asunto.
english: It goes without saying that confidentiality is a priority in this matter.

target: De no mediar inconveniente, la firma se realizaria el jueves.
english: Barring any issues, the signing would take place on Thursday.
grammar: De no mediar = barring / if there are no (very formal legal/business register).

target: Los fines de semana me levanto tarde.
english: On weekends I get up late.
grammar: "Los fines de semana" — using "los" with a time expression means "every" or "on." Los lunes = on Mondays. Without "los" it's a specific one.

target: Esta sartén antiadherente es muy práctica porque nada se pega.
english: This non-stick pan is very practical because nothing sticks.

target: El tren de alta velocidad conecta las dos ciudades en menos de tres horas.
english: The high-speed train connects the two cities in less than three hours.
grammar: "hay que" + infinitive = one must (impersonal). "Tener cuidado con" = to be careful with. "Tener" expression.

target: El bosque de pinos huele increíble después de llover.
english: The pine forest smells incredible after it rains.
grammar: impersonal expression "es + adjective + que" triggers subjunctive: es importante que, es necesario que, es mejor que.

target: Es natural que sientas nostalgia cuando estás lejos de casa.
english: It's natural that you feel homesick when you are far from home.

target: Acabábamos de llegar cuando empezó a llover a cántaros.
english: We had just arrived when it started pouring (lit. raining pitchers).

target: Cada vez más personas se informan a través de internet.
english: More and more people get their information through the internet.
grammar: "resultar" (like gustar). "Expresarse" = to express oneself (reflexive). "En otro idioma" = in another language.

target: Los malentendidos surgen cuando no nos expresamos con claridad.
english: Misunderstandings arise when we don't express ourselves clearly.
grammar: "según" = according to. "Lo que me han dicho" = what they told me. "Me han dicho" — impersonal 3rd plural.

target: Ir al grano significa hablar directamente sin rodeos.
english: To get to the point means to speak directly without beating around the bush (lit. to go to the grain means to speak without detours).

target: Diste en el clavo: esa es exactamente la solución al problema.
english: You hit the nail on the head (lit. you hit the nail): that's exactly the solution to the problem
grammar: proverb. "Lo que" = that which. "Relucir" = to shine. Spanish proverbs often use present tense for timeless truths.

target: Tirar la toalla no es una opción; vamos a seguir intentándolo.
english: Throwing in the towel is not an option (lit. throwing the towel) — we're going to keep trying

target: No es pan comido; este examen requiere mucha preparación.
english: It's not a piece of cake (lit. it's not bread eaten) — this exam requires a lot of preparation

target: Cada maestrillo tiene su librillo; cada persona tiene su manera de hacer las cosas.
english: To each their own (lit. each little teacher has their little book) — everyone has their own way of doing things

target: Más vale tarde que nunca; al menos has empezado a estudiar.
english: Better late than never — at least you've started studying

target: A quien madruga, Dios le ayuda; por eso me levanto temprano.
english: The early bird catches the worm (lit. God helps those who rise early) — that's why I get up early

target: No hay mal que por bien no venga; quizás esta crisis traiga oportunidades.
english: Every cloud has a silver lining (lit. there is no bad that doesn't come for good) — perhaps this crisis will bring opportunities

target: En boca cerrada no entran moscas; a veces es mejor callarse.
english: Silence is golden (lit. in a closed mouth, no flies enter) — sometimes it's better to keep quiet

target: La sopa está rica; es una receta de mi abuela.
english: The soup tastes great — it's my grandmother's recipe

target: La manzana es verde pero no está madura todavía.
english: The apple is green but it isn't ripe yet.

target: Más vale tarde que nunca.
english: Better late than never

target: No es oro todo lo que reluce.
english: All that glitters is not gold (lit. not all that shines is gold).

target: A quien madruga, Dios le ayuda.
english: The early bird catches the worm (lit. God helps those who rise early)
grammar: Literally: "God helps those who rise early."

target: En boca cerrada no entran moscas.
english: Silence is golden (lit. in a closed mouth, no flies enter)
grammar: Literally: "Flies don't enter a closed mouth."

target: Cada maestrillo tiene su librillo.
english: Each teacher has their own method (lit. each little teacher has their little book)

target: No hay mal que por bien no venga.
english: Every cloud has a silver lining (lit. there is no bad that doesn't come for good).

target: ¿Puedes echarme una mano?
english: Can you give me a hand? (lit. can you throw me a hand?)

target: Más vale prevenir que curar.
english: Prevention is better than cure (lit. better to prevent than to cure)

target: Más vale solo que mal acompañado.
english: Better alone than in bad company.

target: Ojos que no ven, corazón que no siente.
english: Out of sight, out of mind (lit. eyes that don't see, heart that doesn't feel)

target: Al mal tiempo, buena cara.
english: Put on a brave face in bad times (lit. to bad weather, good face)

target: Cuando el río suena, agua lleva.
english: Where there's smoke, there's fire (lit. when the river sounds, it carries water)

target: Estoy de acuerdo contigo.
english: I agree with you.
grammar: Some expressions always use estar regardless of the general rules: "estar de acuerdo" (agree), "estar a punto de" (about to).

target: Es tarde, vámonos.
english: It's late, let's go.
grammar: Ser for time: "Son las tres." Time always uses ser.

target: El idioma es difícil al principio.
english: The language is difficult at first.
grammar: "El idioma" is masculine. Greek-origin -ma words are masculine: idioma, problema, tema, sistema.

target: Me flipa esta serie.
english: I'm blown away by this series (lit. it flips me out)
grammar: "Flipar — colloquial," uses the gustar pattern: the thing causing the feeling is the subject. "Me flipar..." = "... is [feeling] to me." Use singular for one thing, plural for multiple.

target: Ella se da cuenta de todo.
english: She notices everything (lit. she gives herself account of everything).
grammar: "Darse cuenta de" = to realize. Fixed reflexive expression: "Me di cuenta de que..." = I realized that...

target: Dicho de otro modo, no estamos preparados.
english: In other words, we're not ready.
grammar: "Dicho de otro modo" = in other words. Introduces a rephrasing or clarification.

target: Se me cayó el alma al suelo cuando me enteré.
english: My heart sank when I found out (lit. my soul fell to the floor)
grammar: "Se me cayó el alma al suelo" — accidental se construction (se + indirect object). Literally "my soul fell to the floor on me." The "se me" removes agency.

target: No me cabe en la cabeza que haya dicho eso.
english: I can't wrap my head around the fact that he said that (lit. it doesn't fit in my head)
grammar: Idiomatic: no me cabe en la cabeza — I can't believe/understand

target: Me costó un ojo de la cara arreglar el carro.
english: It cost me an arm and a leg to fix the car (lit. it cost me an eye from the face)
grammar: "Costar un ojo de la cara" — uses gustar-like construction with indirect object. "Me costó un ojo de la cara" = It cost me an arm and a leg.

target: Estoy hasta las narices de que no me hagan caso.
english: I'm fed up with being ignored (lit. I'm up to the nostrils)
grammar: "Estar hasta las narices" — estar + preposition phrase for emotional state. Similar: "estar harto/a" (to be fed up).

target: Menos mal que llegamos a tiempo para el vuelo.
english: Thank goodness we arrived in time for the flight (lit. less bad).
grammar: "Menos mal que..." = Thank goodness that... Expresses relief: "Menos mal que llegaste."

target: Se las arregla muy bien sola.
english: She manages very well on her own (lit. she fixes them for herself).
grammar: "Arreglárselas" = to manage/cope. Reflexive + las (fixed): "Ya me las arreglaré" = I'll figure it out. "Las" has no specific referent.

target: Más vale tarde que nunca, ¿no?
english: Better late than never, right?

target: Me pilla un poco lejos, pero merece la pena.
english: It's a bit far for me, but it's worth it (lit. it catches me a bit far)

target: Es pan comido, no te preocupes.
english: It's a piece of cake, don't worry (lit. it's eaten bread)

target: No le des más vueltas; lo hecho, hecho está.
english: Don't overthink it (lit. don't give it more turns) — what's done is done
grammar: Dar vueltas — to overthink; lo hecho, hecho está — what's done is done

target: Vamos a ir al grano: necesitamos más presupuesto.
english: Let's get to the point (lit. let's go to the grain): we need more budget

target: Ella es de Colombia pero está viviendo en España.
english: She's from Colombia but she's living in Spain.
grammar: Ser + de for origin ("Es de España"), estar + gerund for actions in progress ("Está hablando"). Two different uses in one sentence.

target: Mi carro es viejo pero está en buenas condiciones.
english: My car is old but it's in good condition.
grammar: Ser for inherent quality (viejo), estar for condition (en buenas condiciones)

target: La calle donde está tu casa es muy bonita.
english: The street where your house is is very pretty.

target: Más vale que te des prisa o vamos a perder el tren.
english: You'd better hurry up or we're going to miss the train (lit. it's more worth)
grammar: Más vale que + subjunctive — you'd better

target: El taxi es más rápido que el metro a esta hora.
english: The taxi is faster than the subway at this hour.

target: Esta playa es la más limpia de la costa.
english: This beach is the cleanest on the coast.

target: Esta isla es más pequeña pero más tranquila que la otra.
english: This island is smaller but quieter than the other.

target: Esta oficina es más grande que la de Madrid.
english: This office is bigger than the one in Madrid.

target: Esta sucursal es la más rentable de la red.
english: This branch is the most profitable in the network.

target: Es raro que el sistema falle a esta hora.
english: It's strange that the system crashes at this time.

target: El turno que me toca esta semana es el de noche.
english: The shift I have this week is the night one.

target: Siendo como es temporada alta, los precios están por las nubes.
english: Being peak season as it is, the prices are sky-high (lit. prices are through the clouds)

target: Si no llega a ser por ese mapa, nos habríamos perdido.
english: If it hadn't been for that map, we would have gotten lost (lit. if it doesn't get to be for that map).
grammar: "Si no llega a ser por" — colloquial alternative to "si no hubiera sido por" for past counterfactuals.

target: No bien habíamos desembarcado cuando empezó a llover a cántaros.
english: No sooner had we disembarked than it started pouring (lit. raining pitchers).

target: Sea como sea, el viaje nos cambió la perspectiva.
english: Be that as it may, the trip changed our perspective.
grammar: "Sea como sea" (be that as it may) — concessive expression with duplicated subjunctive.

target: A sabiendas de que llovería, decidimos ir igualmente.
english: Knowing full well that it would rain, we decided to go anyway.
grammar: "A sabiendas de que" (knowing full well that) — concessive expression implying deliberate awareness.

target: Si la bisabuela levantara la cabeza, no reconocería la casa.
english: If great-grandma could see this, she wouldn't recognize the house (lit. if she raised her head)
grammar: Levantar la cabeza = idiomatic: if they could see (from beyond)

target: Me di cuenta de que había olvidado las llaves.
english: I realized I had forgotten the keys (lit. I gave myself account).
grammar: Darse cuenta de = to realize, a common reflexive expression.

target: Me costó un ojo de la cara.
english: It cost me an arm and a leg (lit. it cost me an eye from the face)

target: No me vengas con esas; dime la verdad.
english: Don't give me that (lit. don't come to me with those) — tell me the truth

target: Meter la pata es algo que todos hacemos de vez en cuando.
english: Putting your foot in it (lit. stepping in it) is something we all do from time to time
grammar: Meter la pata = to put one's foot in it (make a blunder).

target: Ese trabajo me tiene hasta las narices.
english: That job has me fed up to here (lit. up to the nostrils)

target: No le des más vueltas; ya está decidido.
english: Don't overthink it (lit. don't give it more turns) — it's already decided

target: Quien mucho abarca, poco aprieta.
english: Jack of all trades, master of none (lit. who covers too much, squeezes little)

target: Eso es pan comido; lo termino en cinco minutos.
english: That's a piece of cake (lit. that's eaten bread) — I'll finish it in five minutes
grammar: Pan comido = piece of cake (very easy).

target: Ese negocio fue viento en popa desde el principio.
english: That business went smoothly from the start (lit. went wind at the stern)
grammar: Viento en popa = smooth sailing (literally: wind at the stern).

target: Estoy entre la espada y la pared.
english: I'm between a rock and a hard place (lit. between the sword and the wall)

target: Me pilló por sorpresa; no me lo esperaba para nada.
english: It caught me by surprise (lit. it caught/grabbed me) — I didn't expect it at all

target: Aquí hay gato encerrado; algo no cuadra.
english: Something fishy is going on (lit. there's an enclosed cat here) — something doesn't add up
grammar: Hay gato encerrado = something fishy (literally: there's a locked-up cat).

target: Eso es harina de otro costal; no tiene nada que ver.
english: That's a different kettle of fish (lit. that's flour from another sack) — it has nothing to do with it

target: Me lo contó con pelos y señales.
english: He told me in great detail (lit. with hairs and signs)

target: Tirar la toalla no es una opción.
english: Throwing in the towel is not an option (lit. throwing the towel)

target: Me lo pasé bomba en la fiesta de anoche.
english: I had a blast at last night's party (lit. I had a bomb of a time)

target: No muerdas la mano que te da de comer.
english: Don't bite the hand that feeds you (lit. don't bite the hand that gives you to eat).

target: Ir al grano es lo mejor en una reunión de trabajo.
english: Getting to the point is best in a work meeting (lit. going to the grain).
grammar: Ir al grano = to get to the point.

target: Se le fue la lengua y contó el secreto.
english: He let it slip and told the secret (lit. his tongue got away from him)

target: Tener un as bajo la manga siempre es útil.
english: Having an ace up your sleeve is always useful (lit. having an ace under the sleeve)

target: No todo lo que brilla es oro.
english: All that glitters is not gold (lit. not all that shines is gold).

target: Me tienes en ascuas; dime qué pasó.
english: You're keeping me in suspense (lit. you have me on embers) — tell me what happened

target: El viaje fue pan comido; todo salio a las mil maravillas.
english: The trip was a piece of cake (lit. eaten bread) — everything went wonderfully

target: Habia gato encerrado con la oferta del hotel tan barato.
english: There was something fishy about the hotel deal being so cheap (lit. there was an enclosed cat)

target: Vamos a ir al grano y no perdamos mas el tiempo.
english: Let's get to the point and stop wasting time (lit. let's go to the grain).

target: En mi opinión, esta película es muy aburrida.
english: In my opinion, this movie is very boring.

target: Puesto que no hay suficiente presupuesto, tendremos que reducir gastos.
english: Since there isn't enough budget, we will have to cut expenses.

target: Cuanto más practiques, mejor hablarás; así es como funciona el aprendizaje.
english: The more you practice, the better you'll speak — that's how learning works.

target: El alquiler es muy caro en esta ciudad.
english: Rent is very expensive in this city.

target: Cuanto más estudio, más me doy cuenta de lo que no sé.
english: The more I study, the more I realize what I don't know.
grammar: "Cuanto más...más..." = The more...the more: "Cuanto más estudio, más aprendo." Correlative comparative structure.

target: La gastronomía de esta región es conocida mundialmente.
english: The cuisine of this region is known worldwide.

target: Dicho de otra manera, necesitamos un cambio.
english: In other words, we need a change.

target: Cuanto más practiques, mejor hablarás.
english: The more you practice, the better you'll speak.

target: Puesto que ya es tarde, nos vamos.
english: Since it's already late, we're leaving.

target: Yo tomo el autobús para ir al trabajo.
english: I take the bus to go to work.

target: Mi tía hace el mejor pastel de chocolate.
english: My aunt makes the best chocolate cake.

target: El baño está al fondo a la derecha.
english: The bathroom is at the end on the right.
grammar: Estar for location: "Está en Madrid." Physical position always uses estar (except events).

target: Unas chicas de mi clase son de México.
english: Some girls from my class are from Mexico.

target: La gente es muy amable en esta ciudad.
english: The people are very friendly in this city.

target: Esto es para la cena de esta noche.
english: This is for tonight's dinner.

target: La empresa donde trabajo tiene oficinas en Madrid.
english: The company where I work has offices in Madrid.

target: La mesa que reservé es para cuatro personas.
english: The table I reserved is for four people.

target: El pueblo donde veranea mi familia es precioso.
english: The town where my family spends summers is lovely.

target: Puesto que ya terminamos, podemos irnos.
english: Since we already finished, we can leave.
grammar: "Puesto que" = since / given that. Formal causal connector: "Puesto que no vino, empezamos sin él."

target: Ella conoce a mucha gente en la ciudad.
english: She knows a lot of people in the city.

target: Ella es médica, pero hoy está de vacaciones.
english: She's a doctor, but today she's on vacation.
grammar: Ser for identity (es médica = she's a doctor), estar for current state (está de vacaciones = she's on vacation). Same person, different information → different verb.

target: Mi tía es enfermera en el hospital central.
english: My aunt is a nurse at the central hospital.

target: El problema es que no tenemos suficiente presupuesto.
english: The problem is that we don't have enough budget.

target: Hay una farmacia al final de la calle.
english: There's a pharmacy at the end of the street.

target: La habitación que reservé tiene vistas al mar.
english: The room I booked has sea views.

target: La ciudad donde hice el máster es preciosa.
english: The city where I did my master's degree is beautiful.

target: Este hotel es más cómodo que el anterior.
english: This hotel is more comfortable than the previous one.

target: Viajar en temporada baja es mucho más barato.
english: Traveling in low season is much cheaper.

target: La catedral es más antigua que el castillo.
english: The cathedral is older than the castle.

target: Mi jefe es más exigente que el anterior.
english: My boss is more demanding than the previous one.

target: Este candidato es el más cualificado de todos.
english: This candidate is the most qualified of all.

target: Este contrato es más favorable que el último.
english: This contract is more favorable than the last one.

target: El departamento de ventas es el más eficiente.
english: The sales department is the most efficient.

target: Este software es el más fácil de usar.
english: This software is the easiest to use.

target: La carga de trabajo es mayor en diciembre.
english: The workload is higher in December.

target: El perro es más juguetón que el gato.
english: The dog is more playful than the cat.

target: Mi hermana es tan inteligente como mi padre.
english: My sister is as smart as my father.

target: Puesto que no hay tren directo, tomaremos el autobús.
english: Since there's no direct train, we'll take the bus.

target: Puesto que mi hermana no puede venir, celebraremos otro día.
english: Since my sister can't come, we'll celebrate another day.

target: Cuanto más viajo, más me doy cuenta de lo grande que es el mundo.
english: The more I travel, the more I realize how big the world is.

target: Llegados a este punto, prefiero quedarme un día más.
english: At this point, I'd rather stay one more day.

target: Dicho sea de paso, la comida en el norte fue espectacular.
english: By the way, the food in the north was spectacular.

target: Cuanto más tiempo paso con mis hijos, más los entiendo.
english: The more time I spend with my kids, the more I understand them.

target: Llegados a este punto, los niños ya son bastante independientes.
english: At this point, the kids are quite independent already.

target: Dicho sea de paso, mi hermana cocina mejor que yo.
english: By the way, my sister cooks better than me.

target: Puesto que no había metro, tomamos un taxi.
english: Since there was no metro, we took a taxi.

target: Cuanto más viajo, más aprecio la cultura española.
english: The more I travel, the more I appreciate Spanish culture.
grammar: "Cuanto más... más" = the more... the more. Comparative correlative construction.

target: Dicho esto, el viaje superó con creces mis expectativas.
english: That said, the trip far exceeded my expectations.

target: El haber conocido a gente local enriqueció enormemente la experiencia.
english: Having met local people enormously enriched the experience.

target: Dicho esto, el rendimiento del equipo ha mejorado.
english: That said, the team's performance has improved.

target: Puesto que ya se firmó el acuerdo, no hay marcha atrás.
english: Since the agreement has already been signed, there's no going back.

target: Cuanto más crecen los niños, más independientes se vuelven.
english: The more the kids grow, the more independent they become.
grammar: Cuanto más... más... = the more... the more...

target: Dicho sea de paso, la receta de mi abuela es mucho mejor.
english: By the way, my grandmother's recipe is much better.

target: Cuanto antes aprendan a compartir, mejor.
english: The sooner they learn to share, the better.

target: Puesto que ya estamos todos, podemos empezar.
english: Since we're all here now, we can start.

target: Cuanto más practico, mejor me siento con el idioma.
english: The more I practice, the better I feel with the language.
grammar: Cuanto más...mejor = the more...the better, comparative correlation.

target: Cuanto más leo sobre el tema, más preguntas tengo.
english: The more I read about the topic, the more questions I have.

target: Dicho esto, pasemos al siguiente punto del orden del día.
english: That said, let's move on to the next item on the agenda.

target: Terminada la reunión, todos se dirigieron a la salida.
english: Once the meeting was over, everyone headed for the exit.
grammar: Absolute participial clause: participle + noun = 'once X was done'.

target: Resueltos los problemas técnicos, se procedió a la inauguración del sistema.
english: Once the technical problems were resolved, the system inauguration proceeded.

target: Considerando que los plazos son ajustados, convendría empezar cuanto antes.
english: Considering that the deadlines are tight, it would be advisable to start as soon as possible.

target: El haber vivido en varios países le dio una perspectiva única.
english: Having lived in several countries gave him a unique perspective.
grammar: Nominalized infinitive: el + infinitive functions as a noun (subject).

target: Abierta la convocatoria, los candidatos disponen de treinta días para presentar su solicitud.
english: With the call for applications open, candidates have thirty days to submit their application.

target: No es sino trabajando todos juntos como lograremos superar esta crisis.
english: It is only by working all together that we will manage to overcome this crisis.
grammar: No es sino + gerund + como = it is only by... that (emphatic cleft).

target: Vistas las circunstancias, la dirección ha decidido aplazar el proyecto.
english: Given the circumstances, management has decided to postpone the project.

target: Cuantos más obstáculos encontraba, más decidido estaba a conseguirlo.
english: The more obstacles he encountered, the more determined he was to achieve it.

target: Dicho lo cual, paso a presentar las conclusiones del estudio.
english: That being said, I will now present the conclusions of the study.

target: Llegados a este punto, no queda más remedio que tomar una decisión drástica.
english: Having reached this point, there's no choice but to make a drastic decision.
grammar: Llegados a este punto = absolute participial, 'having reached this point'.

target: Cumplidos los requisitos, se procederá a la evaluación de las candidaturas.
english: Once the requirements are met, the evaluation of candidacies will proceed.

target: Hechas las presentaciones, comenzó la negociación.
english: Once the introductions were made, the negotiation began.

target: Es precisamente por eso por lo que debemos actuar con cautela.
english: That is precisely why we must act with caution.
grammar: Es por eso por lo que = it's because of that that (cleft sentence).

target: El no haber previsto este escenario nos ha puesto en una situación complicada.
english: Not having foreseen this scenario has put us in a complicated situation.

target: Sentados a la mesa, repasaron uno por uno los puntos del acuerdo.
english: Seated at the table, they reviewed the points of the agreement one by one.

target: Cuanto mayor es la responsabilidad, mayor es la presión.
english: The greater the responsibility, the greater the pressure.

target: Analizada la situación, se optó por una estrategia más conservadora.
english: Once the situation was analyzed, a more conservative strategy was chosen.

target: Es solo cuando pierdes algo cuando te das cuenta de su valor.
english: It is only when you lose something that you realize its value.

target: Agotadas todas las vías de negociación, recurrieron a los tribunales.
english: Having exhausted all avenues of negotiation, they turned to the courts.

target: El saber escuchar es tan importante como el saber hablar.
english: Knowing how to listen is as important as knowing how to speak.

target: Puesto en contexto, el comentario no resultaba tan ofensivo.
english: Put in context, the comment wasn't so offensive.

target: Concluida la fase de pruebas, el producto se lanzará al mercado.
english: Once the testing phase is concluded, the product will be launched on the market.

target: Dicho esto, pasemos al siguiente tema.
english: That said, let's move to the next topic.

target: Dicho esto, pasemos al siguiente punto del orden del dia.
english: That said, let's move on to the next item on the agenda.

target: Dicho lo cual, pasamos a tratar el tema de la reestructuracion.
english: That being said, let's move on to the topic of restructuring.

target: Leido el comunicado, los periodistas empezaron a hacer preguntas.
english: Once the statement was read, the journalists started asking questions.
grammar: Absolute participial clause: participle + noun = 'once X was done'.

target: No fue sino despues de mucho pensarlo cuando tome la decision.
english: It wasn't until after thinking about it a lot that I made the decision.
grammar: Cleft sentence: No fue sino...cuando = It wasn't until...that.

target: El no haber estudiado lo suficiente fue lo que le costo el examen.
english: Not having studied enough was what cost him the exam.

target: Cuanto mas al sur ibamos, mas calor hacia.
english: The further south we went, the hotter it got.

target: El haber viajado tanto le permitio entender culturas muy diferentes.
english: Having traveled so much allowed him to understand very different cultures.

target: Cuantos mas anos pasan, mas se parecen los hermanos entre si.
english: The more years go by, the more the siblings resemble each other.

target: El haber trabajado en equipo fue clave para el exito del proyecto.
